1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124

10	/-!
11	# Abelian-variety rigidity: genus-`0` final assembly
12	
13	This file is the **genus-`0` final layer** of the project's committed characteristic-free
14	route (route (c)) to abelian-variety rigidity. It assembles the headline
15	`rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — the char-free replacement (no `[CharZero kbar]`) for
16	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` (which remains in
17	tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and still carries `[CharZero]`).
18	
19	The abstract Mumford Rigidity Lemma chain (`rigidity_lemma`) and the two Milne §I.1 corollaries
20	(`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` = Cor 1.5, `av_regularMap_isHom_of_zero` = Cor 1.2) it
21	implies live **upstream** in `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityLemma`, which is imported here. That
22	chain is PROVEN axiom-clean (iters 157–162); this file consumes it.
23	
24	The final assembly has three links specific to the genus-`0` route:
25	
26	1. `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` — every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` into an abelian variety is
27	   constant. Proved (route resolved iter-164) by the **𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut**: the total scaling
28	   action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾ₘ → ℙ¹`, `(x, λ) ↦ λx`, feeds the proven Cor 1.5
29	   (`hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity`) — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO
30	   `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`, char-general. (Still a scaffold `sorry` pending the concrete ℙ¹/𝔾ₘ/σ_× infra.)
31	2. `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` — a smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
32	   isomorphic to `ℙ¹` (blocked on Riemann–Roch).
33	3. `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` — THE HEADLINE consumed by `genusZeroWitness`.
34	
35	The file also exposes the project-local bridge `iotaGm_isDominant` — the dominance of the
36	canonical `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` inclusion — which becomes provable once Lane A
37	(`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects`) ships the concrete chartwise body of `gmScalingP1`.
38

43	## Encoding notes
44	
45	Mathlib `b80f227` packages no `ℙ¹` as a `Scheme`, so the projective line is encoded by its
46	abstract characterisation: a smooth proper geometrically irreducible `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` of
47	relative dimension `1` with `genus = 0` (see
48	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ProjectiveLineBar`).
49	The signatures of declarations 1–3 are **provisional** (`SCAFFOLD` comments mark them); the prover
50	may refine the encoding when the bodies are filled. Declaration 3 is pinned verbatim to
51	`rigidity_over_kbar`'s signature minus `[CharZero kbar]`, because it is the exact signature the
52	consumer (`genusZeroWitness.key`) needs.
53	-/

79	/-- **Sub-task (b) range containment (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the topological image of
80	the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1] ∈ ℙ¹` lies inside the open image of the chart-1 affine open
81	`Proj.awayι (X 1) : Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1)) ⟶ Proj 𝒜`.
82	
83	**Mathematical content.** `onePt.left = Proj.fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 (evalIntoGlobal v) _`
84	with `v 0 = v 1 = 1`. Since `evalIntoGlobal v (X 1) = (ΓSpecIso k̄).inv 1 = 1` is a unit in
85	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`, the preimage of `D₊(X 1) ⊆ Proj 𝒜` under `onePt.left` is
86	`(Spec k̄).basicOpen 1 = ⊤`, so the image of `onePt.left` lies entirely in
87	`Set.range (Proj.awayι (X 1)) = D₊(X 1)`.
88	
89	The range chain (iter-184 closure):
90	(i) `Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange` + `Proj.opensRange_awayι` rewrite the RHS into
91	`↑(Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 (X 1))`; (ii) pointwise destructure to `onePt.left x ∈ basicOpen`;
92	(iii) `Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage` + `change` expose `onePt.left = fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 φ hφ`
93	(`pointOfVec` unfolds through `Over.homMk` even though `evalIntoGlobal`/`pointOfVec` are
94	private — defeq is unaffected by name visibility); (iv) `fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen`
95	reduces to `(Spec k̄).basicOpen ((ΓSpecIso k̄).inv.hom (eval (·=1) (X 1)))`;
96	(v) `Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit` + `simp` closes via `eval_X` + `map_one` + `isUnit_one`.
97	Axiom-clean: `#print axioms` = `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
98	
99	Iter-190: extracted from the previous `∃`-packed `iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` (verdict A of
100	the iter-189 mathlib-analogist Lane E `projappiso` analysis) so the chart-1 lift
101	`iotaGm_r_1` is exposed as a named `noncomputable def`. Downstream
102	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can then apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`
103	directly to `iotaGm_r_1` without re-deriving the range witness via `cancel_mono`. -/
104	private lemma iotaGm_r_1_range_subset (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
105	    Set.range ⇑(ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left ⊆
106	      Set.range ⇑(Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
107	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
108	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
109	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
110	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)) := by
111	  -- Iter-190 prover closure (point-witness reshape). The iter-184 closed body relied on
112	  -- `rw [← Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]` to expose the RHS `Set.range` as a `(...).opensRange`
113	  -- coercion; in the standalone signature here, elaboration types the LHS at
114	  -- `Set ↥(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` whereas `Proj.awayι _ _ _ _` is typed at
115	  -- `Set ↥(Proj 𝒜)`, so the rewrite pattern does not match. We sidestep this by
116	  -- destructuring point-wise FIRST, then `change`-ing the membership goal into
117	  -- `(...).opensRange.1` (a defeq reshape that works because the underlying carriers
118	  -- `↥(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and `↥(Proj 𝒜)` are defeq through
119	  -- `Scheme.asOver` / `OverClass.asOver`). The remainder of the chain is identical to
120	  -- the iter-184 body.
121	  rintro _ ⟨x, rfl⟩
122	  change (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left.base x ∈ (Proj.awayι _ _ _ _).opensRange.1
123	  rw [Proj.opensRange_awayι]
124	  change x ∈ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left ⁻¹ᵁ Proj.basicOpen _ _
125	  change x ∈ Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) _ _ ⁻¹ᵁ _
126	  rw [Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen _ _ _
127	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1)
128	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)]
129	  refine (Scheme.basicOpen_of_isUnit _ ?_).symm ▸ TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top x
130	  change IsUnit ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
131	    ((MvPolynomial.eval (fun _ : Fin 2 => (1 : kbar)))
132	      ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
133	        Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)))
134	  simp

136	/-- **Sub-task (b) lift (iter-190 Lane E refactor):** the chart-1 factor
137	`r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))` of the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1] ∈ ℙ¹` through
138	the chart-1 open immersion `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Provided by the universal property
139	`IsOpenImmersion.lift` of the open immersion `Proj.awayι (X 1)` together with the
140	range-containment witness `iotaGm_r_1_range_subset`.

142	Iter-190 refactor: replaces the existential `∃ r_1, ...` of the previous
143	`iotaGm_onePt_chart1_factor` (verdict A of the iter-189 mathlib-analogist Lane E
144	`projappiso` analysis) with a named `noncomputable def`, so that the consumer
145	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app`
146	directly to `iotaGm_r_1` instead of re-deriving the lift identity via `cancel_mono`. -/
147	private noncomputable def iotaGm_r_1 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
148	    Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
149	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
150	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
151	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
152	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1))) :=
153	  IsOpenImmersion.lift
154	    (Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
155	      ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
156	        Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) 1)
157	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)
158	      (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm 1))

160	    (iotaGm_r_1_range_subset kbar)

169	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar 1)

179	    ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left) ≫
180	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =

186	/-- **Sub-task f section (iter-183 Lane E; iter-191 refactored to specialise on `iotaGm_r_1`):**
187	the chart-1 section `s : Gm.left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover).X 1`, built as the `pullback.lift` over
188	`pullback q (awayι (![X 0, X 1] 1))` with the iotaGm-prefix as `q`-component and

191	**Iter-191 refactor** (per iter-190 review §7 + progress-critic STUCK-corrective scope):
192	Previously parametrised over abstract `(r_1, h_r_1)`. Now specialised on the named
193	`iotaGm_r_1` / `iotaGm_r_1_fac` directly, so the downstream consumer
194	`iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion` can apply `IsOpenImmersion.lift_app` to

196	private noncomputable def iotaGm_chart1_section (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
197	    (Gm kbar).left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X (1 : Fin 2) :=
198	  pullback.lift
199	    (pullback.lift
200	      ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left)

206	      rfl)

216	   ↪ Spec k̄[u]               (localization at `t`, via `IsOpenImmersion.of_isLocalization`)

226	/-- **Blueprint sub-lemma `lem:iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval` (iter-192 Lane E
227	extraction):** the chart-`1` Proj.appIso evaluation that bridges
228	`isLocElem → [X_0/X_1] → u → 1`.

230	This is the substantive morphism-level equation identifying the chart-`1`
231	composition `iotaGm_chart1_section ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1 : Gm.left ⟶ ℙ¹.left`
232	with the canonical 3-step open-immersion factorisation
233	`Spec.map(algMap MvPoly→GmRing) ≫ Spec.map(iso.toRingHom) ≫ Proj.awayι X_1`.
234	
235	**Mathematical content** (per blueprint `lem:iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval`).
236	The chart-`1` ring map of `gmScalingP1_chart 1` sends `isLocElem ∈ Away 𝒜 X_1`
237	(the chart-`1` affine coordinate `X_0/X_1`) to `isLocElem ⊗ algMap(X())` in the
238	tensor product `Away 𝒜 X_1 ⊗ GmRing`. The section `iotaGm_chart1_section`'s
239	chart-`1` factor is `Gm.hom ≫ iotaGm_r_1`, where `iotaGm_r_1` is the chart-`1`
240	lift of the `k̄`-point `onePt = [1:1]`. Composing the section with the chart map
241	collapses the tensor through `Algebra.TensorProduct.lid` (the trivial `Gm`-twist
242	at `λ = 1`), yielding the canonical inclusion via the chart-`1` ring iso.

244	The key evaluation chain: `isLocElem ∈ Away 𝒜 X_1` lifts via
245	`(Proj.awayι X_1).appIso ⊤ .inv` to `[X_0/X_1] ∈ HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 X_1`
246	(the ratio in `Γ(Proj 𝒜, D₊(X_1))`); the chart-`1` ring iso forward sends
247	`[X_0/X_1] ↦ u`; finally, evaluation at the identity section `λ = 1` lands at
248	`1 ∈ kbar` after `Algebra.TensorProduct.lid` collapse.

256	private lemma iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
257	    iotaGm_chart1_section kbar ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2) =
258	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
259	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) ≫
260	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
261	          (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom) ≫
262	          Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
263	            (MvPolynomial.X 1 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
264	            (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos := by
265	  -- Prove the equation `s ≫ chart 1 = canonical 3-step OI`.
266	  -- Stage 1: Fuse the two trailing `Spec.map`s on the RHS into a single `Spec.map`
267	  --   via `Spec.map_comp` + `CommRingCat.ofHom_comp`, so both sides end with
268	  --   `Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom f_ring) ≫ Proj.awayι (X 1)` where
269	  --   `f_ring = (algMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
270	  --             (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom`.
271	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
272	  -- Stage 2: Unfold `gmScalingP1_chart` on the LHS and re-associate so both
273	  --   sides end with `Proj.awayι (X 1)`. Apply `congr 1` to cancel the
274	  --   trailing `Proj.awayι (X 1)` factor (open immersion ⟹ mono, but `congr 1`
275	  --   is structural and avoids needing the mono instance directly).
276	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
277	  rw [← Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc]
278	  congr 1
279	  -- Stage 3 (iter-185 attack): the `(gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar 1).hom` in the LHS is
280	  -- inaccessible to `unfold` (the def is `private` in `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`),
281	  -- but its body is a 4-step iso chain `pullbackSymmetry ≪≫ pullbackRightPullbackFstIso ≪≫
282	  -- pullback.congrHom _ _ ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso`, with the two `pullback.congrHom`-equality
283	  -- proofs `Prop`-typed (hence proof-irrelevant). A `change` with `_, _` placeholders for
284	  -- those two proofs forces Lean to reconstruct them by unification and lets the kernel
285	  -- discharge the defEq via proof irrelevance — sidestepping the private-name barrier.
286	  change (iotaGm_chart1_section kbar ≫
287	      (pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
288	        pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
289	        pullback.congrHom _ _ ≪≫
290	        pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)).hom) ≫ _ = _

292	  -- skeleton so the LHS becomes a fully concrete chain

294	  --    pullback.congrHom.hom ≫ pullbackSpecIso.hom ≫ Spec.map (eval₂Hom_comp_iso)`.

297	  -- both sides so both end with the SAME morphism `Spec.map(iso.toRingHom)`. With the
298	  -- iso instance `h_iso_isIso` (= `IsIso (CommRingCat.ofHom iso.toRingHom)`),
299	  -- `Spec.map(iso.toRingHom)` is itself iso; `cancel_iso_hom_right_assoc` cancels it.
300	  rw [CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, Spec.map_comp, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, Spec.map_comp]
301	  haveI h_iso_isIso : IsIso (CommRingCat.ofHom
302	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom) := by
303	    rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom
304	          = (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toCommRingCatIso.hom from rfl]
305	    exact (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toCommRingCatIso.isIso_hom
306	  haveI : IsIso (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
307	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom)) := inferInstance
308	  -- Stage 6: fully left-associate `≫`s so both sides syntactically end with the iso
309	  -- `Spec.map (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom`; then `cancel_mono`
310	  -- on the iso (`IsIso → Mono`) discards the trailing factor.
311	  simp only [← Category.assoc]
312	  apply (CategoryTheory.cancel_mono (f := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
313	      (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar 1).toRingHom))).mpr
314	  -- Stage 7: both sides now target the affine `Spec (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Use

316	  refine ext_of_isAffine ?_

318	  -- This reduces the goal to an explicit ring-map equation

346	/-- **Iter-182 Lane E primary helper (refactored iter-183):** the composed morphism
347	`(lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm)).left ≫ gmScalingP1.left : Gm.left ⟶ ℙ¹.left` is an
348	open immersion. -- See `iotaGm_isOpenImmersion` below for the consumer; the chart-1
349	substantive content is now packaged in `iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval`. -/
350	private lemma iotaGm_chart1_composition_isOpenImmersion [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
351	    IsOpenImmersion (iotaGm_chart1_section kbar
352	      ≫ gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2)) := by
353	  -- The composition equals the canonical inclusion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] → ℙ¹` sending
354	  -- `λ ↦ [λ : 1]`, which factors as three open immersions:

523	private theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
524	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
525	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
526	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
527	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A)
528	    (hf0 : ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f = η[A]) :
529	    f = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
530	  -- W-axis collapse via `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`, precomposed with `onePt`:
531	  -- `lift zeroPt onePt ≫ gmScalingP1 = zeroPt`.
532	  have hcollapse :
533	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar) ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar
534	        = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar := by
535	    have hbase := gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero kbar
536	    -- Rewrite `lift zeroPt onePt = onePt ≫ lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm)`.
537	    have hreshape :
538	        lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
539	          = Gm.onePt kbar ≫
540	            lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar)) := by
541	      rw [comp_lift, Category.comp_id]
542	      congr 1
543	      rw [← Category.assoc,
544	        toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
545	    rw [hreshape, Category.assoc, hbase, ← Category.assoc,
546	      toUnit_unique (Gm.onePt kbar ≫ toUnit (Gm kbar)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
547	  -- The Cor 1.5 basepoint hypothesis `lift v₀ w₀ ≫ (σ ≫ f) = η[A]`.
548	  have hcorhyp :
549	      lift (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
550	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = η[A] := by
551	    rw [← Category.assoc, hcollapse]; exact hf0
552	  -- Apply Cor 1.5.
553	  have key := hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity
554	    (V := ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (W := Gm kbar)
555	    (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (Gm.onePt kbar)
556	    (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) hcorhyp
557	  -- W-axis restriction: `lift (toUnit Gm ≫ zeroPt) (𝟙 Gm) ≫ σ ≫ f = (1 : Gm ⟶ A)`.
558	  have hfW :
559	      lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
560	        ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) = (1 : Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
561	    rw [← Category.assoc, gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero, Category.assoc, hf0]
562	    exact Hom.one_def.symm
563	  -- `snd ≫ fW = snd ≫ 1 = 1` (using `toUnit` uniqueness).
564	  have hSndFW :
565	      snd (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)
566	        ≫ (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
567	          ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f))
568	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⊗ Gm kbar ⟶ A) := by
569	    rw [hfW, Hom.one_def, ← Category.assoc]
570	    congr 1
571	    exact toUnit_unique _ _
572	  -- `key` now reads `σ ≫ f = fst ≫ fV` (after collapsing the W-axis to 1).
573	  rw [hSndFW, _root_.mul_one] at key
574	  -- Name the V-axis restriction `fV`.
575	  set fV : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A :=
576	    lift (𝟙 (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ Gm.onePt kbar)
577	      ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) with hfVdef
578	  -- Precompose `key` with `gmInP1 := lift (toUnit Gm ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm) : Gm → ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm`
579	  -- ("λ ↦ (1, λ)"). The resulting morphism `ι := gmInP1 ≫ σ : Gm → ℙ¹` is the canonical
580	  -- inclusion `Gm ↪ ℙ¹` ("λ ↦ σ×(1, λ) = λ"); on its image, `f` is constant at `onePt ≫ fV`.
581	  set iotaGm : Gm kbar ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
582	    lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
583	      ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar with hιdef
584	  have hιf :
585	      iotaGm ≫ f = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
586	    calc iotaGm ≫ f
587	        = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
588	            ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar ≫ f) := by rw [hιdef, Category.assoc]
589	      _ = lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
590	            ≫ (fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar) ≫ fV) := by rw [key]
591	      _ = (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) (𝟙 (Gm kbar))
592	            ≫ fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) (Gm kbar)) ≫ fV := by
593	          rw [Category.assoc]
594	      _ = (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar) ≫ fV := by rw [lift_fst]
595	      _ = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
596	          rw [Category.assoc]
597	  -- Separatedness of the target `A` over `Spec k̄` in `OverClass.fromOver` form.
598	  haveI hAsep : IsSeparated A.hom := IsProper.toIsSeparated
599	  haveI : IsSeparated (A.left ↘ Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := hAsep
600	  -- `IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` and the product geom-irred / LOFT / IsReduced
601	  -- instances are all auto-resolved via Lane A's exports in `Genus0BaseObjects`
602	  -- (`projectiveLineBar_isReduced`, `projGm_geomIrred`, `projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`,
603	  -- `projGm_isReduced`). Dominance of `ι.left` is the only file-local bridge, cited
604	  -- explicitly via `iotaGm_isDominant` (the `set`-binding `hιdef` exposes `iotaGm.left`
605	  -- definitionally as `(lift _ _ ≫ gmScalingP1 kbar).left`).
606	  haveI hιDom : IsDominant iotaGm.left := iotaGm_isDominant
607	  -- Globalise: `ι ≫ f = ι ≫ (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ (onePt ≫ fV))` gives, by dominance, `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ c`.
608	  have hf_eq :
609	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
610	    -- Promote the Over morphism equality from the underlying scheme equality.
611	    have hgoal :
612	        iotaGm ≫ f
613	          = iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
614	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)) := by
615	      have hreshape :
616	          iotaGm ≫ (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
617	              (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV))
618	            = toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV) := by
619	        rw [← Category.assoc,
620	          toUnit_unique (iotaGm ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit (Gm kbar))]
621	      rw [hreshape, hιf]
622	    refine Over.OverMorphism.ext ?_
623	    refine ext_of_isDominant_of_isSeparated' (S := Spec (.of kbar))
624	      (X := (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left) (Y := A.left)
625	      (f := f.left)
626	      (g := (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
627	        (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV)).left) iotaGm.left ?_
628	    rw [← Over.comp_left, ← Over.comp_left]
629	    exact congrArg Over.Hom.left hgoal
630	  -- Pin `onePt ≫ fV = η[A]` via the basepoint hypothesis `hf0`.
631	  have hc : ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar ≫ fV = η[A] := by
632	    have hcomp := hf0
633	    rw [hf_eq] at hcomp
634	    rw [← Category.assoc,
635	      toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
636	        (𝟙 _),
637	      Category.id_comp] at hcomp
638	    exact hcomp

641	/-- **A morphism `ℙ¹ → A` is constant.** Over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
642	morphism `f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A` from the projective line into an abelian variety
643	`A` (a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme) is constant: it factors through
644	a single `k̄`-point `a₀ : 𝟙_ ⟶ A`, i.e. `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
645	
646	The single-curve base case of Milne's Proposition 3.10. **Route resolved iter-164: the
647	𝔾ₘ-scaling shortcut** — NO theorem of the cube, NO Milne Thm 3.2, NO `Hom(𝔾ₐ, A) = 0`,
648	char-general. The proof body proceeds by translating in the group `A` to reduce to the
649	basepoint case `zeroPt ≫ f = η[A]` (handled by the helper
650	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`), then un-translating.
651	
652	See blueprint `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`
653	(Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, Prop. 3.10).
654	
655	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via the helper's residuals
656	(three product-instance Mathlib bridges + dominance of the canonical `Gm → ℙ¹` map). Lifts
657	to axiom-clean once those are discharged. -/
658	theorem morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const
659	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
660	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
661	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
662	    (f : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) :
663	    ∃ a₀ : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ A,
664	      f = toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ a₀ := by
665	  -- Witness: `a₀ := zeroPt ≫ f` (the value of `f` at the scaling fixed point `0 ∈ ℙ¹`).
666	  refine ⟨ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f, ?_⟩
667	  -- Translate: form `f' := f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)` and apply the helper to `f'`.
668	  have hf' :
669	      (f / (toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f))
670	        = (1 : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A) := by
671	    apply morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux
672	    -- Show `zeroPt ≫ (f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀)) = η[A]`.
673	    rw [GrpObj.comp_div]
674	    have hv :
675	        ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫
676	            toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫
677	              (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f)
678	          = ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ f := by
679	      rw [← Category.assoc,
680	        toUnit_unique (ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt kbar ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar))
681	          (𝟙 _),
682	        Category.id_comp]
683	    rw [hv, div_self', Hom.one_def, toUnit_unique (toUnit _) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
684	  -- Untranslate: `f / (toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀) = 1` ⟺ `f = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀`.
685	  exact div_eq_one.mp hf'
686	
687	/-- **A genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`.** Over an algebraically closed field
688	`k̄`, a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` with `genus C = 0` is isomorphic — in
689	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))` — to the concrete projective line `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`.
690	
691	Hartshorne's Example IV.1.3.5 (Riemann–Roch). Its formalisation is a genuine sub-build:
692	Mathlib has no Riemann–Roch for curves; this is the dominant long pole flagged by the
693	iter-164 progress-critic.
694	
695	See blueprint `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
696	(Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, Example IV.1.3.5).
697	
698	**Status (iter-166):** signature refactored to the concrete `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`; body
699	remains `sorry` (RR bridge — iter-167+). -/
700	theorem genusZero_curve_iso_P1
701	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
702	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
703	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
704	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0) :
705	    Nonempty (C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar) :=
706	  sorry
707	
708	/-- **Headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-`0` curve.** Over an algebraically closed field
709	`k̄` (arbitrary characteristic — no `[CharZero kbar]`), let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically
710	irreducible curve with `genus C = 0` and `A` an abelian variety (smooth proper geom-irred group
711	scheme). Then every morphism `f : C ⟶ A` killing a `k̄`-point `p` (`p ≫ f = η[A]`) equals the
712	constant morphism at the identity, `f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
713	
714	This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that `genusZeroWitness` consumes
715	(via the `k̄ → k` descent step hosted in `AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian`). Its signature mirrors
716	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` of `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar` **verbatim except**
717	the `[CharZero kbar]` instance is dropped. Combine `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (`C ≅ ℙ¹`) with
718	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` (`ℙ¹ → A` constant) and pin the constant value to `η[A]` via
719	the pointed hypothesis. No `df = 0`, no Serre duality, no Picard representability.
720	
721	**Status (iter-166):** body landed. Carries propagated `sorryAx` via `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`
722	(RR bridge, iter-167+) and `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`'s helper residuals. Once the RR
723	bridge closes and the helper's product-instance + dominance sorries discharge, lifts to
724	axiom-clean. -/
725	theorem rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme
726	    [IsAlgClosed kbar]
727	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
728	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
729	    [IsProper C.hom]
730	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
731	    (_hgenus : genus C = 0)
732	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
733	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
734	    (f : C ⟶ A)
735	    (p : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ C)
736	    (_hf : p ≫ f = η[A]) :
737	    f = (toUnit C ≫ η[A]) := by
738	  -- Transport `f` along `C ≅ ProjectiveLineBar kbar` to a morphism `g : ℙ¹ ⟶ A`.
739	  obtain ⟨φ⟩ := genusZero_curve_iso_P1 _hgenus
740	  set g : ProjectiveLineBar kbar ⟶ A := φ.inv ≫ f with hgdef
741	  -- `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` gives `g = toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ a₀` for some `a₀`.
742	  obtain ⟨a₀, hga₀⟩ := morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const g
743	  -- Pin `a₀ = η[A]` via the pointed hypothesis on `f`.
744	  have hpoint : (p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ g = η[A] := by
745	    rw [hgdef, Category.assoc, ← Category.assoc φ.hom, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
746	    exact _hf
747	  have hcst : a₀ = η[A] := by
748	    rw [hga₀, ← Category.assoc,
749	      toUnit_unique ((p ≫ φ.hom) ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (𝟙 _),
750	      Category.id_comp] at hpoint
751	    exact hpoint
752	  rw [hcst] at hga₀
753	  -- Back-transport: `f = φ.hom ≫ g = φ.hom ≫ toUnit ℙ¹ ≫ η[A] = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`.
754	  calc f
755	      = φ.hom ≫ φ.inv ≫ f := by
756	        rw [← Category.assoc, φ.hom_inv_id, Category.id_comp]
757	    _ = φ.hom ≫ g := by rw [hgdef]
758	    _ = φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ≫ η[A] := by rw [hga₀]
759	    _ = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
760	        rw [← Category.assoc,
761	          toUnit_unique (φ.hom ≫ toUnit (ProjectiveLineBar kbar)) (toUnit C)]
762	
763	
764	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	\chapter{Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-\(0\) curve into an abelian variety are constant}
2	\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}
3	\label{chap:avr_for_rr}
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityLemma.lean
5	% Local macro: diagrammatic (Lean `≫`) composition. Defined locally because this
6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
10	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
11	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
12	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
13	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
14	% keystone directly). The headline prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
15	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
16	% \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
17	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
18	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
19	The genus-\(0\) arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
20	(\(J = \Spec k\)); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
21	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\)
22	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
23	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field \(k\), so the
24	cheaper differential argument (via $H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
25	O(-2)) = 0\(, hence \)df = 0\(, hence \)f$ constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
26	characteristic-\(p\) Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant \(f\) can have \(df = 0\). The
27	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
28	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
29	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
30	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
31	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
32	derives the genus-\(0\) base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
33	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
35	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that \(\mathbb P^1\) is
36	\emph{already complete} and \(f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
37	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
38	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{\(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut} (the project's
39	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
40	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action $\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
41	\mathbb P^1\(, \)(x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\( (the M\"obius scaling fixing both \)0$ and
42	\(\infty\)), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of $\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
43	G_m\( (\cref{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising \)f(0) = \eta_A\( and composing \)h := \sigma_\times
44	\fatsemi f\(, one applies additivity of \)\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)$ over a product
45	(\cref{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
46	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) proper, \(W = \mathbb G_m\), and
47	base points \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\), \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\). Because \(0\) is a \emph{fixed point} of
48	scaling, the \(W\)-axis restriction \(\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)\) is
49	\emph{constant} at \(\eta_A\), so the decomposition collapses to \(h = p \fatsemi f\), i.e.\
50	\(f(\lambda x) = f(x)\). Specialising \(x = 1\) gives that \(f|_{\mathbb G_m}\) is constant, and since
51	\(\mathbb G_m\) is dense in \(\mathbb P^1\) with \(A\) separated, \(f\) is constant
52	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
53	\cref{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
54	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
55	\(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\), and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
56	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive
57	argument (\cref{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the \(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility
58	\cref{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
59	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-\(0\) critical path. The corollary that a
60	pointed regular map of abelian
61	varieties is a homomorphism (\cref{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
62	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-\(0\) path; Milne's
63	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{lem:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
64	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-\(0\) critical path
65	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
66	gives that every morphism \(\mathbb P^1 \to A\) is constant
67	(\cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-\(0\)
68	curves (\cref{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}, \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) over \(\bar k\)) yields the
69	headline \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
70	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
71	neither Serre duality nor the FGA \(\Pic\) representability engine, so the genus-\(0\) arm is
72	fully decoupled from Route~A.
73	\medskip
74	Throughout, \(\bar k\) is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
75	\emph{abelian variety} over \(\bar k\) is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
76	project's Lean encoding \(A\) is an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) carrying
77	\(\mathtt{GrpObj}\), \(\mathtt{IsProper}\), \(\mathtt{Smooth}\), and \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\).
78	\section{The Rigidity Lemma}
79	\begin{lemma}
80	\leanok
81	[Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I)]
82	  \label{thm:rigidity_lemma}
83	  \uses{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
84	  
85	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}
86	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
87	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let \(X\) be a complete variety, \(Y\) and \(Z\)
88	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
89	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a
90	  % morphism \(g \colon Y \to Z\) such that if \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
91	  % projection, \(f = g \circ p_2\)."
92	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43.}
93	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety, and let \(Y\), \(Z\) be any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
94	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism such that for some \(y_0 \in Y\) the image
95	  \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0 \in Z\). Then there is a morphism
96	  \(g \colon Y \to Z\) with \(f = g \circ p_2\), where \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
97	  projection; that is, \(f\) depends only on the \(Y\)-coordinate, and \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\) for any
98	  fixed \(x_0 \in X\).
99	\end{lemma}

1224	[\(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is reduced]
1225	  \label{lem:projlinebar_isReduced}
1226	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_isReduced}
1227	  % Archon-original Lean realisation of an elementary textbook fact (projective space over
1228	  % a field is reduced) via the explicit chart cover of \cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}
1229	  % and the canonical \(\mathrm{val}\)-injection of a homogeneous localization into the
1230	  % corresponding plain localization. No external % SOURCE QUOTE needed.
1231	  The scheme underlying \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is reduced. By the two-chart affine cover
1232	  (\cref{def:projlinebar_affine_cover}) it suffices to show that each chart ring is
1233	  reduced. The chart-\(i\) ring \(\mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i\) embeds into the ordinary
1234	  localization \(\bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]\) of the polynomial ring \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) at
1235	  the powers of \(X_i\) via the canonical injection $\mathrm{val} \colon
1236	  \mathrm{Away}\,\mathcal A\,X_i \hookrightarrow \bar k[X_0, X_1][X_i^{-1}]$
1237	  (\(\mathtt{HomogeneousLocalization.val\_injective}\)); the codomain is a localization of a
1238	  polynomial ring at a non-zero-divisor (the powers of \(X_i\)), hence a domain, hence
1239	  reduced. A subring of a reduced ring is reduced
1240	  (\(\mathtt{Function.Injective.isDomain}\) on the \(\mathrm{val}\)-embedding), so each chart
1241	  ring is a domain --- in particular reduced --- and the global reducedness follows.
1242	\end{lemma}

87	private noncomputable def kbarToAwayRingHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
88	    kbar →+*
89	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)

95	/-- **The inverse direction of the chart-ring iso**: `k̄[u] →+* Away 𝒜 (X i)` via the
96	universal property of `MvPolynomial Unit`, sending `X () ↦ X (otherFin i) / X i`. -/
97	noncomputable def mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
98	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
99	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+*
100	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
101	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
102	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i)
103	    (fun _ : Unit =>

106	        (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)))

108	/-- Round-trip on `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`: `forward ∘ inverse = id`. -/
109	private lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
110	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i).comp
111	        (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) =
112	      RingHom.id (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
113	  apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
114	  · intro r
115	    simp only [RingHom.id_apply, mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway,
116	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C, kbarToAwayRingHom, RingHom.comp_apply,
117	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
118	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply]
119	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
120	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
121	        (Localization.mk ((algebraMap kbar
122	            ((projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) 0) r : _) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
123	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^0, ⟨0, rfl⟩⟩) =
124	      MvPolynomial.C r
125	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
126	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
127	    simp [SetLike.GradeZero.coe_algebraMap, chartEvalRingHom]
128	  · intro _
129	    simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingHom.id_apply,
130	      mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X',
131	      homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly]
132	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
133	      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk]
134	    change (Localization.awayLift (chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
135	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) _)
136	        (Localization.mk ((MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) :
137	            MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ))
138	          ⟨(MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)^(1:ℕ), ⟨1, rfl⟩⟩) =
139	      MvPolynomial.X ()
140	    rw [Localization.awayLift_mk (f := chartEvalRingHom kbar i)
141	      (r := MvPolynomial.X i) (v := 1) (hv := by simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_self])]
142	    simp [chartEvalRingHom_X_other, pow_one]

144	/-- **The inverse map `k̄[u] → Away 𝒜 (X i)` is surjective.**
145	
146	Its image is `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) { isLocalizationElem (X i) (X (otherFin i)) }` since
147	`isLocalizationElem` is the image of the single generator `X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar`
148	and `kbarToAwayRingHom` covers the scalars (via the `kbar ≃ 𝒜 0` bijection). By
149	`Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.GradedAlgebra.HomogeneousLocalization:1064`)
150	specialised to `d = 1`, `ι' = Fin 2`, `v = ![X 0, X 1]`, `dv = ![1, 1]`, this adjoin is `⊤`.
151	
152	Proof structure (iter-172):
153	1. Apply `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` with `d = 1, v = ![X 0, X 1], dv = ![1, 1]` to
154	   get `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) {Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _ | (a, ai) with a₀+a₁=a, ai≤1} = ⊤`.
155	2. Induct on adjoin membership (via `Algebra.adjoin_induction`):
156	   - `mem`: each generator `Away.mk hf a (X 0^a₀ * X 1^a₁) _` equals `isLocalizationElem^k`
157	     where `k = a₀` if i=1 else `a₁`. Hence it's `f (X ()^k)`.
158	   - `algebraMap`: every `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away r` is `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away
159	     (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0) r₀) = algebraMap kbar Away r₀ = f (C r₀)` since `algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0)`
160	     is surjective (see `projectiveLineBar_isProper`).
161	   - `add`/`mul`: `f` is a ring hom. -/
162	private lemma mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway_surjective
163	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
164	    Function.Surjective (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) := by
165	  classical
166	  -- We avoid `set 𝒜 := ...` here because it causes type-class friction with
167	  -- `Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem` and the `SetLike.GradeZero` coercion machinery.
168	  have hfi : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
169	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i
170	  have hgi : (MvPolynomial.X (otherFin i) : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
171	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 1 :=
172	    MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar (otherFin i)
173	  -- The 2-generator vector and degrees for `Away.adjoin_mk_prod_pow_eq_top` (d = 1).
174	  let v : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := ![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1]
175	  let dv : Fin 2 → ℕ := ![1, 1]
176	  have hxd : ∀ j, v j ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar (dv j) := by
177	    intro j; fin_cases j <;> exact MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X _ _
178	  -- Step 1: `Algebra.adjoin (𝒜 0) (range v) = ⊤` (i.e. {X 0, X 1} generates `k̄[X 0, X 1]`
179	  -- over `𝒜 0`). We isolate the induction inside a `have` to avoid motive contamination.
180	  have hx : Algebra.adjoin ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0) (Set.range v) = ⊤ := by
181	    apply top_unique
182	    intro p _
183	    refine MvPolynomial.induction_on p ?C ?add ?mulX
184	    · -- C case: MvPolynomial.C r ∈ adjoin via algebraMap_mem.
185	      intro r
186	      have h : (algebraMap ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
187	          (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))
188	          ⟨MvPolynomial.C r, MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_C _ _⟩ = MvPolynomial.C r :=
189	        SetLike.GradeZero.algebraMap_apply _ _
190	      rw [← h]
191	      exact Subalgebra.algebraMap_mem _ _
192	    · -- add case
193	      intro p₁ p₂ hp₁ hp₂
194	      exact Subalgebra.add_mem _ hp₁ hp₂

325	`Localization.awayLift`) and the inverse map (via `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`). The two
326	round-trips are proved at the underlying-`Localization.Away` level by
327	`HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective`. -/
328	noncomputable def homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
329	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
330	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃+*
331	      MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=
332	  RingEquiv.ofRingHom
333	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
334	    (mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i)
335	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i)
336	    (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_left kbar i)
337	
338	/-- **`kbar`-algebra preservation of `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`** (iter-174 Sub-task A
339	helper per `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3 step 3). The forward map
340	`Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar` carries `algebraMap kbar Away` to
341	`algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)` (i.e. `MvPolynomial.C`).
342	
343	Closed via the inverse's `eval₂Hom_C` action: `inverse (C r) = kbarToAwayRingHom kbar i r =
344	algebraMap kbar Away r` (the last identity is definitional, by the `algebraKbarAway`
345	`Algebra.compHom` instance combined with `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`). The
346	forward round-trip then gives `forward (algebraMap kbar Away r) = C r`. -/
347	lemma homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap
348	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
349	    ((homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom).comp
350	        (algebraMap kbar
351	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
352	            (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar))) =
353	      algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) := by
354	  ext r
355	  -- `algebraMap kbar Away r = mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (C r)` since
356	  -- the `algebraKbarAway` instance is `Algebra.compHom _ (algebraMap kbar (𝒜 0))` and
357	  -- `algebraMap (𝒜 0) Away = HomogeneousLocalization.fromZeroRingHom 𝒜 _` (see
358	  -- `HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_eq`), so the composite is exactly `kbarToAwayRingHom`
359	  -- which equals `inverse (C r)` by `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C`.
360	  have hinv : mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i (MvPolynomial.C r) =
361	      algebraMap kbar
362	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
363	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) r := by
364	    unfold mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway
365	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
366	    rfl
367	  -- Now apply forward (= homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly) and use aux_right's roundtrip.
368	  have hround : (homogeneousLocalizationAwayToMvPoly kbar i)
369	      ((mvPolyToHomogeneousLocalizationAway kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)) =
370	      MvPolynomial.C r :=
371	    RingHom.congr_fun (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_aux_right kbar i) (MvPolynomial.C r)
372	  -- Combine: rewrite the inverse C r as algebraMap kbar Away r, then apply roundtrip.
373	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, RingEquiv.toRingHom_eq_coe,
374	    RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso, RingEquiv.ofRingHom_apply]
375	  rw [← hinv, hround]
376	  rfl
377	
378	end AlgebraicGeometry

380	end

40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r

  - `(· + ·)` is shorthand for `fun x y => x + y`
  - `(f · a b)` is shorthand for `fun x => f x a b`
