395	% \smallsetminus U\(. Because \)V$ is normal, its associated local ring is a discrete
396	% valuation ring \(\mathcal O_Z\) with field of fractions \(k(V)\). The map \(\varphi\)
397	% defines a morphism of schemes \(\operatorname{Spec}(k(V)) \to W\), which the
398	% valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne 1977, II 4.7) shows extends to a
399	% morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_Z) \to W\). This implies that \(\varphi\)
400	% has a representative defined on an open subset that meets \(Z\) in a nonempty set,
401	% which is a contradiction."
402	\begin{proof}
403	  
404	  Following Milne's two-step proof.
405	
406	  \emph{Step 1: rule out codim-\(1\) components.} Suppose for contradiction that \(Z(f)\)
407	  contains a prime divisor \(W \subseteq X\) (i.e.\ a codim-\(1\) irreducible component).
408	  Let \(\eta \in W\) be its generic point, so \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta\}} = 1\). By
409	  \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}\) is a discrete
410	  valuation ring with fraction field the function field \(K(X)\). Any representative
411	  \((U, \varphi_U) \in f\) has \(U \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f) \neq \emptyset\) and so determines
412	  a \(\bar k\)-algebra map from the function field \(K(Y)\) of \(Y\) to \(K(X)\): explicitly,
413	  \(\varphi_U\) defines a morphism of schemes \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X)) \to Y\) (since
414	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(K(X))\) is the generic point of \(X\)). By the valuative criterion
415	  of properness applied to the complete variety \(Y\)
416	  (\emph{cf.}\ Hartshorne~II.4.7, cited verbatim in Milne~3.1's proof), this morphism
417	  extends to a morphism \(\operatorname{Spec}(\mathcal O_{X, \eta}) \to Y\). Standard
418	  algebraic-geometry spreading then exhibits an open neighbourhood \(V\) of \(\eta\) in
419	  \(X\) on which \(f\) has a regular representative; in particular $\eta \in
420	  \mathrm{Dom}(f)\(, contradicting \)\eta \in W \subseteq Z(f)\(. Hence no codim-\)1$
421	  component of \(X\) lies in \(Z(f)\), i.e.\ \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\).
422	
423	  \emph{Step 2: extend across codim-\(\geq 2\) points (the "codim-1-indeterminacy-free
424	  \(\Rightarrow\) extension" half).} Suppose now that \(Z(f)\) has codimension \(\geq 2\)
425	  but is non-empty (we want to deduce \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) under the additional input
426	  that ``\(f\) is already codim-1-indeterminacy-free, by hypothesis''). Pick a closed
427	  point \(x \in Z(f)\) with \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{x\}} \geq 2\). By
428	  \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} applied to the regular local ring
429	  \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\), we have
430	  \(\mathrm{depth}(\mathcal O_{X, x}) = \dim(\mathcal O_{X, x}) \geq 2\). The sheaf
431	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on the punctured neighbourhood \(V \smallsetminus \{x\}\)
432	  (any small affine neighbourhood \(V \ni x\)) has \(H^0_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\)
433	  and \(H^1_{\{x\}}(V, \mathcal O_X) = 0\) (the depth-\(\geq 2\) characterisation of
434	  local-cohomology vanishing, Stacks tag \texttt{0AVF}; cf.\ Hartshorne~III.3.5 for
435	  the surface case). Pull back the coordinates of any affine chart of \(Y\) around the
436	  closure \(\overline{f(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \cap V)}\) along \(f\); the resulting sections of
437	  \(\mathcal O_X\) on \(V \cap \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) extend uniquely to sections on \(V\),
438	  yielding a regular representative of \(f\) on \(V\). Hence \(x \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\),
439	  contradicting \(x \in Z(f)\). Therefore \(Z(f) = \emptyset\) and \(f\) extends to a
440	  regular morphism \(\widetilde f \colon X \to Y\).
441	
442	  Uniqueness of \(\widetilde f\) is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement
443	  principle: two morphisms \(X \to Y\) that agree on the dense open \(\mathrm{Dom}(f)\)
444	  agree on all of \(X\).
445	\end{proof}
446	
447	\begin{remark}
448	  \label{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}
449	  \textbf{Where Auslander--Buchsbaum enters.} Step~1 of the proof uses only normality
450	  + the valuative criterion of properness (Hartshorne~II.4.7) and is characteristic-free.
451	  Step~2 uses the depth-\(\geq 2\) extension property at a codim-\(\geq 2\) regular
452	  point, which is exactly what \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay} of
453	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum} supplies (regular local of dimension \(d\)
454	  \(\Rightarrow\) Cohen--Macaulay of depth \(d\)). For our application the source \(X\) is a
455	  smooth variety, so the regular-local-ring hypothesis is automatic; the Cohen--Macaulay
456	  consequence is what feeds the local-cohomology vanishing.
457	
458	  Concretely, when \(X\) is a smooth surface (e.g.\ \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb P^1\) or
459	  \(C \times C\) for a smooth projective curve \(C\)), the codim-\(\geq 2\) closed points of
460	  \(X\) are exactly the closed points of \(X\) (because \(\dim X = 2\)), and at each such
461	  point \(\mathcal O_{X, x}\) is regular of dimension \(2\), hence Cohen--Macaulay of
462	  depth \(2\). The smooth-surface case is the one A.4.c will invoke when extending the
463	  symmetric-product rational map \(C^{(g)} \dashrightarrow A\) along the Abel--Jacobi
464	  birational equivalence to \(J = \Pic^0_{C/k}\).
465	\end{remark}
466	
467	\section{Milne Lemma 3.3: indeterminacy locus of a map to a group variety is codim 1}
468	\label{sec:milne_lem33}
469	
470	The second input to Milne's Theorem~3.2 is structural: when the target \(Y\) is a group
471	variety, the indeterminacy locus is either empty or of \emph{pure} codimension \(1\).
472	Combined with \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s ``codimension \(\geq 2\)'' conclusion,
473	this forces the locus to be empty.
474	
475	\begin{lemma}
476	
477	\leanok
478	  [Milne Lemma~3.3: pure-codim-\(1\) structure of the indeterminacy locus into a group
479	  variety]
480	  \label{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}
481	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme}
482	  
483	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17
484	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 23).
485	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "\textsc{Lemma 3.3.} \emph{Let \(\varphi \colon V \dashrightarrow G\)
486	  % be a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety. Then either
487	  % \(\varphi\) is defined on all of \(V\) or the points where it is not defined form a
488	  % closed subset of pure codimension 1 in \(V\) (i.e., a finite union of prime
489	  % divisors).}"
490	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, Lemma~3.3, \S I.3, p.~17.}
491	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(G\) be a group variety over
492	  \(\bar k\) (a smooth group scheme of finite type), and let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow
493	  G\( be a rational map. Then either \)f\( is defined on all of \)X$, or the indeterminacy
494	  locus \(Z(f) \subseteq X\) is a closed subset of pure codimension \(1\) (i.e.\ a finite
495	  union of prime divisors).
496	
497	  Equivalently: it is \emph{never} the case that \(f\) has a non-empty indeterminacy
498	  locus all of whose irreducible components have codimension \(\geq 2\).
499	\end{lemma}
500	
501	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "\textsc{Proof.} Define a rational map
502	%   \(\Phi \colon V \times V \dashrightarrow G\), $\;(x, y) \mapsto \varphi(x) \cdot
503	%   \varphi(y)^{-1}$.
504	% More precisely, if \((U, \varphi_U)\) represents \(\varphi\), then \(\Phi\) is the rational
505	% map represented by
506	%   $U \times U \xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U} G \times G
507	%   \xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}} G
508	%   \xrightarrow{m} G$.
509	% Clearly \(\Phi\) is defined at a diagonal point \((x, x)\) if \(\varphi\) is defined at
510	% \(x\), and then \(\Phi(x, x) = e\). Conversely, if \(\Phi\) is defined at \((x, x)\), then
511	% it is defined on an open neighbourhood of \((x, x)\); in particular, there will be an
512	% open subset \(U\) of \(V\) such that \(\Phi\) is defined on \(\{x\} \times U\). After
513	% possible replacing \(U\) by a smaller open subset (not necessarily containing \(x\)),
514	% \(\varphi\) will be defined on \(U\). For \(u \in U\), the formula
515	%   \(\varphi(x) = \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u)\)
516	% defines \(\varphi\) at \(x\). Thus \(\varphi\) is defined at \(x\) if and only if \(\Phi\) is
517	% defined at \((x, x)\). The rational map \(\Phi\) defines a map
518	%   \(\Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \to k(V \times V)\).
519	% Since \(\Phi\) sends \((x, x)\) to \(e\) if it is defined there, it follows that \(\Phi\)
520	% is defined at \((x, x)\) if and only if
521	%   \(\operatorname{Im}(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subset \mathcal O_{V \times V, (x, x)}\).
522	% Now \(V \times V\) is nonsingular, and so we have a good theory of divisors (AG,
523	% Chapter 12). For a nonzero rational function \(f\) on \(V \times V\), write
524	%   \(\operatorname{div}(f) = \operatorname{div}(f)_0 - \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\),
525	% with \(\operatorname{div}(f)_0\) and \(\operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\) effective divisors
526	% --- note that \(\operatorname{div}(f)_\infty = \operatorname{div}(f^{-1})_0\). Then
527	%   $\mathcal O_{V \times V, (x, x)} = \{f \in k(V \times V) \mid
528	%   \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x)\} \cup \{0\}$.
529	% Suppose \(\phi\) is not defined at \(x\). Then for some $f \in \operatorname{Im}
530	% (\varphi^\ast)\(, \)(x, x) \in \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\(, and clearly \)\Phi$ is
531	% not defined at the points \((y, y) \in \Delta \cap \operatorname{div}(f)_\infty\).
532	% This is a subset of pure codimension one in \(\Delta\) (AG 9.2), and when we identify
533	% it with a subset of \(V\), it is a subset of \(V\) of codimension one passing through
534	% \(x\) on which \(\varphi\) is not defined."
535	\begin{proof}
536	  
537	  Following Milne's proof of Lemma~3.3. The argument has four sub-steps. Throughout we
538	  fix a representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\).
539	
540	  \emph{Sub-step 1: the difference rational map.} Define
541	  \[
542	    \Phi \colon X \times X \dashrightarrow G,
543	    \qquad
544	    \Phi(x, y) \;:=\; \varphi(x) \cdot \varphi(y)^{-1},
545	  \]
546	  via the composition
547	  \[
548	    U \times U
549	    \;\xrightarrow{\varphi_U \times \varphi_U}\; G \times G
550	    \;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{id} \times \mathrm{inv}}\; G \times G
551	    \;\xrightarrow{m}\; G,
552	  \]
553	  where \(\mathrm{inv} \colon G \to G\) is the group-variety inverse and
554	  \(m \colon G \times G \to G\) is the group law. The map \(\Phi\) is rational by
555	  composition: its domain is at least \(U \times U \subseteq X \times X\), which is dense
556	  because \(U\) is dense in \(X\).
557	
558	  \emph{Sub-step 2: \(\Phi\)-defined at \((x, x)\) \(\Leftrightarrow\) \(\varphi\)-defined at
559	  \(x\).} If \(\varphi\) is defined at \(x\), the explicit formula \(\Phi(x, x) = e\) (the
560	  identity of \(G\)) shows \(\Phi\) is defined at \((x, x)\). Conversely, if \(\Phi\) is
561	  defined at \((x, x)\), openness of \(\mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)\) in \(X \times X\) produces an
562	  open set \(W \ni (x, x)\) on which \(\Phi\) is defined; by projection (taking the slice
563	  \(\{x\} \times W' \subseteq W\) for some open \(W' \subseteq X\), possibly shrinking
564	  \(W'\) so \(\varphi\) is defined on \(W'\) as well --- \(W'\) need not contain \(x\)), the
565	  identity
566	  \[
567	    \varphi(x) \;=\; \Phi(x, u) \cdot \varphi(u),
568	    \qquad u \in W',
569	  \]
570	  exhibits a regular representative of \(\varphi\) at \(x\). Hence $x \in
571	  \mathrm{Dom}(\varphi)\( if and only if \)(x, x) \in \mathrm{Dom}(\Phi)$.
572	
573	  \emph{Sub-step 3: rephrase via pullback of local rings.} The rational map \(\Phi\)
574	  pulls \(\mathcal O_{G, e}\) back to a \(\bar k\)-subalgebra of the function field
575	  \(K(X \times X)\):
576	  \[
577	    \Phi^\ast \colon \mathcal O_{G, e} \;\longrightarrow\; K(X \times X).
578	  \]
579	  Because \(\Phi\) sends \((x, x)\) to \(e\) wherever it is defined, \(\Phi\) is defined at
580	  \((x, x)\) if and only if $\Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e}) \subseteq \mathcal O_{X
581	  \times X, (x, x)}\( (i.e.\ every local function on \)G\( near \)e$ has no pole at
582	  \((x, x)\) after pullback).
583	
584	  \emph{Sub-step 4: pole locus is pure codimension \(1\).} The variety \(X \times X\) is
585	  nonsingular (smoothness is stable under products over \(\bar k\)), so the Weil-divisor
586	  apparatus of \cref{def:codim1_cycles} and the order map of
587	  \cref{def:order_at_point} apply on \(X \times X\). For any nonzero rational function
588	  \(f \in K(X \times X)^\ast\) decompose its divisor as
589	  \[
590	    \mathrm{div}(f) \;=\; \mathrm{div}(f)_0 - \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty,
591	  \]
592	  with \(\mathrm{div}(f)_0\) and \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) effective and
593	  \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = \mathrm{div}(f^{-1})_0\) (Milne's notation, matching the
594	  project's \(\div(f) = \sum \ord_Y(f) \cdot Y\) of
595	  \cref{def:principal_divisor}: \(\mathrm{div}(f)_0\) collects the prime divisors with
596	  \(\ord_Y(f) > 0\) and \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) those with \(\ord_Y(f) < 0\)). The local
597	  ring at \((x, x)\) can be characterised as
598	  \[
599	    \mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}
600	    \;=\; \bigl\{\, f \in K(X \times X) \;\big|\;
601	    \mathrm{div}(f)_\infty \text{ does not contain } (x, x) \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
602	  \]
603	  Suppose \(f \in K(X \times X)^\ast\) is not in \(\mathcal O_{X \times X, (x, x)}\);
604	  equivalently, \((x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty)\). The pole divisor
605	  \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty\) is a finite sum of prime divisors of \(X \times X\) (i.e.\ a
606	  closed subset of pure codimension \(1\) in \(X \times X\)). Its intersection with the
607	  diagonal \(\Delta_X \subseteq X \times X\) is a closed subset of pure codimension \(1\)
608	  in \(\Delta_X\) (this is Milne's reference to AG~9.2, the standard fact that a prime
609	  divisor of \(X \times X\) either contains \(\Delta_X\) or meets it in pure codimension
610	  \(1\); on a nonsingular variety this is an instance of Krull's principal-ideal theorem
611	  applied to the local equation of \(\Delta_X\) inside \(X \times X\)).
612	
613	  \emph{Conclusion.} Suppose \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow G\) is not defined on all of
614	  \(X\). Pick \(x \in Z(f)\). By Sub-steps~2--3, there is some $f_0 \in
615	  \Phi^\ast(\mathcal O_{G, e})\( with \)(x, x) \in \mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty)$.
616	  By Sub-step~4 the intersection $\mathrm{supp}(\mathrm{div}(f_0)_\infty) \cap
617	  \Delta_X\( is pure codimension \)1\( in \)\Delta_X\(, passing through \)(x, x)$.
618	  Identifying \(\Delta_X\) with \(X\) via the diagonal isomorphism, the corresponding
619	  closed subset of \(X\) has pure codimension \(1\) and passes through \(x\). By Sub-step~2,
620	  every point of this codim-\(1\) subset is a point where \(\Phi\) is undefined on the
621	  diagonal, hence where \(\varphi\) is undefined. Thus \(Z(\varphi)\) contains a pure
622	  codim-\(1\) closed subset passing through \(x\); since \(x \in Z(\varphi)\) was arbitrary,
623	  every irreducible component of \(Z(\varphi)\) has codimension exactly \(1\), i.e.\
624	  \(Z(\varphi)\) is of pure codimension \(1\).
625	\end{proof}
626	
627	\begin{remark}
628	  \label{rmk:milne_lem33_charfree}
629	  \textbf{Characteristic-free.} The above proof uses only: (i) the group law on \(G\)
630	  and its inverse (formal smoothness of \(G\) as a group object); (ii) the Weil-divisor
631	  apparatus on the nonsingular variety \(X \times X\) (Hartshorne~II.6, or
632	  \cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor} in the project's notation); (iii) the
633	  decomposition of a divisor on a nonsingular variety into effective pieces, and
634	  Krull's principal-ideal theorem on \(X \times X\). None of these has a characteristic
635	  restriction, so \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} holds over every
636	  algebraically closed base field, in arbitrary characteristic. (Milne states the
637	  lemma in this generality at \S I.3, p.~17.)
638	\end{remark}

640	\section{Weil-divisor obstruction to codim-1 extension}
641	\label{sec:weil_obstruction}
642	
643	The proof of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}'s Step~1 (codim-\(1\) ruling-out) goes
644	through the valuative criterion. In the project's Weil-divisor language
645	(\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}) the same obstruction has a cleaner phrasing:
646	the extension fails at a prime divisor \(W\) if and only if some affine coordinate of
647	\(f\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\) has a strictly negative order
648	\(\ord_W(\cdot) < 0\). This is the form the Lean prover needs when consuming
649	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
650	
651	\begin{theorem}
652	
653	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
654	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
655	  
656	  % NOTE (iter-179 review, lvb-checker `codimone-iter179` corrective):
657	  % `\lean{...}` pin DETACHED (Path D2 option (b) per the lvb-checker). The
658	  % substantive Hartshorne-II.6 valuative reformulation (the iff with
659	  % ord_W >= 0) below is the *intended* statement; the iter-179 Lane D
660	  % `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` decl is only a 2-LOC
661	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` reshuffle that does NOT prove the
662	  % ord_W content, so re-pinning `\lean{...}` to it would falsely claim
663	  % kernel-clean formalization of the substantive Hartshorne iff. The
664	  % weaker reshuffle proven this iter will be pinned to its own light
665	  % `\begin{lemma}` block (working title `lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle`)
666	  % by an iter-180+ blueprint-writer dispatch; the substantive Hartshorne
667	  % version stays pinned here but unlinked until a future
668	  % `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg` lemma carries
669	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.order` machinery (pullback `RationalMap →
670	  % function-field ring map`, not yet in Mathlib at b80f227). See
671	  % task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-codimone-iter179.md +
672	  % proof-journal/sessions/session_179/recommendations.md REC-14.
673	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, pp.~130--131 (the valuation \(v_Y\) associated to a
674	  % prime divisor, and the characterisation of \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_Y}\) as the
675	  % subring of \(K(X)\) on which \(v_Y \geq 0\)) (read from
676	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
677	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If \(Y\) is a prime divisor on \(X\), let \(\eta \in Y\) be its generic
678	  % point. Then the local ring \(\mathcal O_{\eta, X}\) of \(X\) at \(\eta\) is a discrete
679	  % valuation ring with quotient field \(K\), the function field of \(X\). We call the
680	  % corresponding discrete valuation \(v_Y\) the \emph{valuation of \(Y\)}."
681	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let \(f \in K^\ast\) be any nonzero rational function on \(X\).
682	  % Then \(v_Y(f)\) is an integer. If it is positive, we say \(f\) has a \emph{zero} along
683	  % \(Y\), of that order; if it is negative, we say \(f\) has a \emph{pole} along \(Y\), of
684	  % order \(-v_Y(f)\)."
685	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation \(v_Y\) and the order map).}
686	  Let \(X\) be a nonsingular variety over \(\bar k\), let \(Y\) be a variety over \(\bar k\),
687	  let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map, and let \(W \subseteq X\) be a
688	  prime divisor with generic point \(\eta_W \in W\) (so
689	  \(\mathrm{codim}_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1\)). Let \((U, \varphi_U)\) be any representative of
690	  \(f\) with \(U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset\). The following are equivalent:
691	  \begin{enumerate}
692	    \item \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) (i.e.\ \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\), equivalently
693	      \(W \not\subseteq Z(f)\)).
694	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood \(V \subseteq Y\) of
695	      the generic image of \(f\) near \(W\), every regular function $g \in
696	      \mathcal O_Y(V)\( pulls back to a rational function \)\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
697	      satisfying
698	      \[
699	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
700	      \]
701	  \end{enumerate}
702	
703	  Consequently: \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free
704	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
705	  \(W \subseteq X\) and every affine open \(V \subseteq Y\) as above, the pullback
706	  \(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\) has \(\ord_W \geq 0\) for all \(g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)\).
707	\end{theorem}
708	
709	\begin{proof}
710	  
711	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\) is a
712	  DVR with fraction field \(K(X)\) and uniformiser determined by \(W\); by
713	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly \(\ord_W\). The local ring at
714	  \(\eta_W\) is therefore characterised inside \(K(X)\) as the subring
715	  \[
716	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
717	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
718	  \]
719	  The rational map \(f\) is defined at \(\eta_W\) if and only if, for any affine open
720	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y\) around the image \(\overline{f(\eta_W)}\),
721	  the pullback \(\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)\) lands in
722	  \(\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}\): this is the standard local characterisation of where a
723	  rational map is regular at a codim-\(1\) point of a smooth variety (it is the
724	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
725	  equivalent to \(\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0\) for every \(g \in A\), which is
726	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~\(\Leftrightarrow\)~(2) is therefore established.
727	
728	  For the consequence, \(f\) is codim-\(1\)-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor \(W\) is
729	  contained in \(Z(f)\), which by the equivalence above is iff the \(\ord_W \geq 0\)
730	  condition holds for every prime divisor \(W\).
731	\end{proof}
732	
733	\begin{remark}
734	  \label{rmk:weil_obstruction_application}
735	  \textbf{Why this form is the prover-facing one.} In the consumer chapter
736	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} the Lean prover combines
737	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in
738	  isolation, the abstract codim-\(\geq 2\) statement
739	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} suffices and \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
740	  is not invoked. The Weil-divisor form is the bridge to the project's
741	  \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API, which is the form A.4.a's own internal sub-proofs
742	  use when establishing \emph{specific} extensions in genus-\(0\) classifying-map

743	  arguments and in the symmetric-product Albanese chapter
744	  \cref{chap:Albanese_AlbaneseUP}.
745	\end{remark}
746	
747	\begin{lemma}
748	
749	\leanok
750	  [Definedness at a prime divisor, repackaged as a through-the-point partial map]
751	  \label{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle}
752	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point}
753	  Let \(X\) and \(Y\) be varieties over \(\bar k\) and let
754	  \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map. For a prime divisor
755	  \(W \subseteq X\) with generic point \(\eta_W = W.\mathrm{point}\), the
756	  following are equivalent:
757	  \begin{enumerate}
758	    \item \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) (\(f\) is defined at the generic point of
759	      \(W\)).
760	    \item There exists a partial-map representative \((U, \varphi_U)\) of \(f\) ---
761	      i.e.\ a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) on a dense open
762	      \(U \subseteq X\) whose induced rational map equals \(f\) --- whose domain
763	      \(U\) contains \(\eta_W\).
764	  \end{enumerate}
765	\end{lemma}
766	
767	\begin{proof}
768	
769	  The lemma is a definitional reshuffle of the Mathlib membership predicate
770	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap.mem\_domain}, which already states that
771	  \(\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)\) iff there is some partial-map representative of
772	  \(f\) defined at \(\eta_W\); the only content here is reordering the two
773	  conjuncts under the existential so the \(\varphi_U \mapsto f\) component lands
774	  first (matching the way later witnesses in the project construct partial-map
775	  representatives). The forward and backward implications are immediate from
776	  the unfolded definition.
777	\end{proof}
778	
779	\begin{remark}
780	  \label{rmk:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle_scope}
781	  \textbf{Relationship to \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}.}
782	  \cref{lem:mem_domain_partial_map_reshuffle} is the iter-179 Lane~D honest
783	  fallback for the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 ``regular = no pole''
784	  reformulation that \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} states in its full
785	  \(\ord_W \geq 0\) form. The reshuffle captures only the
786	  definitional half of the equivalence (existence of a partial-map
787	  representative through a prime-divisor generic point); upgrading it to the
788	  substantive \(\ord_W \geq 0\) form requires the
789	  \texttt{Scheme.RationalMap}-to-function-field pullback machinery, which is
790	  not shipped by Mathlib at commit \texttt{b80f227}. See the iter-179
791	  \texttt{\% NOTE} block on \cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} for the
792	  history.
793	\end{remark}

907	\section{Out of scope}
908	\label{sec:codim1_out_of_scope}
909	
910	This chapter packages \emph{only} the two extension inputs and the Weil-divisor
911	reformulation needed for A.4.a. The following adjacent content is explicitly out of
912	scope:
913	\begin{itemize}
914	  \item \textbf{The Auslander--Buchsbaum formula and the regular \(\Rightarrow\)
915	    Cohen--Macaulay corollary.} Proved in \cref{chap:Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum}
916	    as \cref{thm:auslander_buchsbaum} and \cref{cor:regular_cohen_macaulay}; this
917	    chapter only consumes them (in Step~2 of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension}; see
918	    \cref{rmk:codim1_role_of_ab}).
919	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.2 itself.} The combination of
920	    \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} into the
921	    statement ``a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety
922	    extends'' lives in \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} as
923	    \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}; this chapter only supplies the two inputs.
924	  \item \textbf{Milne Theorem~3.4 / Corollary~3.7 / Theorem~3.8 / Proposition~3.9 /
925	    Proposition~3.10.} The remainder of Milne~\S I.3 (the ``map between AVs is
926	    translated homomorphism'' family of results, and the
927	    \(\mathbb A^1 \dashrightarrow A\) / unirational\(\dashrightarrow A\) constancy
928	    statements) is downstream of \cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends} but is not
929	    needed by A.4 in the project's current routing: the genus-\(0\) base case is
930	    handled via the proven Rigidity Lemma + \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut
931	    (\cref{chap:RigidityKbar}), and the positive-genus arm consumes only the bare
932	    Theorem~3.2 statement.
933	  \item \textbf{Weil-divisor degree on \(X \times X\) for \(X\) a curve.} The proof of
934	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} invokes the Weil-divisor apparatus on the
935	    smooth surface \(X \times X\) but does \emph{not} need the degree map of
936	    \cref{def:divisor_degree} (which is curve-specific); only the codim-\(1\)
937	    decomposition \(\div(f) = \div(f)_0 - \div(f)_\infty\) on a nonsingular surface is
938	    used. The bilinear degree formula on \(X \times X\) for \(X\) a curve (intersection
939	    numbers, Hartshorne~V.1) is out of scope.
940	  \item \textbf{Cartier divisors and the Cartier/Weil comparison.} On a smooth variety
941	    they coincide and the chapter uses Weil throughout; the Cartier formalism is not
942	    introduced.
943	  \item \textbf{Generalisation to non-algebraically-closed base fields.} The
944	    project's positive-genus arm runs over \(\bar k\) throughout; descent of the
945	    extension to a non-algebraically-closed base, if ever needed, would require
946	    Galois-descent machinery and is not in scope.
947	  \item \textbf{Resolution of indeterminacy by blow-ups} (Hironaka-style, for higher
948	    indeterminacy codimensions on a smooth variety with a non-group-variety target):
949	    out of scope. The combination of \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} and
950	    \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} for \(G\) a group variety is sharper than any
951	    blow-up-based approach because it produces an extension on the original \(X\)
952	    rather than on a blow-up; no blow-up apparatus is needed.
953	\end{itemize}

14	/-!
15	# Cotangent complex of a submersive presentation
16	
17	Let `P` be a submersive presentation of `S` as an `R`-algebra and
18	denote by `I` the kernel `R[X] → S`. We show
19	
20	- `SubmersivePresentation.free_cotangent`: `I ⧸ I ^ 2` is `S`-free on the classes of `P.relation i`.
21	- `SubmersivePresentation.subsingleton_h1Cotangent`: `H¹(L_{S/R}) = 0`.
22	- `SubmersivePresentation.free_kaehlerDifferential`: `Ω[S⁄R]` is `S`-free on the images of `dxᵢ`
23	  where `i ∉ Set.range P.map`.
24	- `SubmersivePresentation.rank_kaehlerDifferential`: If `S` is non-trivial, the rank of
25	  `Ω[S⁄R]` is the dimension of `P`.
26	
27	We also provide the corresponding instances for standard smooth algebras as corollaries.
28	
29	We keep the notation `I = ker(R[X] → S)` in all docstrings of this file.
30	-/
31	
32	@[expose] public section
33	
34	namespace Algebra
35	
36	variable {R S ι σ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
37	
38	section
39	
40	open Extension Module MvPolynomial
41	
42	namespace PreSubmersivePresentation
43	
44	/--
45	Given a pre-submersive presentation, this is the composition
46	`I ⧸ I ^ 2 → ⊕ S dxᵢ → ⊕ S dxᵢ` where the second direct sum runs over
47	all `i : σ` induced by the injection `P.map : σ → ι`.
48	
49	If `P` is submersive, this is an isomorphism. See `SubmersivePresentation.cotangentEquiv`.
50	-/
51	noncomputable def cotangentComplexAux [Finite σ] (P : PreSubmersivePresentation R S ι σ) :
52	    P.toExtension.Cotangent →ₗ[S] σ → S :=
53	  Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite S S σ ∘ₗ Finsupp.lcomapDomain _ P.map_inj ∘ₗ
54	    P.cotangentSpaceBasis.repr.toLinearMap ∘ₗ P.toExtension.cotangentComplex
55	
56	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
57	lemma cotangentComplexAux_apply [Finite σ] (P : PreSubmersivePresentation R S ι σ)
58	    (x : P.ker) (i : σ) :
59	    P.cotangentComplexAux (Cotangent.mk x) i = (aeval P.val) (pderiv (P.map i) x.val) := by
60	  dsimp only [cotangentComplexAux, LinearMap.coe_comp, LinearEquiv.coe_coe, Function.comp_apply,
61	    cotangentComplex_mk]
62	  simp only [Generators.toExtension_Ring, Finsupp.lcomapDomain_apply,
63	    Finsupp.linearEquivFunOnFinite_apply, Finsupp.comapDomain_apply,
64	    Generators.cotangentSpaceBasis_repr_tmul, one_mul]
65	
66	lemma cotangentComplexAux_zero_iff [Finite σ] {P : PreSubmersivePresentation R S ι σ} (x : P.ker) :
67	    P.cotangentComplexAux (Cotangent.mk x) = 0 ↔
68	      ∀ i : σ, (aeval P.val) (pderiv (P.map i) x.val) = 0 := by
69	  rw [funext_iff]
70	  simp_rw [cotangentComplexAux_apply, Pi.zero_apply]
71	
72	end PreSubmersivePresentation
73	
74	namespace SubmersivePresentation
75	
76	variable [Finite σ] (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ)
77	
78	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
79	lemma cotangentComplexAux_injective : Function.Injective P.cotangentComplexAux := by
80	  rw [← LinearMap.ker_eq_bot, eq_bot_iff]
81	  intro x hx
82	  obtain ⟨(x : P.ker), rfl⟩ := Cotangent.mk_surjective x
83	  rw [Submodule.mem_bot, Cotangent.mk_eq_zero_iff]
84	  rw [LinearMap.mem_ker, P.cotangentComplexAux_zero_iff] at hx
85	  have : x.val ∈ Ideal.span (Set.range P.relation) := by
86	    rw [P.span_range_relation_eq_ker]
87	    exact x.property
88	  obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := Finsupp.mem_ideal_span_range_iff_exists_finsupp.mp this
89	  have heq (i : σ) :
90	      aeval P.val (pderiv (P.map i) <| c.sum fun i a ↦ a * P.relation i) = 0 := by
91	    rw [hc]
92	    apply hx
93	  simp only [Finsupp.sum, map_sum, Derivation.leibniz, smul_eq_mul, map_add, map_mul,
94	    Presentation.aeval_val_relation, zero_mul, add_zero] at heq
95	  have heq2 : ∑ i ∈ c.support,
96	      aeval P.val (c i) • (fun j ↦ aeval P.val (pderiv (P.map j) (P.relation i))) = 0 := by
97	    ext j
98	    simp only [Finset.sum_apply, Pi.smul_apply, smul_eq_mul, Pi.zero_apply]
99	    apply heq
100	  have (i : σ) : aeval P.val (c i) = 0 := by

248	  simp only [rank_eq_card_basis P.basisKaehler, Fintype.card_compl_set,
249	    Presentation.dimension, Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, Set.card_range_of_injective P.map_inj]

257	instance : Module.Free S (Generators.localizationAway S r).toExtension.Cotangent :=
258	  inferInstanceAs <|
259	    Module.Free S ((SubmersivePresentation.localizationAway S r).toExtension.Cotangent)

262	/-- The image of `g * X - 1` in `I/I²` if `I` is the kernel of the canonical presentation
263	of the localization of `S` away from `g`. -/
264	noncomputable
265	abbrev Generators.cMulXSubOneCotangent : (Generators.localizationAway S r).toExtension.Cotangent :=
266	  Extension.Cotangent.mk ⟨C r * X () - 1, C_mul_X_sub_one_mem_ker _⟩
267	
268	lemma Generators.cMulXSubOneCotangent_eq :
269	    cMulXSubOneCotangent S r = Extension.Cotangent.mk ⟨C r * X () - 1, C_mul_X_sub_one_mem_ker _⟩ :=
270	  rfl

272	lemma SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent_localizationAway_apply (x : Unit) :
273	    (SubmersivePresentation.localizationAway S r).basisCotangent x =
274	      Generators.cMulXSubOneCotangent S r :=
275	  basisCotangent_apply _ _
276	
277	variable (S) in
278	/--
279	The basis of `(g * X - 1) / (g * X - 1)²` given by the image of `g * X - 1`.
280	
281	This is def-eq to `(SubmersivePresentation.localizationAway T g).basisCotangent`, but
282	```
283	(SubmersivePresentation.localizationAway T g).toExtension =
284	  (Generators.localizationAway T g).toExtension
285	```
286	is not reducibly def-eq. Hence using the general `SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent` leads
287	to `erw` hell.
288	-/
289	noncomputable
290	def Generators.basisCotangentAway (r : R) [IsLocalization.Away r S] :
291	    Module.Basis Unit S (localizationAway S r).toExtension.Cotangent :=
292	  (SubmersivePresentation.localizationAway S r).basisCotangent
293	
294	lemma Generators.basisCotangentAway_apply (x : Unit) :
295	    basisCotangentAway S r x = cMulXSubOneCotangent S r :=
296	  SubmersivePresentation.basisCotangent_apply _ _
297	
298	end LocalizationAway

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Cotangent.Basis

10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.Locus

12	/-!
13	# Standard smooth of free Kaehler differentials
14	
15	In this file we show a presentation independent characterization of being
16	standard smooth: An `R`-algebra `S` of finite presentation is standard smooth if and only if
17	`H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`.
18	
19	From this we deduce relations of standard smooth with other local properties.
20	
21	## Main results
22	
23	- `IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`: An `R`-algebra `S` of finite
24	  presentation is standard smooth if and only if `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on
25	  `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`.
26	- `Etale.iff_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_zero`: An `R`-algebra `S` is
27	  étale if and only if it is standard smooth of relative dimension zero.
28	- `IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth`: If `S` is `R`-smooth at a prime `p`,
29	  it is standard smooth on a standard open containing `p`.
30	
31	## Notes
32	
33	For an example of an algebra with `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` finite and free, but
34	`S` not standard smooth over `R`, consider `R = ℝ` and `S = R[x,y]/(x² + y² - 1)` the
35	coordinate ring of the circle. One can show that then `Ω[S⁄R]` is `S`-free on `ω = xdy - ydx`,
36	but there are no `f g : S` such that `ω = g df`.
37	-/
38	
39	public section
40	
41	namespace Algebra
42	
43	open KaehlerDifferential
44	
45	variable {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
46	
47	/-- If `H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`, then `S`
48	is `R`-standard smooth. -/
49	theorem IsStandardSmooth.of_basis_kaehlerDifferential [FinitePresentation R S]
50	    [Subsingleton (H1Cotangent R S)]
51	    {I : Type*} (b : Module.Basis I S (Ω[S⁄R])) (hb : Set.range b ⊆ Set.range (D R S)) :
52	    IsStandardSmooth R S := by
53	  nontriviality S
54	  obtain ⟨n, ⟨P⟩⟩ := (FiniteType.iff_exists_generators (R := R) (S := S)).mp inferInstance
55	  choose f' hf' using hb
56	  let P := P.extend fun i ↦ f' ⟨i, rfl⟩
57	  have hb (i : I) : b i = D R S (P.val (Sum.inr i)) := by simp [P, hf']
58	  have : Function.Bijective (P.cotangentRestrict _) :=
59	    P.cotangentRestrict_bijective_of_basis_kaehlerDifferential Sum.inl_injective
60	      Set.isCompl_range_inl_range_inr.symm b hb
61	  let bcot' : Module.Basis (Fin n) S P.toExtension.Cotangent :=
62	    .ofRepr (.ofBijective (P.cotangentRestrict _) this)
63	  have : Finite I := Module.Finite.finite_basis b
64	  obtain ⟨Q, bcot, hcomp, hbcot⟩ := P.exists_presentation_of_basis_cotangent bcot'
65	  let P' : PreSubmersivePresentation R S (Unit ⊕ Fin n ⊕ I) (Unit ⊕ Fin n) :=
66	    { __ := Q
67	      map := Sum.map _root_.id Sum.inl
68	      map_inj := Sum.map_injective.mpr ⟨fun _ _ h ↦ h, Sum.inl_injective⟩ }
69	  have hcompl : IsCompl (Set.range (Sum.inr ∘ Sum.inr)) (Set.range P'.map) := by
70	    simp [P', ← eq_compl_iff_isCompl, Set.ext_iff, Set.mem_compl_iff]
71	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (P'.cotangentRestrict P'.map_inj) := by
72	    apply P'.cotangentRestrict_bijective_of_basis_kaehlerDifferential P'.map_inj hcompl b
73	    intro k
74	    simp only [hb, ← hcomp, P', Function.comp_def]
75	  let P'' : SubmersivePresentation R S _ _ :=
76	    ⟨P', P'.isUnit_jacobian_of_cotangentRestrict_bijective bcot hbcot hbij⟩
77	  exact P''.isStandardSmooth
78	
79	/-- An `R`-algebra `S` of finite presentation is standard smooth if and only if
80	`H¹(S/R) = 0` and `Ω[S⁄R]` is free on `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` for some `sᵢ : S`. -/
81	theorem IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential [FinitePresentation R S] :
82	    IsStandardSmooth R S ↔ Subsingleton (H1Cotangent R S) ∧
83	      ∃ (I : Type) (b : Module.Basis I S (Ω[S⁄R])), Set.range b ⊆ Set.range (D R S) := by
84	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ⟨inferInstance, ?_⟩, fun ⟨h, ⟨_, b, hb⟩⟩ ↦ .of_basis_kaehlerDifferential b hb⟩
85	  obtain ⟨ι, σ, _, _, ⟨P⟩⟩ := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.out (R := R) (S := S)
86	  exact ⟨_, P.basisKaehler, by simp [Set.range_subset_iff]⟩
87	
88	/-- `S` is an étale `R`-algebra if and only if it is standard smooth of relative dimension `0`. -/
89	theorem Etale.iff_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_zero :
90	    Etale R S ↔ IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R S := by
91	  refine ⟨fun h ↦ ?_, fun _ ↦ inferInstance⟩
92	  nontriviality S
93	  suffices h : IsStandardSmooth R S by
94	    simp [IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.iff_of_isStandardSmooth]
95	  rw [IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential]
96	  refine ⟨inferInstance, ⟨Empty, Module.Basis.empty Ω[S⁄R], ?_⟩⟩
97	  simp [Set.range_subset_iff]
98	
99	variable (R) in
100	/-- If `S` is `R`-smooth at a prime `p`, then `S` is `R`-standard-smooth in a neighbourhood of `p`:
101	there exists a basic open `p ∈ D(f)` of `Spec S` such that `S[1/f]` is standard smooth. -/
102	theorem IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth [FinitePresentation R S] (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime]
103	    [IsSmoothAt R p] :
104	    ∃ (f : S), f ∉ p ∧ IsStandardSmooth R (Localization.Away f) := by
105	  -- By replacing `S` by some `S[1/g]` we may assume `S` is globally smooth.
106	  wlog h : Smooth R S
107	  · obtain ⟨g, hg, hsm⟩ := IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_smooth R p
108	    have _ : (Ideal.map (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g)) p).IsPrime := by
109	      apply IsLocalization.isPrime_of_isPrime_disjoint (.powers g) _ _ ‹_›
110	      rwa [Ideal.disjoint_powers_iff_notMem _ (Ideal.IsPrime.isRadical ‹_›)]
111	    obtain ⟨g', hg', hstd⟩ := this (R := R) (p.map (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g))) hsm
112	    have : IsLocalization.Away (g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1) (Localization.Away g') :=
113	      .mul_of_associated _ _ g' <| IsLocalization.Away.associated_sec_fst g g'
114	    let e : Localization.Away (g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1) ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away g' :=
115	      Localization.algEquiv _ _
116	    refine ⟨g * (IsLocalization.Away.sec g g').1, ?_, .of_algEquiv (e.restrictScalars R).symm⟩
117	    refine Ideal.IsPrime.mul_notMem ‹_› hg fun hmem ↦ hg' ?_
118	    rw [Ideal.mem_iff_of_associated (IsLocalization.Away.associated_sec_fst g g').symm]
119	    exact Ideal.mem_map_of_mem (algebraMap S (Localization.Away g)) hmem
120	  -- `Ω[Sₚ⁄R]` is projective, so free over the local ring `Sₚ` and
121	  -- a basis extends to a neighbourhood `D(g)`.
122	  obtain ⟨κ, a, b, hb⟩ := Module.exists_basis_of_span_of_flat _
123	    (span_range_map_derivation_of_isLocalization R _ (Localization.AtPrime p) p.primeCompl)
124	  let e : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] Ω[S⁄R] :=
125	    Finsupp.linearCombination S fun i : κ ↦ D R S (a i)
126	  let l₁ : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] (κ →₀ Localization.AtPrime p) :=
127	    Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap S (Localization.AtPrime p))
128	  let l₂ : Ω[S⁄R] →ₗ[S] Ω[Localization.AtPrime p⁄R] := map R R S (Localization.AtPrime p)
129	  let eₚ : (κ →₀ Localization.AtPrime p) →ₗ[Localization.AtPrime p] Ω[Localization.AtPrime p⁄R] :=
130	    IsLocalizedModule.mapExtendScalars p.primeCompl l₁ l₂ (Localization.AtPrime p) e
131	  have : eₚ = b.repr.symm := by
132	    ext i
133	    trans IsLocalizedModule.map p.primeCompl l₁ l₂ e <| l₁ <| Finsupp.single i 1
134	    · simp [eₚ, -IsLocalizedModule.map_apply, l₁]
135	    · simp [l₂, e, hb]
136	  have heₚ : Function.Bijective eₚ := this ▸ b.repr.symm.bijective
137	  have : Finite κ := Module.Finite.finite_basis b
138	  obtain ⟨g, hg, h⟩ := Module.FinitePresentation.exists_notMem_bijective e p l₁ l₂ heₚ
139	  let l₁ₜ : (κ →₀ S) →ₗ[S] (κ →₀ Localization.Away g) :=
140	    Finsupp.mapRange.linearMap (Algebra.linearMap S _)
141	  let l₂ₜ : Ω[S⁄R] →ₗ[S] Ω[Localization.Away g⁄R] :=
142	    map R R S (Localization.Away g)
143	  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_bijective_iff_localizedModuleMap_bijective l₁ₜ l₂ₜ] at h
144	  let eₜ' : (κ →₀ Localization.Away g) →ₗ[Localization.Away g] Ω[Localization.Away g⁄R] :=
145	    IsLocalizedModule.mapExtendScalars (Submonoid.powers g) l₁ₜ l₂ₜ (Localization.Away g) e
146	  refine ⟨g, hg, .of_basis_kaehlerDifferential (.ofRepr (LinearEquiv.ofBijective eₜ' h).symm) ?_⟩
147	  rintro - ⟨i, rfl⟩
148	  exact ⟨algebraMap S _ (a i), by simp +zetaDelta [IsLocalizedModule.map_linearCombination]⟩
149	
150	variable (R S) in
151	/-- If `S` is `R`-smooth, there exists a cover by basic opens `D(sᵢ)` such that
152	`S[1/sᵢ]` is `R`-standard-smooth. -/
153	theorem Smooth.exists_span_eq_top_isStandardSmooth [Smooth R S] :
154	    ∃ (s : Set S), Ideal.span s = ⊤ ∧ ∀ x ∈ s, IsStandardSmooth R (Localization.Away x) := by
155	  choose f hf₁ hf₂ using IsSmoothAt.exists_notMem_isStandardSmooth R (S := S)
156	  /- #adaptation_note Before https://github.com/leanprover/lean4/pull/13166
157	  (replacing grind's canonicalizer with a type-directed normalizer), the original proof was:
158	  `refine ⟨Set.range (fun p : PrimeSpectrum S ↦ f p.asIdeal), ?_, by grind⟩`

162	    simp only [Set.mem_range, forall_exists_index, forall_apply_eq_imp_iff]; intro; apply hf₂⟩

14	/-!
15	# Regular local rings
16	
17	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, we define `IsRegularLocalRing` as
18	`(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`. This definition is equivalent to
19	the dimension of the cotangent space over the residue field being equal to `ringKrullDim R`,
20	(see `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
21	
22	# Main Definition and Results
23	
24	* `IsRegularLocalRing` : A Noetherian local ring is regular if
25	  `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`,
26	  i.e. its maximal ideal can be generated by `dim R` elements.
27	
28	* `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` : the equivalence of `IsRegularLocalRing` and
29	  `Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R`
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	open IsLocalRing
36	
37	/-- A Noetherian local ring is said to be regular if its maximal ideal
38	can be generated by `dim R` elements. -/
39	@[stacks 00KU]
40	class IsRegularLocalRing (R : Type*) [CommRing R] : Prop extends
41	    IsLocalRing R, IsNoetherianRing R where
42	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R
43	
44	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
45	
46	lemma isRegularLocalRing_iff [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
47	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R :=
48	  ⟨fun ⟨h⟩ ↦ h, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩
49	
50	namespace IsRegularLocalRing
51	
52	lemma of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
53	    (le : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank ≤ ringKrullDim R) : IsRegularLocalRing R :=
54	  (isRegularLocalRing_iff _).mpr (le_antisymm le (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal R))
55	
56	variable {R} in
57	lemma of_ringEquiv [IsRegularLocalRing R] {R' : Type*} [CommRing R']
58	    (e : R ≃+* R') : IsRegularLocalRing R' := by
59	  have := e.isLocalRing
60	  have := isNoetherianRing_of_ringEquiv R e
61	  rwa [isRegularLocalRing_iff, ← ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv e, ← map_ringEquiv_maximalIdeal e,
62	    Ideal.spanFinrank_map_eq_of_ringEquiv, ← isRegularLocalRing_iff]
63	
64	lemma iff_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
65	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R := by
66	  rw [isRegularLocalRing_iff, spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
67	
68	instance [IsLocalRing R] [IsDomain R] [IsPrincipalIdealRing R] : IsRegularLocalRing R := by
69	  by_cases isf : IsField R
70	  · let _ := isf.toField
71	    simp [isRegularLocalRing_iff, maximalIdeal_eq_bot]
72	  · apply of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
73	    rcases IsPrincipalIdealRing.principal (maximalIdeal R) with ⟨x, hx⟩
74	    simpa only [(IsPrincipalIdealRing.ringKrullDim_eq_one R) isf,
75	      Nat.cast_le_one, ← Set.ncard_singleton x, hx] using
76	        Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_singleton x)
77	
78	end IsRegularLocalRing
79

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Kaehler
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Spectrum.Prime.FreeLocus
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Support
11	
12	/-!
13	# Smooth locus of an algebra
14	
15	Most results in this file are proved for algebras of finite presentations.
16	Some of them are true for arbitrary algebras but the proof is substantially harder.
17	
18	## Main results
19	- `Algebra.smoothLocus` : The set of primes that are smooth over the base.
20	- `Algebra.basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff` :
21	  `D(f)` is contained in the smooth locus if and only if `A_f` is smooth over `R`.
22	- `Algebra.smoothLocus_eq_univ_iff` :
23	  The smooth locus is the whole spectrum if and only if `A` is smooth over `R`.
24	- `Algebra.isOpen_smoothLocus` : The smooth locus is open.
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	variable (R A : Type*) [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [Algebra R A]
32	
33	namespace Algebra
34	
35	variable {A} in
36	/--
37	An `R`-algebra `A` is smooth at a prime `p` of `A` if `Aₚ` is formally smooth over `R`.
38	
39	This does not imply `Aₚ` is smooth over `R` under the mathlib definition
40	even if `A` is finitely presented,
41	but it can be shown that this is equivalent to the stacks project definition that `A` is smooth
42	at `p` if and only if there exists `f ∉ p` such that `A_f` is smooth over `R`.
43	See `Algebra.basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff_smooth` and `Algebra.isOpen_smoothLocus`.
44	-/
45	@[stacks 00TB]
46	abbrev IsSmoothAt (p : Ideal A) [p.IsPrime] : Prop :=
47	  Algebra.FormallySmooth R (Localization.AtPrime p)
48	
49	/-- `Algebra.smoothLocus R A` is the set of primes `p` of `A`
50	such that `Aₚ` is formally smooth over `R`. -/
51	def smoothLocus : Set (PrimeSpectrum A) := { p | IsSmoothAt R p.asIdeal }
52	
53	variable {R A}
54	
55	attribute [local instance] Module.finitePresentation_of_projective in
56	lemma smoothLocus_eq_compl_support_inter [EssFiniteType R A] :
57	    smoothLocus R A = (Module.support A (H1Cotangent R A))ᶜ ∩ Module.freeLocus A Ω[A⁄R] := by
58	  ext p
59	  simp only [Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_compl_iff, Module.notMem_support_iff,
60	    Module.mem_freeLocus]
61	  refine (Algebra.formallySmooth_iff _ _).trans (and_comm.trans ?_)
62	  congr! 1
63	  · have := IsLocalizedModule.iso p.asIdeal.primeCompl
64	      (H1Cotangent.map R R A (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal))
65	    exact this.subsingleton_congr.symm
66	  · trans Module.Free (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) Ω[Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal⁄R]
67	    · have : EssFiniteType A (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) :=
68	        .of_isLocalization _ p.asIdeal.primeCompl
69	      have : EssFiniteType R (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal) := .comp _ A _
70	      exact ⟨fun _ ↦ Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing, fun _ ↦ inferInstance⟩
71	    · have := IsLocalizedModule.iso p.asIdeal.primeCompl
72	        (KaehlerDifferential.map R R A (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal))
73	      have := this.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization
74	        p.asIdeal.primeCompl (Localization.AtPrime p.asIdeal)
75	      exact ⟨fun H ↦ H.of_equiv' this.symm, fun H ↦ H.of_equiv' this⟩
76	
77	lemma basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff [FinitePresentation R A] {f : A} :
78	    ↑(PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) ⊆ smoothLocus R A ↔
79	      Algebra.FormallySmooth R (Localization.Away f) := by
80	  rw [smoothLocus_eq_compl_support_inter, Set.subset_inter_iff, Set.subset_compl_comm,
81	    PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_eq_zeroLocus_compl, compl_compl,
82	    ← LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff_support_subset,
83	    Algebra.formallySmooth_iff, iff_comm, and_comm]
84	  congr! 1
85	  · have := IsLocalizedModule.iso (.powers f) (H1Cotangent.map R R A (Localization.Away f))
86	    rw [this.subsingleton_congr]
87	  · rw [← PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_eq_zeroLocus_compl, Module.basicOpen_subset_freeLocus_iff]
88	    have := IsLocalizedModule.iso (.powers f)
89	        (KaehlerDifferential.map R R A (Localization.Away f))
90	    have := this.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
91	    exact ⟨fun _ ↦ .of_equiv this.symm, fun _ ↦ .of_equiv this⟩
92	
93	lemma basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff_smooth [FinitePresentation R A] {f : A} :
94	    ↑(PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f) ⊆ smoothLocus R A ↔
95	      Algebra.Smooth R (Localization.Away f) := by
96	  have : FinitePresentation A (Localization.Away f) := IsLocalization.Away.finitePresentation f
97	  rw [basicOpen_subset_smoothLocus_iff]
98	  exact ⟨fun H ↦ ⟨H, .trans _ A _⟩, fun H ↦ H.1⟩
99	
100	lemma smoothLocus_eq_univ_iff [FinitePresentation R A] :

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2025 Andrew Yang. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Etale.Field
9	public import Mathlib.FieldTheory.SeparablyGenerated
10	
11	/-!
12	
13	# Smooth algebras over fields
14	
15	We show that separably generated extensions of fields are smooth.
16	In particular finitely generated field extensions over perfect fields are smooth.
17	
18	-/
19	
20	@[expose] public section
21	
22	variable {K L ι : Type*} [Field L] [Field K] [Algebra K L]
23	
24	open scoped IntermediateField.algebraAdjoinAdjoin in
25	lemma Algebra.FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent {v : ι → L}
26	    (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v) :
27	    Algebra.FormallySmooth K (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) := by
28	  have : Algebra.FormallySmooth K (adjoin K (Set.range v)) :=
29	    .of_equiv hb.aevalEquiv
30	  have : Algebra.FormallySmooth (adjoin K (Set.range v))
31	      (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) :=
32	    .of_isLocalization (nonZeroDivisors _)
33	  exact .comp _ (adjoin K (Set.range v)) _
34	
35	/-- Purely transcendental extensions are formally smooth. -/
36	lemma Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_algebraicIndependent {v : ι → L}
37	    (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v) (hb' : IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v) = ⊤) :
38	    Algebra.FormallySmooth K L := by
39	  have := Algebra.FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent hb
40	  rw [hb'] at this
41	  exact .of_equiv IntermediateField.topEquiv
42	
43	/-- Separably generated extensions are formally smooth. -/
44	lemma Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_algebraicIndependent_of_isSeparable
45	    {v : ι → L} (hb : AlgebraicIndependent K v)
46	    [Algebra.IsSeparable (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) L] :
47	    Algebra.FormallySmooth K L := by
48	  have := FormallySmooth.adjoin_of_algebraicIndependent hb
49	  have : FormallyEtale (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) L :=
50	    Algebra.FormallyEtale.of_isSeparable _ L
51	  exact .comp _ (IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range v)) _
52	
53	instance (priority := low) Algebra.FormallySmooth.of_perfectField
54	    [PerfectField K] [Algebra.EssFiniteType K L] : Algebra.FormallySmooth K L := by
55	  obtain ⟨s, hs, H⟩ := exists_isTranscendenceBasis_and_isSeparable_of_perfectField K L
56	  have : Algebra.IsSeparable (↥(IntermediateField.adjoin K (Set.range ((↑) : s → L)))) L := by
57	    convert H <;> simp
58	  exact .of_algebraicIndependent_of_isSeparable hs.1

243	lemma formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff {f : X ⟶ Y} {x : X} (U : Y.Opens)
244	      (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (V : X.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (hVU : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U)
245	      (hx : x ∈ V) :

247	    (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth ↔
248	      hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩ ∈ Algebra.smoothLocus Γ(Y, U) Γ(X, V) := by
249	  letI := (f.appLE U V hVU).hom.toAlgebra
250	  have : (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal.LiesOver (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩).asIdeal :=
251	    ⟨congr($(IsAffineOpen.comap_primeIdealOf_appLE U hU V hV hVU hx).1).symm⟩
252	  trans Algebra.FormallySmooth
253	    (Localization.AtPrime (hU.primeIdealOf ⟨f x, hVU hx⟩).asIdeal)
254	    (Localization.AtPrime (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨x, hx⟩).asIdeal)
255	  · rw [← formallySmooth_algebraMap]
256	    exact RingHom.FormallySmooth.respectsIso.arrow_mk_iso_iff
257	      (IsAffineOpen.arrowStalkMapIso f U hU V hV hVU hx)
258	  · exact Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_restrictScalars.symm

265	      x ∈ V ∧ (f.appLE U V hVU).hom.Smooth := by
266	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨U, hU, rfl⟩, hxU, -⟩ :=
267	    Y.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ (f x)) isOpen_univ
268	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨V, hV, rfl⟩, hxV, hVU⟩ :=

276	  · rwa [← PrimeSpectrum.mem_basicOpen, IsAffineOpen.primeIdealOf,
277	      ← hV.fromSpec_preimage_basicOpen, Scheme.Hom.mem_preimage, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply,
278	      IsAffineOpen.isoSpec_hom, IsAffineOpen.toSpecΓ_fromSpec] at hrx
279	  · have := hV.isLocalization_basicOpen r

282	      (IsLocalization.algEquiv (.powers r) _ Γ(X, X.basicOpen r)).toRingEquiv hr
283	    ext
284	    dsimp

286	      IsLocalization.map_eq]

290	lemma Scheme.Hom.isOpen_smoothLocus [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] :
291	    IsOpen { x | (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth } := by
292	  refine isOpen_iff_forall_mem_open.mpr fun x hx ↦ ?_
293	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hVU, hxV, H⟩ := exists_smooth_of_formallySmooth_stalk f x hx

299	def Scheme.Hom.smoothLocus (f : X ⟶ Y) [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] : X.Opens :=

303	    x ∈ f.smoothLocus ↔ (f.stalkMap x).hom.FormallySmooth := .rfl

306	    f.smoothLocus = ⊤ := by
307	  rw [← top_le_iff]
308	  rintro x -
309	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨U, hU, rfl⟩, hxU, -⟩ :=
310	    Y.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open (Set.mem_univ (f x)) isOpen_univ
311	  obtain ⟨_, ⟨V, hV, rfl⟩, hxV, hVU⟩ :=
312	    X.isBasis_affineOpens.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxU (U.2.preimage f.continuous)
313	  have := f.smooth_appLE hU hV hVU
314	  algebraize [(f.appLE U V hVU).hom]
315	  rw [Scheme.Hom.mem_smoothLocus, formallySmooth_stalkMap_iff U hU V hV hVU hxV]
316	  exact inferInstanceAs (Algebra.IsSmoothAt _ _)

318	lemma Scheme.Hom.smoothLocus_eq_top_iff {f : X ⟶ Y} [LocallyOfFinitePresentation f] :
319	    f.smoothLocus = ⊤ ↔ Smooth f := by
320	  refine ⟨fun H ↦ ?_, fun _ ↦ f.smoothLocus_eq_top⟩
321	  refine IsZariskiLocalAtSource.iff_exists_resLE.mpr fun x ↦ ?_
322	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hVU, hxV, H⟩ :=
323	    exists_smooth_of_formallySmooth_stalk f _ (H.ge (Set.mem_univ x))
324	  refine ⟨U, V, hxV, hVU, ?_⟩
325	  have : IsAffine _ := hU
326	  have : IsAffine _ := hV
327	  rw [HasRingHomProperty.iff_of_isAffine (P := @Smooth)]
328	  exact (RingHom.Smooth.propertyIsLocal.respectsIso.arrow_mk_iso_iff
329	    (arrowResLEAppIso f U V hVU)).mpr H

278	  refine ⟨J, hJ₁, le_antisymm ?_ (le_trans ?_ (J.height_le_spanRank ?_)),
279	    le_antisymm (Ideal.height_mono hJ₁) hJ₃⟩
280	  · convert hJ₂
281	    exact Cardinal.ofENat_eq_nat.mpr (ENat.coe_toNat (I.height_ne_top hI)).symm
282	  · exact Cardinal.ofENat_le_ofENat_of_le hJ₃
283	  · rintro rfl
284	    exact hI (top_le_iff.mp hJ₁)
285	
286	/-- In a commutative Noetherian ring `R`, a prime ideal `p` has height no greater than `n` if and
287	only if it is a minimal ideal over some ideal generated by no more than `n` elements. -/
288	lemma Ideal.height_le_iff_exists_minimalPrimes (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime]
289	    (n : ℕ∞) : p.height ≤ n ↔ ∃ I : Ideal R, p ∈ I.minimalPrimes ∧ I.spanRank ≤ n := by
290	  constructor
291	  · intro h
292	    obtain ⟨I, hI, e₁, e₂⟩ := exists_spanRank_eq_and_height_eq p (IsPrime.ne_top ‹_›)
293	    refine ⟨I, Ideal.mem_minimalPrimes_of_height_eq hI e₂.ge, e₁.symm ▸ ?_⟩
294	    norm_cast
295	  · rintro ⟨I, hp, hI⟩
296	    exact le_trans
297	      (Ideal.height_le_spanRank_toENat_of_mem_minimalPrimes I p hp)
298	      (by simpa using (Cardinal.toENat.monotone' hI))
299	
300	/-- If `p` is a prime in a Noetherian ring `R`, there exists a `p`-primary ideal `I`
301	spanned by `p.height` elements. -/
302	lemma Ideal.exists_finset_card_eq_height_of_isNoetherianRing (p : Ideal R) [p.IsPrime] :
303	    ∃ s : Finset R, p ∈ (span s).minimalPrimes ∧ s.card = p.height := by
304	  obtain ⟨I, hI, hr⟩ := (p.height_le_iff_exists_minimalPrimes <| p.height).mp le_rfl
305	  have hs : I.generators.Finite := (IsNoetherian.noetherian I).finite_generators
306	  refine ⟨hs.toFinset, by rwa [hs.coe_toFinset, span, I.span_generators], ?_⟩
307	  rw [← Set.ncard_eq_toFinset_card (hs := hs), (IsNoetherian.noetherian I).generators_ncard]
308	  refine le_antisymm ?_ ?_
309	  · rw [Submodule.fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank.mpr (IsNoetherian.noetherian I)] at hr
310	    exact Cardinal.nat_le_ofENat.mp hr
311	  · convert_to p.height ≤ I.spanRank.toENat
312	    · symm
313	      simpa [Submodule.fg_iff_spanRank_eq_spanFinrank] using (IsNoetherian.noetherian I)
314	    · exact I.height_le_spanRank_toENat_of_mem_minimalPrimes _ hI
315	
316	/-- If `I ≤ p` and `p` is prime, the height of `p` is bounded by the height of `p ⧸ I R` plus
317	the span rank of `I`. -/
318	lemma Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le {I p : Ideal R} [p.IsPrime] (hrp : I ≤ p) :
319	    p.height ≤ (p.map (Quotient.mk I)).height + I.spanFinrank := by
320	  classical
321	  let p' := p.map (algebraMap R (R ⧸ I))
322	  have : p'.IsPrime := isPrime_map_quotientMk_of_isPrime hrp
323	  obtain ⟨s, hps, hs⟩ := exists_finset_card_eq_height_of_isNoetherianRing p'
324	  have hsp' : (s : Set (R ⧸ I)) ⊆ (p' : Set _) := fun _ hx ↦ hps.1.2 (subset_span hx)
325	  have : Set.SurjOn (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) p s := by
326	    refine Set.SurjOn.mono subset_rfl hsp' fun x hx ↦ ?_
327	    obtain ⟨x, rfl⟩ := Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective x
328	    exact ⟨x, by simpa [hrp, sup_of_le_left, p'] using hx⟩
329	  obtain ⟨o, hsubset, ho, himgo⟩ := s.exists_subset_injOn_image_eq_of_surjOn (p : Set R) this
330	  have hI : I.FG := IsNoetherian.noetherian I
331	  let t : Finset R := o ∪ (Submodule.FG.finite_generators hI).toFinset
332	  suffices h : p.height ≤ t.card by
333	    refine le_trans h (hs ▸ ?_)
334	    norm_cast
335	    have : (Submodule.FG.finite_generators hI).toFinset.card = I.spanFinrank := by
336	      rw [← Set.ncard_eq_toFinset_card (hs := Submodule.FG.finite_generators hI)]
337	      exact Submodule.FG.generators_ncard hI
338	    grind
339	  refine Ideal.height_le_card_of_mem_minimalPrimes_span_finset ?_
340	  rw [Finset.coe_union, Set.Finite.coe_toFinset, span_union, sup_comm, span,
341	    Submodule.span_generators]
342	  refine Ideal.mem_minimalPrimes_sup hrp ?_
343	  convert hps
344	  simp [Ideal.map_span, ← himgo]
345	
346	lemma height_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_spanFinrank {p I : Ideal R} [p.IsPrime] (h : I ≤ p) :
347	    p.height ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ I) + I.spanFinrank := by
348	  trans (p.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk I)).height + I.spanFinrank
349	  · norm_cast; exact Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le h
350	  · gcongr
351	    have : (Ideal.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk I) p).IsPrime :=
352	      Ideal.isPrime_map_quotientMk_of_isPrime h
353	    exact Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top Ideal.IsPrime.ne_top'
354	
355	lemma ringKrullDim_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_spanFinrank (I : Ideal R)
356	    (h : I ≤ Ring.jacobson R) :
357	    ringKrullDim R ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ I) + I.spanFinrank := by
358	  nontriviality R
359	  rw [ringKrullDim_le_iff_isMaximal_height_le]
360	  intro m hm
361	  exact height_le_ringKrullDim_quotient_add_spanFinrank <|
362	    le_trans h <| Ring.jacobson_le_of_isMaximal m
363	
364	/-- If `p` is a prime ideal containing `s`, the height of `p` is bounded
365	by the sum of the height of the image of `p` in `R ⧸ (s)` and the cardinality of `s`. -/
366	lemma Ideal.height_le_height_add_encard_of_subset (s : Set R) {p : Ideal R} [p.IsPrime]
367	    (hrm : s ⊆ p) : p.height ≤ (p.map (Quotient.mk (span s))).height + s.encard := by
368	  apply le_trans (Ideal.height_le_height_add_spanFinrank_of_le (I := span s) (p := p) ?_) ?_
369	  · rwa [span_le]

5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Extension.Presentation.Submersive
9	
10	/-!
11	# Standard smooth algebras
12	
13	A standard smooth algebra is an algebra that admits a `SubmersivePresentation`. A standard
14	smooth algebra is of relative dimension `n` if it admits a submersive presentation of
15	dimension `n`.
16	
17	While every standard smooth algebra is smooth, the converse does not hold. But if `S` is `R`-smooth,
18	then `S` is `R`-standard smooth locally on `S`, i.e. there exists a set `{ t }` of `S` that
19	generates the unit ideal, such that `Sₜ` is `R`-standard smooth for every `t` (TODO, see below).
20	
21	## Main definitions
22	
23	All of these are in the `Algebra` namespace. Let `S` be an `R`-algebra.
24	
25	- `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth`: `S` is `R`-standard smooth if `S` admits a submersive
26	  `R`-presentation.
27	- `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.relativeDimension`: If `S` is `R`-standard smooth this is the dimension
28	  of an arbitrary submersive `R`-presentation of `S`. This is independent of the choice
29	  of the presentation (TODO, see below).
30	- `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n`: `S` is `R`-standard smooth of relative dimension
31	  `n` if it admits a submersive `R`-presentation of dimension `n`.
32	
33	## TODO
34	
35	- Show that locally on the target, smooth algebras are standard smooth.
36	
37	## Notes
38	
39	This contribution was created as part of the AIM workshop "Formalizing algebraic geometry"
40	in June 2024.
41	
42	-/
43	
44	@[expose] public section
45	
46	universe t t' w w' u v
47	
48	open TensorProduct Module MvPolynomial
49	
50	variable (n m : ℕ)
51	
52	namespace Algebra
53	
54	variable (R : Type u) (S : Type v) (ι : Type w) (σ : Type t) [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
55	
56	attribute [local instance] Fintype.ofFinite
57	
58	/--
59	An `R`-algebra `S` is called standard smooth, if there
60	exists a submersive presentation.
61	-/
62	class IsStandardSmooth : Prop where
63	  out : ∃ (ι σ : Type) (_ : Finite σ), Finite ι ∧ Nonempty (SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ)
64	
65	variable [Finite σ]
66	
67	variable {R S ι σ} in
68	lemma SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmooth [Finite ι] (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ) :
69	    IsStandardSmooth R S := by
70	  exact ⟨_, _, _, inferInstance, ⟨P.reindex (Fintype.equivFin _).symm (Fintype.equivFin _).symm⟩⟩
71	
72	/--
73	The relative dimension of a standard smooth `R`-algebra `S` is
74	the dimension of an arbitrarily chosen submersive `R`-presentation of `S`.
75	
76	Note: If `S` is non-trivial, this number is independent of the choice of the presentation as it is
77	equal to the `S`-rank of `Ω[S/R]`
78	(see `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`).
79	-/
80	noncomputable def IsStandardSmooth.relativeDimension [IsStandardSmooth R S] : ℕ :=
81	  letI := ‹IsStandardSmooth R S›.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose
82	  ‹IsStandardSmooth R S›.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.2.some.dimension
83	
84	/--
85	An `R`-algebra `S` is called standard smooth of relative dimension `n`, if there exists
86	a submersive presentation of dimension `n`.
87	-/
88	class IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension : Prop where
89	  out : ∃ (ι σ : Type) (_ : Finite σ) (_ : Finite ι) (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ),
90	    P.dimension = n
91	
92	variable {R S ι σ n} in
93	lemma SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension [Finite ι]
94	    (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ) (hP : P.dimension = n) :
95	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S := by
96	  refine ⟨⟨_, _, _, inferInstance,
97	    P.reindex (Fintype.equivFin _).symm (Fintype.equivFin σ).symm, ?_⟩⟩
98	  simp [hP]
99	
100	variable {R} {S}
101	
102	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth
103	    [H : IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] : IsStandardSmooth R S :=
104	  ⟨_, _, _, H.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
105	    H.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.nonempty⟩
106	
107	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algebraMap_bijective
108	    (h : Function.Bijective (algebraMap R S)) :
109	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R S :=
110	  ⟨_, _, _, inferInstance,
111	    SubmersivePresentation.ofBijectiveAlgebraMap h, Presentation.ofBijectiveAlgebraMap_dimension h⟩
112	
113	variable (R) in
114	instance IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.id :
115	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R R :=
116	  IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algebraMap_bijective Function.bijective_id
117	
118	instance (priority := 100) IsStandardSmooth.finitePresentation [IsStandardSmooth R S] :
119	    FinitePresentation R S := by
120	  obtain ⟨_, _, _, _, ⟨P⟩⟩ := ‹IsStandardSmooth R S›
121	  exact P.finitePresentation_of_isFinite
122	
123	lemma IsStandardSmooth.of_algEquiv {T : Type*} [CommRing T] [Algebra R T] (e : S ≃ₐ[R] T)
124	    [IsStandardSmooth R S] : IsStandardSmooth R T := by
125	  obtain ⟨_, _, _, _, ⟨P⟩⟩ := ‹IsStandardSmooth R S›
126	  exact (P.ofAlgEquiv e).isStandardSmooth
127	
128	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algEquiv {T : Type*} [CommRing T] [Algebra R T]
129	    (e : S ≃ₐ[R] T) [IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] :
130	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R T := by
131	  obtain ⟨_, _, _, _, ⟨P, hP⟩⟩ := ‹IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S›
132	  exact (P.ofAlgEquiv e).isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (by simpa)
133	
134	section Composition
135	
136	variable (R S T) [CommRing T] [Algebra R T] [Algebra S T] [IsScalarTower R S T]
137	
138	lemma IsStandardSmooth.trans [IsStandardSmooth R S] [IsStandardSmooth S T] :
139	    IsStandardSmooth R T where
140	  out := by
141	    obtain ⟨_, _, _, _, ⟨P⟩⟩ := ‹IsStandardSmooth R S›
142	    obtain ⟨_, _, _, _, ⟨Q⟩⟩ := ‹IsStandardSmooth S T›
143	    exact ⟨_, _, _, inferInstance, ⟨Q.comp P⟩⟩
144	
145	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.trans [IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
146	    [IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension m S T] :
147	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (m + n) R T where
148	  out := by
149	    obtain ⟨_, _, _, _, P, hP⟩ := ‹IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S›
150	    obtain ⟨_, _, _, _, Q, hQ⟩ := ‹IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension m S T›

import Mathlib

open Algebra

universe u v

private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {S : Type v} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    [Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R])]
    (M : Submonoid S)
    (Sₘ : Type v) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
    Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
  exact Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sₘ) (S := M)
    (M := Ω[S⁄R]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sₘ⁄R])
    (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)

#check @module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization

import Mathlib

open Algebra

universe u v

-- Rank of Kähler differentials at the stalk equals the relative dimension.
private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {S : Type v} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
    (n : ℕ) [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
    (M : Submonoid S) (hM : 0 ∉ M)
    (Sₘ : Type v) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
  -- Sₘ is nontrivial (because 0 ∉ M)
  haveI : Nontrivial Sₘ := IsLocalization.nontrivial S M Sₘ hM
  -- Sₘ is standard smooth of relative dimension n over R via composition
  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 S Sₘ :=
    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_isLocalization M
  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension (0 + n) R Sₘ :=
    Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.trans 0 n R S Sₘ
  rw [zero_add] at this
  haveI := this
  exact Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n

#check @rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension

84	/--
85	An `R`-algebra `S` is called standard smooth of relative dimension `n`, if there exists
86	a submersive presentation of dimension `n`.
87	-/
88	class IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension : Prop where
89	  out : ∃ (ι σ : Type) (_ : Finite σ) (_ : Finite ι) (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ),
90	    P.dimension = n
91	
92	variable {R S ι σ n} in
93	lemma SubmersivePresentation.isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension [Finite ι]
94	    (P : SubmersivePresentation R S ι σ) (hP : P.dimension = n) :
95	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S := by
96	  refine ⟨⟨_, _, _, inferInstance,
97	    P.reindex (Fintype.equivFin _).symm (Fintype.equivFin σ).symm, ?_⟩⟩
98	  simp [hP]
99	
100	variable {R} {S}
101	
102	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.isStandardSmooth
103	    [H : IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] : IsStandardSmooth R S :=
104	  ⟨_, _, _, H.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose,
105	    H.out.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.choose_spec.nonempty⟩
106	
107	lemma IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algebraMap_bijective
108	    (h : Function.Bijective (algebraMap R S)) :
109	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R S :=
110	  ⟨_, _, _, inferInstance,
111	    SubmersivePresentation.ofBijectiveAlgebraMap h, Presentation.ofBijectiveAlgebraMap_dimension h⟩
112	
113	variable (R) in
114	instance IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.id :
115	    IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 R R :=
116	  IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.of_algebraMap_bijective Function.bijective_id
117

import Mathlib

open Algebra

universe u

private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
    (n : ℕ) [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
    (M : Submonoid S)
    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := inferInstance
  have hrank :
      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) =
      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank S (Ω[S⁄R])) :=
    Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sₘ M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
  rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential n] at hrank
  simpa using hrank

#check @rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension

import Mathlib

open Algebra

universe u

private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
    (n : ℕ) [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
    (M : Submonoid S)
    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S := hS.isStandardSmooth
  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
  have hrank :
      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) =
      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank S (Ω[S⁄R])) :=
    Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sₘ M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
  rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential (S := S) n] at hrank
  rw [Cardinal.lift_id'] at hrank
  rw [Cardinal.lift_id'] at hrank
  exact hrank.symm

#check @rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension

axiom-clean intermediate helper: given an algebra-level
`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance (typically arising from

/-- **Stage 4 (Stacks 00T7(2), Kähler-differentials freeness).** Iter-192
axiom-clean intermediate helper: given an algebra-level
`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance (typically arising from
`Stage 3 = exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`),
the module of Kähler differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` is `Module.Free` over `S`. The
basis is the `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` family witnessed by
`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`.

Axiom-clean: re-export of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
+ `Module.Free.of_basis`. -/
private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
    Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := by
  obtain ⟨_, _, b, _⟩ :=
    (Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential
      (R := R) (S := S)).mp ‹_›
  exact Module.Free.of_basis b

/-- **Stage 5a (Stacks 02JK, freeness transports through localisation, algebra-side).**
Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` whose Kähler
differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` are `S`-free (e.g.\ when `S` is `R`-standard smooth via
`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`), the localisation `Sₘ` at any
submonoid `M ⊆ S` has Kähler differentials `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` which are `Sₘ`-free.

Axiom-clean: composes `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (Mathlib re-export)
with `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`. This is the substantive localisation-of-freeness
step that Stage 5 of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline uses; extracted as a named helper
here so the main proof body of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` reads as a clean
chain of named lemmas rather than an inline `IsLocalizedModule` + `Module.free_of_*`
maneuver. -/
private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    [Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R])]
    (M : Submonoid S)
    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
    Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
  exact Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sₘ) (S := M)
    (M := Ω[S⁄R]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sₘ⁄R])
    (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)

/-- **Stage 5b (Stacks 02JK + 00T7, rank computation through localisation, algebra-side).**
Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` that is
`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n` over `R` and a localisation `Sₘ` at any
submonoid `M` (with `Sₘ` non-trivial), the `Sₘ`-rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` equals `n`. This
upgrades Stage 5a's freeness conclusion with a *specific* rank.

Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
(Mathlib, gives `rank S Ω[S⁄R] = n`) with `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`
(Mathlib, transports module rank through `IsLocalizedModule`). The combination
provides the rank of the stalk-side Kähler differentials, which is one of the two
ingredients of the regularity conclusion at the stalk (the other ingredient is the
Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula, still a Mathlib gap). -/
private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
    (n : ℕ) [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
    (M : Submonoid S)
    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Nontrivial Sₘ]
    [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
    Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) = n := by
  haveI : Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S := hS.isStandardSmooth
  haveI : Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.free_kaehlerDifferential
  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
  have hrank :
      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R])) =
      Cardinal.lift.{u, u} (Module.rank S (Ω[S⁄R])) :=
    Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free Sₘ M
      (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
  rw [Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential
        (S := S) n] at hrank
  simpa using hrank

374	For a smooth integral variety `X` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, the
375	stalk of `X` at every point is a regular local ring.
