**Mathlib status at commit `b80f227` (iter-193 Lane M↓ Stage 5a/5b expansion).**
The 6-stage proof chain is now scaffolded as follows.

* Stage 1 (`stalkMap_flat_of_smooth`): smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk.
  Axiom-clean (iter-191).
* Stage 2 (`exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth`): smooth ⟹ standard
  smooth ring-hom presentation locally. Axiom-clean (iter-191).
* Stage 3 (`exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`):
  scheme-to-algebra bridge: composes Stage 2 with the
  `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` bridge and the affine-open stalk
  localisation, producing an algebra-side `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth Γ(Spec, U)
  Γ(X.left, V)` + `IsLocalization.AtPrime` on the stalk. Axiom-clean
  (iter-192).
* Stage 4 (`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`):
  standard-smooth algebra ⟹ Kähler differentials `Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec, U)]`
  are `Module.Free` over the section algebra. Axiom-clean (iter-192).
* Stage 5a (`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization`): localisation
  transports Kähler-differentials freeness. Axiom-clean (iter-193). The
  Stage 5 chain in the body now contracts to a one-liner application of
  this helper. Was previously inline; extraction makes the proof body read
  as a clean sequence of named substrate lemmas.
* Stage 5b (`rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension`):
  the `Sₘ`-rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` equals the relative dimension `n` of the
  standard-smooth algebra `R → S`. Axiom-clean (iter-193). Composes
  `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
  (Mathlib re-export) with `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`.
  This supplies the *specific rank* that downstream Stage 6 needs as the
  dimension witness for the regularity conclusion.
* Stage 6 (Stacks `02JK`/`00OE`, the substantive Mathlib gap): two specific
  bridges remain unbuilt at commit `b80f227`:
    (a) Cotangent ↔ Kähler bridge over a field base: the conormal
        sequence `m/m² ↪ Ω[A⁄R] ⊗_A k(p) ↠ Ω[k(p)⁄R] → 0` is right exact,
        and the first map is injective when the residue extension
        `k(p)/R` is separable (Stacks 02JK). Over `R = kbar` algebraically
        closed and a closed point `z` the conclusion is immediate
        (`Ω[k(p)⁄kbar] = 0` since `k(p) = kbar`), but for non-closed `z`
        the residue field is a transcendental extension and the
        injectivity requires the full conormal apparatus.
    (b) Smooth-algebra dimension formula: `ringKrullDim Aₚ = relative dim`
        (Stacks 00OE). Standard-smooth presentations give the rank of
        `Ω[Aₚ⁄R]` (= relative dim, via Stage 5b), and the dimension
        formula provides the matching `ringKrullDim` side. Not yet
        packaged in Mathlib.

Once Stages 5-6 close upstream, this helper closes axiom-clean and every

`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`) inherits the closure automatically.

660	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
661	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
662	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
663	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
664	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
665	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
666	
667	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
668	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
669	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
670	
671	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
672	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
673	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
674	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
675	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
676	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
677	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
678	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
679	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
680	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
681	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
682	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
683	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
684	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f :=
685	  sorry
686	
687	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
688	
689	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
690	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
691	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
692	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
693	
694	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
695	
696	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
697	codim 1.**
698	
699	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
700	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
701	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
702	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
703	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
704	
705	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
706	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
707	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
708	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
709	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
710	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
711	
712	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
713	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
714	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
715	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
716	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
717	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
718	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
719	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]

# AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean — iter-193 prover report

## Summary

Lane M↓ Stages 5a + 5b axiom-clean substrate helpers landed. The Stage 5
chain in the body of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` contracts from a
12-line inline localisation-of-freeness maneuver into a single named
helper application. The Stages 5a/5b helpers separately capture the
*freeness-transports-through-localisation* and *rank-equals-relative-dim*
content as standalone substrate lemmas that any downstream consumer can
reuse.

**Headline sorry count: 3 → 3** (file-flat; **HARD BAR partially met** —
2 axiom-clean substrate helpers shipped but 0 of 3 headline sorries
closed). **PUSH-BEYOND not met**: gap (b) Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra
dimension formula remains a genuine Mathlib gap and blocks
`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` body closure.

## Helpers added (axiom-clean, kernel axioms only)

### `module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization` (Stage 5a)
- **Statement**: For an `R`-algebra `S` with `Ω[S⁄R]` free over `S` and
  any submonoid `M ⊆ S`, the localisation `Sₘ` has `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` free over
  `Sₘ`.
- **Proof**: `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` +
  `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`.
- **Axioms used**: `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` (kernel
  only).
- **Use site**: now consumed inline by `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`
  in place of the previous 12-line inline assembly.

### `rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension` (Stage 5b)
- **Statement**: For an `R`-algebra `S` that is
  `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n` and a non-trivial localisation
  `Sₘ`, the rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` over `Sₘ` equals `n`.
- **Proof**: `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
  + `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`.
- **Axioms used**: `propext`, `Classical.choice`, `Quot.sound` (kernel
  only).
- **Use site**: not yet consumed inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`
  (it provides the *specific rank* `n` for the would-be Stage 6
  regularity conclusion; Stage 6 is blocked by the Stacks 00OE Mathlib
  gap below, so the helper sits as standalone substrate for the next
  closure attempt).

## Residual gaps (Stage 6 — Stacks 00OE + 02JK)

Per the iter-193 docstring of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`:

- **(a) Cotangent ↔ Kähler bridge over a field base.** The conormal
  sequence `m/m² ↪ Ω[A⁄R] ⊗_A k(p) ↠ Ω[k(p)⁄R] → 0` (Stacks 02JK) is
  right exact, and the first map is injective when `k(p)/R` is separable.
  Over `R = kbar` algebraically closed and `z` a closed point the
  conclusion is immediate (`Ω[k(p)⁄kbar] = 0` since `k(p) = kbar`); for
  non-closed `z` the residue field is a transcendental extension and the
  injectivity requires the full conormal apparatus. Mathlib has no
  packaged `cotangentSpace_iso_baseChange_kaehlerDifferential_over_field`
  helper as of `b80f227`.

- **(b) Smooth-algebra dimension formula** (Stacks 00OE):
  `ringKrullDim Aₚ = relative dim`. Stage 5b supplies the
  `rank Ω` side of this equation; the `ringKrullDim` side requires
  Stacks 00OE which is not packaged in Mathlib at `b80f227`. Searched
  for `Smooth.*ringKrullDim`, `relativeDimension.*ringKrullDim`,
  `dimension.*krullDim` — no hits.

## `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` (line 434)

### Attempt 1: refactor body using Stage 5a helper
- **Approach**: replace the inline `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`
  call (with explicit Mₛ/M/Rₛ/S/R arguments) with a one-line
  application of `module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization`.
- **Result**: PARTIAL — body now reads as a clean chain
  Stage 1 → 3 → 4 → 5a → `sorry` (gap 6); the localisation-of-freeness
  inline assembly is gone.
- **Lemmas found**:
  - `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free` (rank transports
    through `IsLocalizedModule`).
  - `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (the Kähler module map
    is an `IsLocalizedModule`).
  - `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule` (free transports).

### Attempt 2: PUSH-BEYOND close axiom-clean
- **Approach**: combine Stage 5a (freeness) + Stage 5b (rank = n) + the
  conjectured Stage 6 bridges (a) + (b) to derive `IsRegularLocalRing`.
- **Result**: FAILED — gap (b) Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra dimension
  formula is a genuine Mathlib gap; without it we cannot produce
  `ringKrullDim (stalk z) = n` and so cannot match `finrank
  CotangentSpace` (which would come from gap (a)) against
  `ringKrullDim` via `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`.
- **Dead end**: do NOT attempt body-level closure of
  `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` without first either landing
  Stacks 00OE in Mathlib OR building a project-side
  `ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension_of_standardSmooth`
  substrate lemma.

## Other sorries on the file (touched-not-closed)

### `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (line 673)
- Milne Theorem 3.1: rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete
  variety extends if codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
- Requires:
  1. Valuative criterion of properness on function fields.
  2. `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (currently blocked on
     `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` via
     `smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`).
  3. Depth-≥2 extension at codim-≥2 points (Cohen–Macaulay from
     `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`).
- Untouched this iter — out of scope for the Lane M↓ Stage 5-6 directive.

### `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (line 714)
- Milne Lemma 3.3: rational map to a group variety has indeterminacy
  locus empty or pure codim 1.
- Requires the difference-map construction `Φ(x, y) := f(x) · f(y)⁻¹` +
  Weil divisor decomposition `div(f) = div(f)₀ - div(f)∞` + Krull's
  principal ideal theorem on `X × X`.
- Untouched this iter — out of scope for the Lane M↓ Stage 5-6 directive.

## Recommendations for iter-194+

1. **Build Stacks 00OE project-side substrate** (`ringKrullDim` of
   localisation of a standard-smooth algebra equals its relative
   dimension). This is the single gap unblocking
   `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` axiom-clean closure. Estimate
   ~150-200 LOC; uses `IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension` Stages 3-5
   already in this file.

2. **Build Stacks 02JK project-side substrate** (cotangent ↔ Kähler over
   a field): `m/m² ≃ Ω[A⁄R] ⊗_A k(p)` when `R` is a field and `k(p)/R`
   is separable. Combined with (1), closes Stage 6.

3. **Stage 5b helper consumption** — once (1) lands, the body of
   `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` collapses to:
   ```
   obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := ‹IsStandardSmooth _ _›.exists_relativeDimension
   have hRank := rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension n _ _
   have hDim  := ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension n _ _
   have hCot  := cotangentSpace_finrank_eq_rank_kaehler_of_field n _ _
   rw [IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace]
   omega  -- combine hRank + hDim + hCot
   ```

4. **DO NOT** attempt to type-wrap the Stage 6 gap as a typed sorry
   helper and use it to "close" `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`:
   that would just move sorries around without making progress.

## Blueprint marker recommendation for review agent

- `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one}` —
  body still calls `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` (sorry); no
  `\leanok` for the proof block. Statement block can carry `\leanok`
  (skeleton is fine).
- `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth}` —
  body is `sorry`; no `\leanok` on proof.
- `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme}` —
  body is `sorry`; no `\leanok` on proof.
- New auxiliary helpers (Stages 5a, 5b) are unpinned to blueprint and
  do not need `\leanok` action.

## Build state

`lean_diagnostic_messages` returns 3 sorry warnings only; no errors, no
new dependencies, no new axioms. Mathlib import set unchanged.

1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi, OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | read | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Your **active mode** is injected directly into your invocation prompt — read it carefully, it defines your goal, workflow, and constraints for this session.
127	- When your mode specifies `read_blueprint: true`, you are permitted to **read** your assigned blueprint chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/<your_slug>.tex`) for the informal proof sketch. You may never write to blueprint chapters.
128	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
129	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
130	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
131	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
132	
133	### Subagents
134	
135	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
136	
137	### Review Agent
138	
139	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
140	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
141	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
142	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
143	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
144	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
145	
146	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
147	
148	These run automatically between phases:
149	
150	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
151	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
152	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
153

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).

42	/-! ### Recipe 1 helpers (iter-184 Lane B, `analogies/gmscaling-projection-idiom.md`):
43	project-side simp helpers for `pullback.map ≫ pullback.fst/snd`.
44	
45	Mathlib's `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` are `@[reassoc]` ONLY, not
46	`@[simp]`, so the abbrev `pullback.map _ _ _ _ i₁ i₂ i₃ _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _`
47	is not collapsed by `simp` even though the equation holds definitionally. These
48	two local simp lemmas unblock projection-chain collapse for
49	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` whose STEP 4 uses
50	`asIso (pullback.map …)`. Candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution. -/
51	
52	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
53	lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
54	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
55	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
56	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
57	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
58	        Limits.pullback.fst g₁ g₂ =
59	      Limits.pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁ :=
60	  Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
61	
62	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
63	lemma pullback_map_snd_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
64	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
65	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
66	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
67	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
68	        Limits.pullback.snd g₁ g₂ =
69	      Limits.pullback.snd f₁ f₂ ≫ i₂ :=
70	  Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _
71	
72	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
73	
74	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
75	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
76	definition). -/
77	
78	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
79	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
80	
81	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
82	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
83	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
84	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
85	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
86	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
87	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
88	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
89	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
90	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
91	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
92	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
93	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
94	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
95	  rfl
96	
97	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
98	
99	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
100	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
101	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
102	
103	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
104	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
105	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
106	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
107	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
108	
109	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
110	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
111	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
112	
113	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
114	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
115	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
116	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
117	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
118	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
119	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
120	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
121	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
122	
123	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
124	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
125	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
126	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
128	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
129	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
130	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
131	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
132	        GmRing kbar))
133	
134	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
135	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
136	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
137	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
138	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
139	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
140	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
141	
142	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
143	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
144	
145	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
146	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
147	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
148	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
149	
150	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
151	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers

296	The body is a chain of iso steps:
297	1. `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso q awayι_1 (cover.f 0)`: paste the outer pullback

308	   outer pullback to `pullback q (Spec.map (awayMap _ _) ≫ awayι_0)`.
309	6. `pullback.congrHom rfl (Proj.SpecMap_awayMap_awayι ...)`: identify
310	   `Spec.map (awayMap _ _) ≫ awayι_0 = awayι_(X_0 * X_1)`.

321	            ((MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) * MvPolynomial.X 1))

359	/-- **The substantive `(0, 1)` cross case of `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (iter-181
360	Lane B helper).
361	
362	This is the *single* non-trivial ring-level identity for the cocycle:
363	
364	`pullback.fst ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 0 =
365	   pullback.snd ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≫ chart 1`.
366	
367	On the basic-open intersection `D₊(X 0 · X 1) ⊆ ProjectiveLineBarScheme` both chart
368	coordinates are units (chart-0 coord `t = X 1 / X 0`, chart-1 coord `u = X 0 / X 1`,
369	with `t · u = 1`). Under `σ_×([X 0 : X 1], λ) = [λ · X 0 : X 1]` the two chart maps
370	satisfy on the intersection:
371	
372	* chart-0: `t ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹` (i.e. `t/λ`)
373	* chart-1: `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` (i.e. `λ · u`)
374	
375	Substituting `u = 1/t` on the chart-1 side gives `λ · u = λ · (1/t) = λ/t`, and on the
376	chart-0 side `t/λ` corresponds to `u' = 1/(t/λ) = λ/t` under the `u ↔ 1/t` change of
377	coords. Hence the ring-level identity is `λ · u = (1/t) · λ` in
378	`Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar`, which after multiplying both sides by `t`
379	reduces to `λ · u · t = λ`, true because `u · t = 1`.

823	/-- **Helper:** `TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K` is a domain for any field
824	`K` over `kbar`. This is the "geometrically irreducible affine line" tensor identity,
825	established directly from `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` (no `IsLocalization`
826	machinery needed). -/
827	private lemma isDomain_mvPolyUnit_tensor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
828	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra kbar K] :
829	    IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K) := by
830	  haveI : IsDomain (MvPolynomial Unit K) := inferInstance
831	  -- Iso chain: TensorProduct kbar (MvPoly Unit kbar) K
832	  --   ≃ₐ[kbar] TensorProduct kbar K (MvPoly Unit kbar) (by Algebra.TensorProduct.comm)
833	  --   ≃ₐ[K]    MvPoly Unit K                          (by MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv)
834	  let e1 : TensorProduct kbar K (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit K :=
835	    (MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv (σ := Unit) kbar K).toRingEquiv
836	  let e2 : TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K ≃+* TensorProduct kbar K (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :=
837	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K).toRingEquiv
838	  refine Function.Injective.isDomain (e1.toRingHom.comp e2.toRingHom) ?_
839	  exact e1.injective.comp e2.injective
840	
841	/-- **`(𝔸¹ = Spec k̄[t]).hom` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`** as a scheme
842	morphism `Spec (k̄[t]) ⟶ Spec k̄`. For any field `K` with `Algebra kbar K`, the pullback
843	is `Spec (k̄[t] ⊗_k̄ K) ≅ Spec (K[t])`, which is irreducible because `K[t]` is a domain. -/
844	private lemma affineLine_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
845	    GeometricallyIrreducible
846	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))) := by
847	  refine ⟨?_⟩
848	  rw [geometrically_iff_of_commRing_of_isClosedUnderIsomorphisms]
849	  intro K _ _
850	  haveI hdom : IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K) :=
851	    isDomain_mvPolyUnit_tensor kbar K
852	  -- `Spec` of a domain is irreducible.
853	  haveI hirr : IrreducibleSpace
854	      (Spec (CommRingCat.of (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K))) := by
855	    change IrreducibleSpace
856	      (PrimeSpectrum (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K))
857	    infer_instance
858	  -- Transport via `pullbackSpecIso`.
859	  -- The Iso (pullbackSpecIso ...).symm : Spec(...) ≅ pullback ... gives a homeomorphism
860	  -- from which IrreducibleSpace transfers.
861	  exact (pullbackSpecIso kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K).symm.hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff.mp
862	    hirr
863	
864	/-- **`𝔾_m` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Iter-191 closure via the
865	basic-open / open-immersion route:
866	
867	* `(Gm).hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))` factors as
868	  `Spec.map (algebraMap (MvPoly Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)) ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (MvPoly Unit kbar))`
869	  via `IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply`.
870	* The first arrow is an open immersion (Mathlib instance
871	  `isOpenImmersion_SpecMap_localizationAway`: `Gm = D₊(t) ⊂ 𝔸¹`).
872	* The second arrow is `GeometricallyIrreducible` (`affineLine_geomIrred`).
873	* `Surjective` of the composition is immediate because the target `Spec k̄` is a single
874	  point and the source is nonempty.
875	* Conclude via the Mathlib instance
876	  `[IsOpenImmersion f] [GeometricallyIrreducible g] [Surjective (f ≫ g)] →
877	  GeometricallyIrreducible (f ≫ g)`.
878	
879	Exported here for Lane B and for the `projGm_geomIrred` derivation. -/
880	instance gm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
881	    GeometricallyIrreducible (Gm kbar).hom := by
882	  -- Rewrite (Gm).hom as a composition Gm ↪ 𝔸¹ → Spec k̄.
883	  haveI hg_irr : GeometricallyIrreducible
884	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))) :=
885	    affineLine_geomIrred kbar
886	  have hcomp : (Gm kbar).hom =
887	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) ≫
888	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))) := by
889	    show Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = _
890	    rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
891	    congr 1
892	  rw [hcomp]
893	  -- Surjective: target Spec k̄ is subsingleton, source is nonempty.
894	  haveI hsurj : Surjective (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
895	        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) ≫
896	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))) := by
897	    constructor
898	    intro p
899	    obtain ⟨x⟩ : Nonempty (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))) := inferInstance
900	    refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
901	    exact Subsingleton.elim _ _
902	  haveI := hg_irr
903	  haveI := hsurj
904	  -- Mathlib's open-immersion + GeometricallyIrreducible composition instance
905	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.Geometrically.Irreducible`-:131).
906	  set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
907	    exact inferInstance

909	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).hom` is geometrically irreducible.** Derives from the individual factors
910	via `GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` (with `UniversallyOpen` discharged for free by smoothness
911	of each factor). The `(X ⊗ Y).hom = pullback.fst ≫ X.hom` defeq unfolds, then
912	`GeometricallyIrreducible.comp` chains `pullback.fst`'s GI (by base-change stability of GI
913	from `gm_geomIrred`) with `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`.
914	
915	Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hProdGI`). Axiom-clean given the
916	individual GI scaffolds. -/
917	instance projGm_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
918	    GeometricallyIrreducible ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
919	  change GeometricallyIrreducible
920	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
921	  exact GeometricallyIrreducible.comp _ _
922	
923	/-- **`(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left` is reduced.** Iter-191 closure via the chart-local
924	recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3 Application 2:
925	
926	* Cover `(PLB ⊗ Gm).left` by the existing `gmScalingP1_cover`; the chart-`i`
927	  source is `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗_kbar GmRing kbar)` via
928	  `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`.
929	* `(Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗_kbar GmRing kbar` is a domain by Substrate 2
930	  (`gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain` at the degree-1 generator
931	  `(![X 0, X 1] i)`).
932	* `IsDomain → IsReduced` on the carrier ring; `Spec` of a reduced ring is
933	  reduced; transport reducedness along `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso.hom` (an
934	  iso, hence `IsOpenImmersion`) via `isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion`.
935	* Conclude `IsReduced ((PLB ⊗ Gm).left)` via `IsReduced.of_openCover`. -/
936	instance projGm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
937	    IsReduced ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left := by
938	  haveI hchart : ∀ i : (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).I₀,
939	      IsReduced ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i) := by
940	    intro (i : Fin 2)
941	    have hf_deg : ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
942	                    Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i) ∈
943	          projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
944	      projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i
945	    have hm : 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i := projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i
946	    have hne : ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
947	                  Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i) ≠ 0 := by
948	      fin_cases i <;> simp
949	    haveI hdom : IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar
950	        (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
951	          ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
952	            Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
953	        (GmRing kbar)) :=
954	      AlgebraicGeometry.gmRing_tensor_homogeneousAway_isDomain kbar hm _ hf_deg hne
955	    exact isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom
956	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover _ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar)

1586	      The chart-agreement equation
1587	      \(\mathrm{pullback.fst} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,0 =
1588	      \mathrm{pullback.snd} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,\bar k\,1\)
1589	      on the cover intersection
1590	      \(\mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_0.f)\,
1591	      ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_1.f)\) is the statement that two morphisms
1592	      \(\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1 \colon \mathrm{(intersection)} \to
1593	      \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) agree on that intersection scheme. \emph{The key
1594	      observation is that \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\) is separated over \(\bar k\)},
1595	      so the diagonal
1596	      \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}} \colon
1597	        \overline{\mathbb P^1} \longrightarrow
1598	        \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1599	      is a closed immersion. Because \(\overline{\mathbb P^1} = \Proj(\bar k[X_0, X_1])\)
1600	      is the \(\Proj\) of a graded \(\bar k\)-algebra, its separatedness over \(\Spec
1601	      \bar k\) is the standard Stacks fact ``\(\Proj(S)\) is separated'' (Stacks tag
1602	      \texttt{01KU} / lemma \texttt{lemma-proj-separated}); Mathlib supplies the
1603	      categorical packaging via \(\mathtt{IsSeparated}\) and
1604	      \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\).
1605	      \emph{Bypass.} Reformulate the cocycle as: the pair-morphism
1606	      \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1) \colon \mathrm{(intersection)}
1607	      \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1608	      (built via \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\) on
1609	      \(\mathrm{chart}_0 := \mathrm{pullback.fst} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,
1610	      \bar k\,0\) and
1611	      \(\mathrm{chart}_1 := \mathrm{pullback.snd} \fatsemi \mathtt{gmScalingP1\_chart}\,
1612	      \bar k\,1\)) \emph{factors through the diagonal
1613	      \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\)}. By Mathlib's
1614	      \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift\_iff\_range\_subset}\) (the universal property
1615	      of a closed immersion: a morphism factors through a closed immersion iff its
1616	      set-theoretic image is contained in the image of the immersion), the
1617	      factorization is equivalent to the set-level containment of the cocycle's image
1618	      in the image of \(\Delta\), which on points is exactly the chart equality on
1619	      the \emph{closed points} of the intersection. The closed-point check is
1620	      discrete (each closed point of the intersection lies over a single \(\bar k\)-
1621	      point of \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \times \mathbb G_m\) and the two chart maps both
1622	      evaluate to the same \(\bar k\)-point of \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1623	      corresponding to that \((x, \lambda)\)), and bypasses the simp coverage gap
1624	      that obstructs (III.a)/(III.b) entirely.
1625	      \emph{Concrete prover recipe.} The path closes in four steps:
1626	      \begin{enumerate}
1627	        \item Fix the diagonal
1628	          \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}} \colon \overline{\mathbb P^1}
1629	            \longrightarrow \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k}
1630	            \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1631	          via Mathlib's \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Limits.prod.lift}\,(\mathbf
1632	          1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\,(\mathbf 1_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}})\), and
1633	          obtain the closed-immersion witness from
1634	          \(\mathtt{IsSeparated.diagonal\_isClosedImmersion}\) applied to the
1635	          separated structure morphism
1636	          \(\overline{\mathbb P^1}.\mathrm{hom} \colon \overline{\mathbb P^1} \to
1637	          \Spec \bar k\).
1638	        \item Build the pair-morphism
1639	          \((\mathrm{chart}_0, \mathrm{chart}_1) \colon
1640	            \mathrm{pullback}\,((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_0.f)\,
1641	              ((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_1.f)
1642	            \longrightarrow
1643	            \overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\)
1644	          via \(\mathtt{prod.lift}\) on the two named chart restrictions above.
1645	        \item Show this pair factors through \(\Delta_{\overline{\mathbb P^1}}\)
1646	          via the universal property of the intersection's pullback structure
1647	          combined with \(\mathtt{IsClosedImmersion.lift}\) (kernel-inequality
1648	          form; the \(\mathtt{\_iff\_range\_subset}\) variant is NOT in Mathlib
1649	          at \texttt{b80f227} per iter-188 verification, so this step requires
1650	          either upstream contribution or a project-side bridge plus
1651	          \(\mathtt{IsReduced}\) on the intersection): produce the section
1652	          \(\mathrm{(intersection)} \to \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) whose post-
1653	          composition with \(\Delta\) equals \((\mathrm{chart}_0,
1654	          \mathrm{chart}_1)\).
1655	        \item The factorization reduces, by composing with the two projections
1656	          \(\mathtt{prod.fst}\) and \(\mathtt{prod.snd}\) out of the codomain
1657	          \(\overline{\mathbb P^1} \times_{\bar k} \overline{\mathbb P^1}\), to
1658	          two scheme-equalities on the components --- each of which is a direct
1659	          chart-bridge equality on \((\mathtt{gmScalingP1\_cover})_i\)'s structure
1660	          (i.e.\ the \emph{single-chart} content, not the full cocycle), discharged
1661	          using the same chart-ring iso \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and
1662	          \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses.
1663	          The chart-\(1\) instance of the per-component equality threads
1664	          through the named \(\mathrm{Proj.appIso}\) evaluation
1665	          \cref{lem:iotaGm_chart1_appIso_eval}, which packages the basic-open
1666	          ring-map identification
1667	          \(\mathtt{isLocElem} \mapsto [X_0/X_1] \mapsto 1\) at the global-sections
1668	          level so the prover can target it directly without re-deriving the
1669	          \((\mathrm{Proj.appIso}).\mathrm{inv}\) image of \(\mathtt{isLocElem}\)
1670	          inline (the iter-188 through iter-191 budget-wall step). The chart-\(0\)
1671	          instance is the symmetric statement with roles of \(X_0, X_1\)
1672	          interchanged.
1673	      \end{enumerate}

1700	  \noindent\textbf{(IV) Substantive ring-level residual.} All three pickup paths reduce,
1701	  after their structural setup, to the same ring-level identity. On the basic-open
1702	  intersection \(D_+(X_0 \cdot X_1) \subseteq \overline{\mathbb P^1}\) both chart
1703	  coordinates are units (the chart-\(0\) coordinate \(t = X_1/X_0\) and the chart-\(1\)
1704	  coordinate \(u = X_0/X_1\) are reciprocals: \(t \cdot u = 1\)), and the agreement
1705	  reduces to the ring-level identity
1706	  \[ \lambda \cdot u \ =\ \frac{1}{t} \cdot \lambda \quad
1707	     \text{in } \mathrm{Localization.Away}\,t \otimes_{\bar k} \mathtt{GmRing}\,\bar k, \]
1708	  i.e.\ to the unit-of-the-localisation identity \(t \cdot u = 1\)
1709	  (\(\mathtt{IsLocalization.Away.mul\_invSelf}\)) together with commutativity of the
1710	  tensor product. The proof routes through the chart-ring iso
1711	  \cref{def:proj_chart_ring_iso} and the same \(\mathtt{pullbackSpecIso}\) bridge that
1712	  \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} uses; consequently \cref{lem:gmscaling_chart_agreement}
1713	  remains gated on \cref{def:gmscaling_chart} acquiring its concrete body \emph{and} on
1714	  one of the (III.a)--(III.c) structural setups landing. See
1715	  \(\mathtt{analogies/gmscaling{\text-}projection{\text-}idiom.md}\) (iter-184) for the
1716	  full Recipe~1/2/3 decomposition and empirical simp-probe verification.
1717	\end{lemma}

14	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` cocycle proof (path (III.c) of

20	applications and, if upstreamed, by Mathlib itself.  Their construction
21	follows the recipe of `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` (§2) and the prover-facing
22	specification at `blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`.

24	## Contents (this iter)
25

27	  Substrate 1 (iter-189, axiom-clean): the closed-immersion universal
28	  property phrased as a topological range containment (under reduced

38	set_option autoImplicit false
39	set_option linter.style.setOption false
40	set_option linter.style.show false
41	set_option linter.unusedSimpArgs false
42	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000

50	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

52	namespace IsClosedImmersion
53	
54	/--
55	**Closed immersion lift by range inclusion.**
56	For a closed immersion `i : Z ⟶ X` whose source `Z` is reduced and a
57	quasi-compact morphism `g : Y ⟶ X` whose source `Y` is reduced, `g` factors
58	through `i` if and only if the topological range of `g` is contained in the
59	topological range of `i`.
60	
61	This is the topological-range form of the universal property of closed
62	immersions; the kernel-inequality form is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` /
63	`IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac`.  The two forms are interchanged by the Galois
64	connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support` on ideal sheaves
65	(`IdealSheafData.gc` / `IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`).
66	
67	This is **Substrate 1** of the Lane B Cross01 cocycle proof; see
68	`blueprint/src/chapters/Genus0BaseObjects_Cross01Substrate.tex`,
69	`Theorem~\ref{thm:IsClosedImmersion_lift_iff_range_subset}`.
70	-/
71	theorem lift_iff_range_subset
72	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
73	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
74	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔ Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base := by
75	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
76	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: range pushdown.
77	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
78	    rintro _ ⟨y, rfl⟩
79	    refine ⟨h.base y, ?_⟩
80	    simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_base]
81	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substantive content.
82	    intro hrange
83	    -- Helper: the kernel of a ring hom into a reduced ring is a radical ideal.
84	    have ker_isRadical : ∀ {R S : Type u} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring S]
85	        [_root_.IsReduced S] (f : R →+* S), (RingHom.ker f).IsRadical := by
86	      intro R S _ _ _ f x hx
87	      obtain ⟨n, hxn⟩ := hx
88	      rw [RingHom.mem_ker] at hxn ⊢
89	      have h₁ : (f x) ^ n = 0 := by
90	        rw [← map_pow]; exact hxn
91	      exact eq_zero_of_pow_eq_zero h₁
92	    -- Step 1: For a closed immersion, the support of its kernel equals its range.
93	    -- (`support_ker` gives `closure (range)`; closed range collapses the closure.)
94	    have hi_range_eq : (i.ker.support : Set X) = Set.range i.base := by
95	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
96	      exact i.isClosedEmbedding.isClosed_range.closure_eq
97	    -- Step 2: `g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support` (set-theoretic, via `closure_minimal`).
98	    have hsup_set : (g.ker.support : Set X) ⊆ (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
99	      rw [Scheme.Hom.support_ker]
100	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.support.isClosed
101	      exact hrange.trans hi_range_eq.ge
102	    -- Promote the set inclusion to the `Closeds X` ordering.
103	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := hsup_set
104	    -- Step 3: `g.ker` is radical (because `Y` is reduced, so each `Γ(Y, g ⁻¹ᵁ U)` is reduced
105	    -- and the ring kernel is therefore radical, pointwise on affine opens).
106	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
107	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
108	      intro U
109	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
110	      exact ker_isRadical (g.app U.1).hom
111	    -- Step 4: `i.ker` is radical (same argument; closed immersions are quasi-compact).
112	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
113	      refine le_antisymm ?_ (Scheme.IdealSheafData.le_radical _)
114	      intro U
115	      rw [Scheme.IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, Scheme.Hom.ker_apply]
116	      exact ker_isRadical (i.app U.1).hom
117	    -- Step 5: thread the Galois connection `vanishingIdeal ⊣ support`.
118	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
119	      calc i.ker
120	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
121	        _ = Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal i.ker.support :=
122	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support.symm
123	        _ ≤ Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal g.ker.support :=
124	              Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
125	        _ = g.ker.radical := Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support
126	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
127	    -- Step 6: feed the kernel inequality to Mathlib's `IsClosedImmersion.lift`.
128	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker, IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
129	
130	end IsClosedImmersion
131	
132	/--
133	**Substrate 2 — `Away f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄` is a domain.**
134	
135	For a non-zero homogeneous element `f` of positive degree `d` in the
136	`projectiveLineBar` grading on `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄`, the tensor
137	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading k̄) f ⊗_{k̄} GmRing k̄`
138	is an integral domain.
139	
140	The proof identifies the tensor with the localization
141	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` via the
142	chain of natural isomorphisms in `analogies/lane-b-substrate.md` §3:
143	* `Away f ⊗_{k̄} 𝕜[t] ≃ 𝕜[t][Away f]` (`MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`),
144	* tensor base change with the localization
145	  `𝕜[t] → GmRing k̄` produces `Localization.Away (X ())`
146	  (`IsLocalization.Away.tensorRight`).
147	
148	`Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit (Away f))` is a domain because
149	it is the localization of `MvPolynomial Unit (Away f)` (a polynomial ring
150	over the domain `Away f`) at the powers of the non-zero generator `X ()`.

12	## Question
13	
14	For the Lane B (`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01`) iter-188 (III.c)
15	separated-locus structural setup, the substantive residual is a pair-
16	morphism factorization through the diagonal closed immersion. The
17	blueprint cites `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` as the
18	substrate; iter-188 verified this is NOT in Mathlib at b80f227. Two
19	questions:
20	
21	1. Is there a Mathlib variant of "morphism factors through closed
22	   immersion iff topological range subset" under any reasonable
23	   reduced-source hypothesis?
24	2. What is the cleanest project-side construction (with LOC), and what
25	   blocks the tensor-reducedness gap that also obstructs the other two
26	   off-target sorries (`gm_geomIrred`, `projGm_isReduced`)?

45	## Decisions identified
46	
47	### Decision 1: `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` (the substantive substrate)
48	
49	- **Mathlib idiom**: NONE at b80f227. The only `lift` variant shipped
50	  is `IsClosedImmersion.lift` with the kernel-inequality form
51	  (`f.ker ≤ g.ker`), at
52	  `.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/ClosedImmersion.lean:200-214`.
53	  The `lift_iff_range_subset` variant — equating factor-through with
54	  set-theoretic range containment under a reduced-source hypothesis —
55	  is not in Mathlib (verified iter-188 via `lean_leansearch` and
56	  re-verified iter-189 via `lean_leanfinder`).
57	
58	- **Mathlib substrate that DOES exist** (and which a project-side build
59	  would chain): the iter-189 search recovered the complete chain of
60	  primitives needed:
61	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker`
62	    (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/IdealSheaf/Basic.lean:689`):
63	    ideal-sheaf kernel of a scheme morphism.
64	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.ker_apply`
65	    (same file, `:698-734`): for `[QuasiCompact f]`,
66	    `f.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom` on each affine open.
67	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.support_ker`
68	    (same file, `:839-878`): for `[QuasiCompact f]`,
69	    `f.ker.support = closure (Set.range f)`.
70	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.range_subset_ker_support`
71	    (same file, `:754-764`): the unconditional inclusion
72	    `Set.range f ⊆ f.ker.support`.
73	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal_support`
74	    (same file, `:631-639`):
75	    `vanishingIdeal I.support = I.radical` — the Galois-connection
76	    pinning of "support determines radical".
77	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.gc`
78	    (same file, `:622-625`):
79	    `GaloisConnection (support ·) (vanishingIdeal ·)` (with the
80	    `Closedsᵒᵈ` ordering).
81	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι`
82	    (`IdealSheaf/Subscheme.lean`):
83	    `Set.range I.subschemeι.base = I.support` — for a closed immersion
84	    `i`, `Set.range i.base = i.ker.support`.
85	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift`,
86	    `lift_fac`, `lift_fac_assoc`

90	- **Project's current path**: iter-188 lands a structural setup that
91	  introduces `s_pair := pullback.lift (chart0_via_fst) (chart1_via_snd) hPLB_agree`
92	  and the closed-immersion diagonal `hΔ`, leaving a single direct

101	- **Cost of divergence**: Building `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset`
102	  project-side costs ~40-60 LOC; the alternative (waiting on Mathlib

107	
108	### Decision 2: Tensor reducedness of `(Away (X_0·X_1)) ⊗_kbar (GmRing kbar)`
109	
110	This is the SECOND substrate needed, blocking three sorries
111	simultaneously (the cocycle factorization plus the two off-target
112	`gm_geomIrred` / `projGm_isReduced` instances at L755, L779).
113	
114	- **Mathlib idiom check**: NONE direct. The closest shipped:
115	  - `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced` class
116	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Nilpotent/GeometricallyReduced.lean:47`):
117	    requires `IsReduced (AlgebraicClosure k ⊗_k A)`. The bridge
118	    "k alg-closed ⟹ IsReduced ⟹ IsGeometricallyReduced k" is NOT
119	    shipped (the file's own TODO confirms: "for every field extension
120	    K of k the K-algebra K ⊗[k] A is reduced" — not landed for the
121	    general field-extension case).
122	  - `Algebra.instIsReducedTensorProductOfIsAlgebraicOfIsGeometricallyReduced`
123	    (same file, `:52`): an instance for `IsReduced (K ⊗_k A)` when
124	    `K/k` algebraic AND `A` geometrically reduced over `k`. Doesn't
125	    apply directly because neither factor here is algebraic over kbar.
126	  - `Subalgebra.LinearDisjoint.isDomain_of_injective`
127	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LinearDisjoint.lean`): the abstract route
128	    "tensor of two domains embedding into a common domain with
129	    linearly-disjoint ranges is a domain". Building the LinearDisjoint
130	    witness for our concrete factors would be its own ~30-50 LOC
131	    sub-task.
132	
133	- **The clean project path** (the analogist's recommended route):
134	  exploit Mathlib's **localization–polynomial tensor product
135	  iso machinery**. The relevant Mathlib lemmas, both shipped:
136	  - `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv`
137	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/BaseChange.lean`):
138	    `TensorProduct R A S ≃ₐ[S] Localization.Away ((algebraMap R S) r)`
139	    when `A = Localization.Away r` over `R`. Tensor of a localization
140	    with anything is a localization (in the tensored ring).
141	  - `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`
142	    (`Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/MvPolynomial.lean`):
143	    `TensorProduct R A (MvPolynomial σ R) ≃ₐ[A] MvPolynomial σ A`.
144	    Tensor with a polynomial ring over the base is just polynomials
145	    over the algebra.
146	
147	  Both factors of our tensor are localizations of polynomial rings
148	  over kbar:
149	  - `GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`
150	    — i.e., `kbar[t][1/t]`.

150	    — i.e., `kbar[t][1/t]`.
151	  - `Away (X_0·X_1) = HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projLineBarGrading) (X_0·X_1)`
152	    — a kbar-algebra that is a domain (already proven in
153	    `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` chain via the `val_injective`
154	    embedding into `Localization.Away (X_0·X_1)`).
155	
156	  Composition:
157	  1. `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar kbar[t] ≃ MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))`
158	     via `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv`.
159	  2. The localization `kbar[t] → kbar[t][1/t] = GmRing` corresponds via
160	     `IsScalarTower` + `IsLocalization.Away.tensorRightEquiv` to
161	     the localization at the same generator's image in the tensored
162	     polynomial ring.
163	  3. Result: `Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing ≃ Localization.Away (X () in MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1)))`.
164	  4. `MvPolynomial Unit (Away (X_0·X_1))` is a polynomial ring over a
165	     domain, hence a domain.
166	  5. Localization at powers of `X ()` (a non-zero divisor in the
167	     polynomial ring over a domain) preserves the domain property
168	     via `IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` +
169	     `powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors`
170	     + `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero` (already in the project's playbook,
171	     used in `projectiveLineBar_isReduced` L734-738).
172	
173	  Therefore `IsDomain (Away (X_0·X_1) ⊗_kbar GmRing)` follows, and
174	  `IsReduced` follows from `IsDomain` directly.
175	
176	- **Verdict**: **NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL** — buildable at ~50-80 LOC.
177	  No new Mathlib infrastructure needed; everything is a chain of
178	  existing iso lemmas. The same pattern unifies the `gm_geomIrred` /
179	  `projGm_isReduced` closures (same generators, different chart degrees).
180	
181	### Decision 3: Where to land the substrate
182	
183	- **Recommended file layout**:
184	  - New helper file: `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Cross01Substrate.lean`
185	    importing `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`, `Points`. Contains:
186	    - `tensor_AwayGm_isDomain` (the tensor reducedness lemma, generic
187	      in the homogeneous generator's degree).
188	    - `IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset` (the substrate lemma,
189	      generic in the schemes).
190	  - Cross01 cocycle body lifts into `GmScaling.lean` (no file move
191	    needed — the substrate is reused from `Cross01Substrate.lean`).
192	
193	- **Why a new file vs inline in `GmScaling.lean`**:
194	  - The substrate is generic (no `gmScalingP1`-specific dependencies).
195	  - The substrate is reused by `gm_geomIrred` and `projGm_isReduced`

198	  - The substrate may be promotable to Mathlib later (the

201	
202	- **Verdict**: **BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER** in a new file `Cross01Substrate.lean`.

262	theorem AlgebraicGeometry.IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset
263	    {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (i : Z ⟶ X) [IsClosedImmersion i] [IsReduced Z]
264	    (g : Y ⟶ X) [QuasiCompact g] [IsReduced Y] :
265	    (∃ h : Y ⟶ Z, h ≫ i = g) ↔
266	      Set.range g.base ⊆ Set.range i.base
267	```
268	
269	Body skeleton (with `≈ 40-60` LOC):
270	
271	```lean
272	  constructor
273	  · -- (⇒) Forward direction: trivial range pushdown.
274	    rintro ⟨h, rfl⟩
275	    intro x ⟨y, rfl⟩
276	    exact ⟨h.base y, rfl⟩
277	  · -- (⇐) Backward direction: the substance.
278	    intro hrange
279	    -- Step 1: closed immersion ⟹ range is closed, equal to ker.support.
280	    have hi_range : Set.range i.base = (i.ker.support : Set X) := by
281	      rw [← IdealSheafData.range_subschemeι]
282	      -- Use that i = subschemeι i.ker (up to iso) via toImage_imageι
283	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC bridge using Scheme.Hom.toImage / imageι
284	    -- Step 2: g.ker.support = closure (range g) ⊆ i.ker.support
285	    --         (latter is closed).
286	    have hsup : g.ker.support ≤ i.ker.support := by
287	      rw [Hom.support_ker]
288	      refine closure_minimal ?_ i.ker.isClosed_supportSet
289	      exact hrange.trans hi_range.le
290	    -- Step 3: Y reduced ⟹ g.ker is radical
291	    --         (since g.ker.ideal U = RingHom.ker (g.app U)
292	    --          and Y reduced ⟹ that kernel is radical).
293	    have hg_rad : g.ker.radical = g.ker := by
294	      ext U : 2
295	      simp only [IdealSheafData.radical_ideal, g.ker_apply]
296	      exact (RingHom.ker_isRadical_iff_reduced_of_surjective
297	        Function.Surjective.rfl).mpr inferInstance  -- needs adjustment
298	      -- Concretely: use `f.app U`'s kernel-is-radical because Y is reduced;
299	      -- the appropriate lemma is `RingHom.ker_isRadical_of_reduced_codomain`.
300	      sorry  -- ~10 LOC; standard "reduced source ⟹ kernel of ring map radical"
301	    -- Step 4: Z reduced ⟹ i.ker is radical (same argument).
302	    have hi_rad : i.ker.radical = i.ker := by
303	      ext U : 2
304	      sorry  -- ~5 LOC, symmetric to Step 3
305	    -- Step 5: combine via Galois connection.
306	    --         g.ker.support ⊆ i.ker.support
307	    --         ⟹ vanishingIdeal(i.ker.support) ≤ vanishingIdeal(g.ker.support)
308	    --         ⟹ i.ker.radical ≤ g.ker.radical (by `vanishingIdeal_support`)
309	    --         ⟹ i.ker ≤ g.ker (by Steps 3, 4).
310	    have hker : i.ker ≤ g.ker := by
311	      calc i.ker
312	          = i.ker.radical := hi_rad.symm
313	        _ = vanishingIdeal i.ker.support := vanishingIdeal_support.symm
314	        _ ≤ vanishingIdeal g.ker.support := vanishingIdeal_antimono hsup
315	        _ = g.ker.radical := vanishingIdeal_support
316	        _ = g.ker := hg_rad
317	    -- Step 6: apply Mathlib's IsClosedImmersion.lift.
318	    exact ⟨IsClosedImmersion.lift i g hker,
319	           IsClosedImmersion.lift_fac i g hker⟩
320	```
321
