165	/-! ## §1. Pin 1 — the morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹` from a globally-generating pair
166	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
167	
168	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
169	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
170	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
171	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
172	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
173	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
174	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
175	proof) yields the pinned morphism
176	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
177	
178	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
179	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
180	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
181	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
182	
183	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
184	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
185	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
186	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
187	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
188	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
189	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
190	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
191	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
192	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
193	section condition.
194	
195	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
196	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
197	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
198	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
199	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
200	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
201	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
202	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
203	
204	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard
205	grading `projectiveLineBarGrading k̄` (the `ℕ`-grading on
206	`MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` by total degree, with `GradedRing` instance
207	via `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra`), then wraps in `Over.homMk` with the
208	section condition proof. The section condition chases through
209	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus
210	`IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)` to reduce to
211	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,
212	which the `halg` hypothesis closes via `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` and
213	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (cf. the analogous chain in
214	`Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:pointOfVec`).
215	
216	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
217	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/
218	noncomputable def morphismToP1OfGlobalSections
219	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
220	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
221	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(X.left, ⊤))
222	    (_halg :
223	      f.comp (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) =
224	        X.hom.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom)
225	    (_hf :
226	      Ideal.map f
227	          (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤) :
228	    X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
229	  -- Underlying scheme morphism: invoke Mathlib's
230	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on `projectiveLineBarGrading kbar`.
231	  -- The result `X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar` is
232	  -- then wrapped via `Over.homMk` with the section condition
233	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`.
234	  Over.homMk
235	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf) <| by
236	    -- Section condition (`Over (Spec k̄)`-compatibility):
237	    --   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`
238	    -- where `ProjectiveLineBar.hom = Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))`.
239	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
240	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
241	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq
242	        (R := kbar) (S := ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0))
243	        (A := MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (fun _ => rfl)
244	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
245	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
246	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
247	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
248	    -- Goal: `X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`.
249	    -- Step 1: convert the `_halg` RingHom equation to a CommRingCat-level equation.
250	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C) =
251	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ X.hom.appTop := by
252	      rw [← MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, _halg, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
253	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom, CommRingCat.ofHom_hom]
254	    -- Step 2: rewrite, distribute `Spec.map`, and apply naturality of `toSpecΓ` plus
255	    -- the `toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso).inv = 𝟙` identity on `Spec (.of kbar)`.
256	    rw [hcc, Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality,
257	      Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
258	
259	/-! ## §2. Pin 2 — the degree of a non-constant `C ⟶ ℙ¹` via its pole divisor
260	(`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`)
261	
262	Hartshorne II §6 + IV §2 opening: for a non-constant morphism
263	`φ : C ⟶ ℙ¹` from a smooth proper curve, the function-field extension
264	`k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite, and the degree
265	`[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] = deg(φ)` is recovered as the degree of the pullback
266	of any closed point of `ℙ¹` to `C`. Specialising to `Q = ∞`, this
267	identifies `deg(φ)` with the degree of the **pole divisor** of the
268	pulled-back affine coordinate `φ^* u`.
269	
270	For the iter-177 file-skeleton the substantive content is the
271	existence of a positive-degree Weil divisor on `C` realising
272	`deg(φ)`; the body in iter-178+ produces this divisor as the pole
273	divisor of `φ^* u` via the Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree
274	identity.
275	
276	This is a *separable* sub-build pin (the lemma is stated independently
277	of the headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` and does not require `g(C) = 0`).
278	-/
279	
280	/-- **A local parameter at the closed point `∞ ∈ ℙ¹_{k̄}`** — a non-zero
281	rational function on `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar)` whose order at `∞` is `1`
282	(in Hartshorne's standard affine convention: `t_∞ = X₀ / X₁`, with `X₁` the
283	coordinate trivialising the chart at `∞`). Returned as a subtype packaging
284	the element together with its non-zero witness, for use in
285	`Hom.poleDivisor` below.
286	
287	**iter-187 substrate gap — single named typed sorry.** The substantive
288	choice (the chart-1 ratio coordinate `X₀ / X₁ ∈
289	HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (X 1)`,
290	embedded into `(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField`) is iter-188+
291	work. The substrate hooks (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`):
292	- `AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBarAffineCover` chart-`1` open
293	  (`Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean`).
294	- `HomogeneousLocalization.Away` to extract the global ratio function on
295	  the chart, then `germToFunctionField` to embed in `K(ℙ¹)`.
296	- The non-zero witness is automatic from injectivity of the ratio-as-a-
297	  HomogeneousLocalization-Away-element (the ratio `X₀ / X₁` is not the
298	  zero element of the localised graded ring).
299	
300	This helper is the **single substrate gap** carrying the iter-188 unblock
301	for the consumer scaffold `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` Steps 1-5
302	below; the gap is **named, typed, and topologically localised**, not a
303	bare body sorry. -/
304	private noncomputable def localParameterAtInfty (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
305	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left] :
306	    { t : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField // t ≠ 0 } := by
307	  classical
308	  -- The grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`.
309	  let 𝒜 : ℕ → Submodule kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
310	    projectiveLineBarGrading kbar
311	  -- The element `f = X 1` is homogeneous of degree 1.
312	  let f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar := MvPolynomial.X 1
313	  have hf : f ∈ 𝒜 1 := MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1
314	  have hf0 : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈ 𝒜 (1 • 1) := by
315	    simpa using MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 0
316	  -- The ratio coordinate `X 0 / X 1 ∈ Away 𝒜 (X 1)` (degree 0).
317	  let x01 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f :=
318	    HomogeneousLocalization.Away.mk 𝒜 hf 1 (MvPolynomial.X 0) hf0
319	  -- Basic facts about `X 0` and `X 1`.
320	  have hf_ne : f ≠ 0 := MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero 1
321	  have hX0_ne : (MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≠ 0 :=
322	    MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero 0
323	  -- Step (a): `x01 ≠ 0` in `Away 𝒜 (X 1)`.
324	  have hx01_ne : x01 ≠ 0 := by
325	    intro heq
326	    have hval :
327	        x01.val =
328	          (0 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val :=
329	      congrArg HomogeneousLocalization.val heq
330	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero,
331	        HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk] at hval
332	    rw [Localization.mk_eq_mk'_apply,
333	        IsLocalization.mk'_eq_zero_iff] at hval
334	    obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := hval
335	    rcases c.2 with ⟨n, hcn⟩
336	    have hcv_ne : (c : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≠ 0 := by
337	      simp only [← hcn]
338	      exact pow_ne_zero _ hf_ne
339	    exact hX0_ne ((mul_eq_zero.mp hc).resolve_left hcv_ne)
340	  -- Step (b): `Away 𝒜 (X 1)` is nontrivial (needed for `Nonempty (basicOpen f)`).
341	  haveI hL : Nontrivial (Localization.Away f) :=
342	    (IsLocalization.injective (Localization.Away f)
343	      (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors hf_ne)).nontrivial
344	  haveI hAway : Nontrivial (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f) := by
345	    refine ⟨1, 0, ?_⟩
346	    intro heq
347	    have hval :
348	        (1 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val =
349	          (0 : HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f).val :=
350	      congrArg HomogeneousLocalization.val heq
351	    rw [HomogeneousLocalization.val_one,
352	        HomogeneousLocalization.val_zero] at hval
353	    exact one_ne_zero hval
354	  -- Step (c): the chart-1 affine open `U = D₊(X 1)` is nonempty (via `awayι`).
355	  let U : (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.Opens := Proj.basicOpen 𝒜 f
356	  haveI hU_nonempty : Nonempty U := by
357	    obtain ⟨p⟩ := (inferInstance :
358	        Nonempty (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 f))))
359	    refine ⟨⟨(Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).base p, ?_⟩⟩
360	    -- The point lies in the image of `awayι`, which equals `basicOpen 𝒜 f`.
361	    have hrange : (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).opensRange = U :=
362	      Proj.opensRange_awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos
363	    rw [show U = (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).opensRange from hrange.symm]
364	    exact ⟨p, rfl⟩
365	  -- Step (d): build the section in `Γ(Proj 𝒜, U)`.
366	  let sec : Γ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left, U) :=
367	    (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom.hom x01
368	  -- Step (e): embed in the function field via `germToFunctionField`.
369	  refine ⟨((ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.germToFunctionField U).hom sec, ?_⟩
370	  -- Step (f): non-zero via injectivity.
371	  intro ht
372	  -- `germToFunctionField` is injective on integral schemes.
373	  have hinj_germ :=
374	    Scheme.germToFunctionField_injective (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left U
375	  have hsec_zero : sec = 0 := by
376	    apply hinj_germ
377	    rw [map_zero]
378	    exact ht
379	  -- `basicOpenIsoAway` is an iso, hence its `.hom.hom` is injective.
380	  have hiso_inj :
381	      Function.Injective
382	        (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.hom
383	          (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom) :=
384	    (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _).1
385	  apply hx01_ne
386	  apply hiso_inj
387	  change sec = (Proj.basicOpenIsoAway 𝒜 f hf Nat.one_pos).hom.hom 0
388	  rw [hsec_zero, map_zero]
389	  rfl

391	/-! ### Iter-190 → iter-191 Lane I Pin 2 corrective substrate
392	(public `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` + Hartshorne~II.6.9 public pin)
393	
394	Per iter-190 plan-phase Pin 2 corrective Option (a): `Hom.poleDivisor` is
395	defined as `positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t_∞) halg) =
396	(φ^* t_∞)_0 = φ^*[∞]`. The iter-191 refactor lifts the underlying
397	`positivePart` def and the `degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`
398	typed-sorry pin from file-local `private` placeholders to the public
399	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` /
400	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`
401	of `WeilDivisor.lean` (resolving the fully-qualified-name clash that
402	arose when both files declared the same `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart`
403	at link time). The consumer in `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank`
404	below directly applies the public pin specialised at
405	`t = (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`. -/
406	
407	/-- **The pole divisor of a morphism `φ : C → ℙ¹` of smooth proper curves
408	over `k̄`.** As a Weil divisor on `C`, it is `φ^*[∞]` — the pullback of the
409	divisor `[∞]` on `ℙ¹` along `φ`.
410	
411	**iter-190 substantive body (Pin 2 corrective Option (a) — positive part of
412	principal).** The body is the *positive part* of the principal Weil divisor
413	on `C` of the pulled-back local parameter
414	`algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) (localParameterAtInfty kbar).val`:
415	
416	  `Hom.poleDivisor φ := positivePart (principal (algebraMap K(ℙ¹) K(C) t_∞) _)`
417	
418	Mathematically, `div(φ^* t_∞) = (φ^* t_∞)_0 − (φ^* t_∞)_∞` on `C`, where
419	`(φ^* t_∞)_0 = φ^*[∞]` (the zeros of `φ^* t_∞` are exactly the preimages
420	of `∞`, where `t_∞` vanishes on `ℙ¹`) and `(φ^* t_∞)_∞ = φ^*[0]` (the
421	poles are the preimages of `0`). Extracting the positive part of
422	`div(φ^* t_∞)` exactly recovers `φ^*[∞]` on the nose.
423	
424	**Why the positive-part wrap is essential (Pin 2 structural-conflict
425	diagnosis, iter-189 → iter-190 corrective).** The naked principal divisor
426	`principal (algebraMap _ _ t_∞) halg` has degree zero on a complete
427	nonsingular curve (Hartshorne II.6.10 / Stacks 0BE3,
428	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `WeilDivisor.lean`),
429	whereas the RHS `Module.finrank K(ℙ¹) K(C)` of
430	`Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` is positive (= deg φ ≥ 1) at every
431	call site that uses the φ-induced algebra structure. The positive-part
432	wrap restores provability: `degree (positivePart (principal (φ^# t_∞)))
433	= degree (φ^*[∞]) = deg(φ) = [K(C):K(ℙ¹)]` (Hartshorne~II.6.9).
434	
435	The φ-dependence is carried by the `[Algebra K(ℙ¹) K(C)]` instance
436	binder, which at every call site is the canonical `φ`-induced
437	function-field map (per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Decision 2).
438	Changing the algebra instance changes the divisor; in particular, the
439	unfolding
440	`Hom.poleDivisor φ ⇝ positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t) _)`
441	is visible to definitional unfolding, *not* opaque behind

571	/-- **Degree of a non-constant morphism to `ℙ¹` via its pole divisor**
572	(Hartshorne IV §2 p. 299 opening + II §6.9 multiplicativity of degree
573	under finite pullback).
574	
575	For a non-constant morphism `φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` from a smooth
576	proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an algebraically closed
577	field `k̄`, there exists a positive integer `d` (= `deg(φ)`) together
578	with a Weil divisor `D` on `C` (= the pole divisor `φ^*[∞]` of the
579	pulled-back affine coordinate) of total degree `d`.
580	
581	The non-constancy hypothesis is encoded as `∀ Q, φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q`
582	(no constant morphism `C ⟶ ℙ¹` agrees with `φ`); this is equivalent to
583	`φ` being surjective onto `ℙ¹_{k̄}` since the image is a closed
584	irreducible subset of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` of dimension ≥ 1, and the only such is
585	`ℙ¹_{k̄}` itself.
586	
587	iter-178+ body: a non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves
588	over `k̄` is automatically finite (proper + quasi-finite). The
589	function-field extension `k̄(ℙ¹) ↪ K(C)` is finite of some degree
590	`d = [K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)] ≥ 1`. Take `D := φ^*[∞] ∈ Div(C)` via the standard
591	"pullback of a Weil divisor along a finite morphism" operation; by
592	Hartshorne II.6.9, `deg(D) = deg(φ) · deg([∞]) = d · 1 = d`. The
593	positivity `d ≥ 1` follows from non-constancy (else `deg(φ) = 0` would
594	force `φ` constant). Both the finite-pullback operation and the
595	multiplicativity-of-degree identity are project-bespoke sub-lemmas
596	threaded inside the iter-178+ body (cf. the closely related
597	`Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` of `RR.1`).
598	
599	Blueprint reference: `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` (Hartshorne IV §2
600	opening, p. 299; II §6.9 p. 137 for multiplicativity).
601

603	signature output existential `∃ d D, 0 < d ∧ D.degree = (d : ℤ)` was
604	flagged `weakened-wrong` by the iter-181 `lean-vs-blueprint-checker`:
605	the existential is discharged by ANY positive-degree divisor on `C` and
606	does not reference `φ`. iter-182 strengthens the output to pin `D` to
607	`Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor φ` (a *specific* divisor depending on `φ`) and
608	pin its degree to `Module.finrank (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField
609	C.left.functionField` (the function-field-extension degree `[K(C) : k̄(ℙ¹)]`).
610	The `[Algebra ...]` instance binder pins the intended `Algebra` structure
611	to be the canonical `φ`-induced function-field map (matching the iter-181
612	Pin 3 sig refinement convention). See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin2.md`
613	(verdict PROCEED) for the body recipe via
614	`Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia`. -/

617	    {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
618	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
619	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsLocallyNoetherian C.left]
620	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne C.left]
621	    [IsIntegral (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left]
622	    (φ : C ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar)
623	    (_hφ_non_const :
624	      ∀ Q : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar,
625	        φ ≠ toUnit C ≫ Q)
626	    [Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left.functionField C.left.functionField]

667	/-- **Degree-`1` non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is an
668	isomorphism** (Hartshorne I Corollary 6.12 specialised to degree `1`,
669	or equivalently Stacks tag `0AVX`).
670	
671	For smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves `C, C'` over an
672	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a non-constant morphism
673	`φ : C ⟶ C'` such that the induced `k̄`-algebra map on function fields
674	`φ^# : K(C') → K(C)` makes `K(C)` a one-dimensional `K(C')`-module
675	(equivalently `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`), the morphism `φ` is an
676	isomorphism of `Over (Spec k̄)`-objects.
677	
678	**Signature refinement (iter-181 Lane I).** The degree-`1` hypothesis
679	is encoded as a typeclass binder
680	`[Algebra C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField]` together with
681	the equation `Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1`.
682	The intended `Algebra` instance at every call site is the canonical
683	function-field map induced by `φ` (the composite of
684	`Scheme.Hom.stalkMap` at the generic point with the `IsFractionRing`
685	extension to the fraction-field; see Mathlib
686	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField`). The previous signature took
687	an abstract existence wrapper
688	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)` which is strictly
689	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs: the birational-extension
690	argument requires the iso to be **induced by `φ` itself**, not an
691	arbitrary ring iso. See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
692	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis prompting this refinement.
693	
694	iter-182+ body: invoke the scheme-theoretic argument of Hartshorne's
695	Corollary I.6.12 (Stacks `0AVX`) — equivalently
696	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
697	(see `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Steps 1–4):
698	
699	1. Reduce to `φ` finite (proper + quasi-finite ⟹ finite via
700	   `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`; quasi-finiteness follows
701	   from the degree-`1` hypothesis collapsing each fibre to a single
702	   point at the generic point and then extending by closedness).
703	2. Apply `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` to get
704	   `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is an iso.
705	3. Identify `normalization φ = C'` via smoothness of `C'` (smooth
706	   proper curve over `k̄` ⟹ regular ⟹ normal). The integral
707	   closure `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is then an equality under the
708	   `finrank = 1` hypothesis (rank-`1` torsion-free coherent inclusion
709	   on a Dedekind base is an iso).
710	4. Compose to extract `C ≅ C'` in the slice category.

712	Alternative route (cleaner conceptually but more sheaf API): the
713	pushforward `φ_* O_C` is a coherent `O_{C'}`-module of generic

717	is the structure morphism of an iso.

793	  -- Using it requires:

2	\chapter{The rational-curve isomorphism \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) (RR.4)}
3	\label{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso}
4	\uses{def:genus, lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections, lem:degree_via_pole_divisor, lem:degree_one_morphism_iso, cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero, thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero, def:codim1_cycles, def:divisor_degree}
5	% (read from references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 314)
6	% + IV \S2, opening of "Hurwitz's Theorem" (degree of a finite morphism of
7	% curves is the function-field extension degree; read from PDF page 316)
8	% + I \S6 Corollary~6.12 (function-field \(\Leftrightarrow\) smooth-projective-curve

12	\section{Setup and motivation}
13	This chapter is the fourth and final sub-build chapter
14	\texttt{RR.1}--\texttt{RR.4} closing the project's headline \emph{RR bridge}
15	\[
16	  \mathtt{genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}
17	  \,\colon\,
18	  \text{a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with
19	  \(g(C) = 0\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).}
20	\]
21	The chain assembled across the four sub-build chapters is:
22	\begin{itemize}
23	  \item \texttt{RR.1} (\texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}): the Weil-divisor
24	    group \(\Div(C)\), the degree map
25	    \(\deg \colon \Div(C) \to \mathbb Z\) (\texttt{def:divisor_degree}), and the
26	    degree-zero principal-divisor identity
27	    (\texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero}).
28	  \item \texttt{RR.2} (\texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RRFormula}): the Riemann--Roch
29	    formula in genus zero \(\ell(D) = \deg(D) + 1\) for \(\deg(D) \geq 0\)
30	    (\texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero}).
31	  \item \texttt{RR.3} (sibling chapter, dispatched parallel iter-174):
32	    the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C([P])\), its global-sections identification
33	    \(\dim_{\bar k} H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) = 2\), and the corollary
34	    \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} producing a non-constant
35	    rational function $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
36	    \bar k \cdot 1\( on a genus-\)0\( curve with a marked \)\bar k$-point.
37	  \item \texttt{RR.4} (\emph{this chapter}): the global classification ``a
38	    smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\)
39	    and a \(\bar k\)-rational point is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\).''
40	\end{itemize}
41	This chapter consumes \texttt{RR.3} (the non-constant function) and produces
42	the Lean target \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} pinned at
43	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean} line~290 and restated as
44	the interface block \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} of
45	\texttt{AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex}. The \texttt{RR.4}-closure of that target
46	also drops the \texttt{sorryAx} propagation tagged on
47	\texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme} via
48	\texttt{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}.
49	\paragraph{Scope of the \(\bar k\)-base classification.} The chapter restricts
50	to the case where the base field \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed. Over $\bar
51	k\( every closed point is \)\bar k\(-rational, so the ``\)C\( has a \)\bar
52	k$-rational point'' hypothesis of Hartshorne's Exercise~IV.1.3 is automatic
53	(the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\) is non-empty,
54	hence has closed points, all of which are \(\bar k\)-rational). The general
55	``\(k \to \bar k\) descent'' step ``genus-\(0\) curve over a non-closed \(k\) with
56	a \(k\)-rational point \(\cong \mathbb P^1_k\)'' is a separate sub-build hosted
57	in \texttt{Jacobian.tex} (the \texttt{genusZeroWitness.key} downstream
58	consumer) and is out of scope here.
59	\paragraph{Standing hypotheses.} Throughout this chapter, \(\bar k\) is an
60	algebraically closed field and \(C\) is a smooth proper geometrically
61	irreducible curve over \(\bar k\). The Lean signature
62	(\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) threads the
63	project's standing curve typeclass package:
64	\begin{quote}
65	  \texttt{\{kbar : Type u\} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]\\
66	   (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]\\
67	   \phantom{}\quad[SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]\\
68	   \phantom{}\quad[GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]}
69	\end{quote}
70	matching the typeclass set used by the headline target
71	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} in
72	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290--296}. The genus
73	\(g(C)\) is the one defined in \texttt{def:genus} of \texttt{Genus.tex},
74	namely \(g(C) = \dim_{\bar k} H^1(C, \mathcal O_C)\) via the project's
75	\(\Module \bar k\)-flavoured sheaf cohomology
76	(\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genus}); the concrete projective line target
77	\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is the project's
78	\texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar~kbar} of
79	\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean}.
80	\section{The morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from two global sections}
81	\begin{lemma}
82	
83	\leanok
84	[Morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) from a globally generating pair of sections]
85	  \label{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}
86	  
87	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}
88	  Let \(X\) be a scheme over a field \(\bar k\), let \(\mathcal L\) be an invertible
89	  sheaf on \(X\), and let \(s_0, s_1 \in H^0(X, \mathcal L)\) be two global
90	  sections whose common zero locus is empty:
91	  \[
92	    \{x \in X : s_0(x) = 0 \text{ and } s_1(x) = 0\} \;=\; \emptyset.
93	  \]
94	  (Equivalently, \(s_0\) and \(s_1\) generate \(\mathcal L\) at every point.) Then
95	  there exists a unique morphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
96	  \[
97	    \varphi_{(s_0, s_1)} \colon X \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
98	  \]
99	  whose pullback of \(\mathcal O_{\mathbb P^1}(1)\) is \(\mathcal L\) and whose
100	  pullbacks of the standard coordinate sections $x_0, x_1 \in
101	  H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal O(1))\( are \)s_0\( and \)s_1$ respectively. On the
102	  basic-open chart $D_+(x_0) = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k} \subset
103	  \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\( the morphism \)\varphi_{(s_0, s_1)}\( is the map ``\)x
104	  \mapsto s_1(x) / s_0(x)\(''; on the chart \)D_+(x_1)\( it is the map ``\)x
105	  \mapsto s_0(x) / s_1(x)$''; the two definitions agree on the overlap
106	  \(D_+(x_0) \cap D_+(x_1) = \mathbb G_{m, \bar k}\) by the cocycle identity
107	  \((s_1 / s_0) \cdot (s_0 / s_1) = 1\).
108	\end{lemma}
109	\begin{proof}
110	  This is the standard linear-system construction in the form of
111	  Hartshorne's Theorem~II.7.1 (``Morphisms to \(\mathbb P^n\)''), specialised to
112	  \(n = 1\). Mathlib (snapshot \texttt{b80f227}) ships the corresponding API
113	  under the name
114	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} in
115	  \texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}:
116	  \begin{quote}
117	  \small
118	  \texttt{def Proj.fromOfGlobalSections \{\(\sigma\) A\} [CommRing A]
119	    [SetLike \(\sigma\) A] [AddSubgroupClass \(\sigma\) A] (\(\mathcal A\) :
120	    \(\mathbb N\) → \(\sigma\)) [GradedRing \(\mathcal A\)] \{X : Scheme\}\\
121	    \phantom{}\quad(f : A →+* X.presheaf.obj \(\top\))\\
122	    \phantom{}\quad(hf : Ideal.map f (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant \(\mathcal A\)).toIdeal = \(\top\)) :\\
123	    \phantom{}\quad(X \(\longrightarrow\) Proj \(\mathcal A\))}
124	  \end{quote}
125	  Specialising to \(\mathcal A = \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) with the standard $\mathbb
126	  N\(-grading by total degree, the inputs are a graded \)\bar k$-algebra
127	  homomorphism \(f \colon \bar k[x_0, x_1] \to H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) and a
128	  proof that the irrelevant ideal \((x_0, x_1) \cdot \bar k[x_0, x_1]\) maps to
129	  the unit ideal of \(H^0(X, \mathcal O_X)\) (i.e.\ no common zeros). The
130	  resulting morphism lands in $\mathrm{Proj}\,\bar k[x_0, x_1] = \mathbb
131	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (Hartshorne~II, Example~7.1.3, the construction of \)\mathbb
132	  P^n\( as a \)\mathrm{Proj}\(). When \)\mathcal L$ is non-trivial the present
133	  lemma's signature is reduced to the Mathlib API by working on the open
134	  cover \(X = D(s_0) \cup D(s_1)\), where on each chart the sections become
135	  honest global functions (\(s_1 / s_0\) on \(D(s_0)\), \(s_0 / s_1\) on \(D(s_1)\),
136	  agreeing on the overlap). The chart-glue is performed by the
137	  project-bespoke wrapper
138	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}, whose body
139	  in \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} chains
140	  \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} with the chart-restriction-and-glue
141	  pattern of \texttt{Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms}.
142	  Uniqueness follows from the standard separation argument for morphisms to
143	  a separated scheme: two morphisms \(\varphi, \psi \colon X \to \mathbb P^1\)
144	  with \(\varphi^* x_i = \psi^* x_i\) for \(i = 0, 1\) agree on the basic-open
145	  charts \(D_+(x_0), D_+(x_1)\) (where they pull back the affine coordinates to
146	  the same rational function), and these charts cover \(\mathbb P^1\), so the
147	  two morphisms agree globally by gluing.
148	\end{proof}
149	\section{Degree of a morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via its pole divisor}
150	% NOTE (iter-190 plan-phase Pin 2 corrective; supersedes iter-182 narrative).
151	% `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor \varphi` is the Weil divisor \(\varphi^*[\infty]\),
152	% defined as `positivePart (principal (\varphi^{\#} t_\infty))` in Lean.
153	% The supporting `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` def lives in
154	% `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` (\ref{def:WeilDivisor_positivePart}); the
155	% Hartshorne~II.6.9 identity that powers the existential degree statement
156	% lives there as \ref{lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank}.
157	\begin{lemma}
158	
159	\leanok
160	[Degree of a non-constant morphism to \(\mathbb P^1\) via the preimage of \(\infty\)]
161	  \label{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor}
162	  
163	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor}
164	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], IV \S2, opening paragraph of "Hurwitz's Theorem"
165	  % (definition of the degree of a finite morphism of curves) (read from
166	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
167	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
168	  % \(f: X \to Y\) of curves is defined to be the degree \([K(X):K(Y)]\) of the
169	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
170	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.2, p.~299, opening of \S2.}
171	  Let \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) be a non-constant morphism
172	  from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C / \bar k\). Then
173	  \(\varphi\) is finite, its function-field extension
174	  \(\bar k(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)\) (the canonical
175	  \(\varphi\)-induced map, supplied as an
176	  \texttt{[Algebra (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField C.functionField]}
177	  instance argument on the Lean side) is finite, and there exists a Weil
178	  divisor \(D \in \Div(C)\) such that
179	  \[
180	    D \;=\; \varphi^*[\infty]
181	    \quad\text{and}\quad
182	    \deg(D) \;=\; [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,].
183	  \]
184	  Equivalently, the morphism \(\varphi\) satisfies
185	  \[
186	    [\,K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)\,] \;=\;
187	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
188	                  \ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [P]\,\right),
189	  \]
190	  where \(t_\infty \in K(\mathbb P^1)\) is a local parameter at \(\infty\)
191	  (equivalently \(t_\infty = 1/u\) for the standard affine coordinate \(u\) on
192	  \(\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\)); and for any closed point
193	  \(Q \in \mathbb P^1\) the same identification gives
194	  \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C):\bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\).
195	  \emph{Convention on the pole divisor.} The Weil divisor
196	  \(\varphi^*[\infty]\) is exposed in Lean as
197	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor \(\varphi\)}, a
198	  noncomputable definition introduced (as a typed \texttt{sorry}) at the
199	  iter-182 plan-phase signature strengthening of this lemma. The body of
200	  \texttt{poleDivisor} is queued for iter-183+ closure via the standard
201	  affine-chart construction (\texttt{Ideal.sum\_ramification\_inertia}
202	  in Mathlib, applied at each closed point \(P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)\)
203	  to read off the local ramification multiplicity
204	  \(\ord_P(\varphi^* t_\infty)\) from the maximal ideal at \(\infty\) in
205	  \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)). On the Lean side, the existential output of
206	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} packages
207	  \(D = \texttt{Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor}\;\varphi\) together with
208	  \(\deg(D) = \operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(\mathbb P^1)} K(C)\),
209	  mirroring the OCofP pattern in which a typed-sorry def
210	  (\texttt{def:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_toFunctionField}) is introduced
211	  upstream of its consumer lemma.
212	\end{lemma}
213	% NOTE (iter-190 plan-phase, supersedes iter-189 review NOTE):
214	% Pin 2 corrective COMMITTED — Option (a). `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` is
215	% being refactored to return the *positive part* of the principal divisor
216	% of `\varphi^{\#} t_\infty`, i.e. `(div(\varphi^{\#} t_\infty))_0
217	% = \varphi^*[\infty]`. The supporting Weil-divisor `positivePart`
218	% definition lives in `RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` as
219	% `def:WeilDivisor_positivePart`; the substantive Hartshorne~II.6.9
220	% identity that powers `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` is now pinned as
221	% `lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` in `WeilDivisor.tex`.
222	% With these substrates in place, `lem:degree_via_pole_divisor` is
223	% mathematically correct as stated: the body's existential witness `D`
224	% is `Hom.poleDivisor \varphi = positivePart (principal (\varphi^{\#}
225	% t_\infty))`, and the degree identity is a one-line corollary of
226	% `lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`. iter-190 prover lane
227	% (Lane I) implements the refactor:
228	%   1. New def `Scheme.WeilDivisor.positivePart` in `WeilDivisor.lean`
229	%      (~10-15 LOC; lattice `D \sqcup 0`).
230	%   2. New lemma
231	%      `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank` as a
232	%      named typed-sorry pin in `WeilDivisor.lean` (~5 LOC signature; body
233	%      ~50-80 LOC via affine-chart `Ideal.sum_ramification_inertia` route
234	%      owed iter-191+).
235	%   3. Refactor `Scheme.Hom.poleDivisor` body in `RationalCurveIso.lean`
236	%      from `principal (algebraMap _ _ t.val) halg` to
237	%      `positivePart (principal (algebraMap _ _ t.val) halg)` (~5 LOC).
238	%   4. Close `Hom.poleDivisor_degree_eq_finrank` body via a 1-line
239	%      rewrite consuming (2) (~3 LOC).
240	% Net iter-190 Lane I: 1 axiom-clean closure (Pin 2 body, modulo named
241	% typed sorry on (2)); 1 NEW named typed-sorry pin
242	% (`degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank`) which is itself an
243	% iter-191+ Hartshorne II.6.9 substrate target. See iter-190 sidecar
244	% `iter/iter-190/plan.md` for the full rationale.
245	\begin{proof}
246	  \uses{def:WeilDivisor_positivePart, lem:degree_positivePart_principal_eq_finrank, def:principal_divisor, def:divisor_degree, thm:principal_deg_zero}
247	  A non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves over \(\bar k\) is
248	  automatically finite. Indeed, the image of \(\varphi\) is a closed
249	  irreducible subset of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) that is not a point (else
250	  \(\varphi\) would be constant) and not empty, so it is all of $\mathbb
251	  P^1_{\bar k}\( (the only proper irreducible closed subset of \)\mathbb
252	  P^1_{\bar k}\( is a point); hence \)\varphi$ is surjective. A surjective
253	  morphism between smooth proper irreducible curves of equal dimension is
254	  finite (proper + quasi-finite, where quasi-finiteness comes from the
255	  fact that \(\varphi\) has zero-dimensional fibres: a positive-dimensional
256	  fibre would force a one-dimensional irreducible component in the fibre,
257	  contradicting irreducibility of \(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) and \(C\) at
258	  generic points). Hence the function-field extension
259	  \(\bar k(\mathbb P^1) = \varphi^\# K(\mathbb P^1) \hookrightarrow K(C)\)
260	  is finite.
261	  The identification \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\) for
262	  any closed point \(Q \in \mathbb P^1\) is the multiplicativity of the
263	  degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9): for a finite morphism
264	  \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) of smooth proper curves and any divisor $D \in
265	  \Div(C')\(, \)\deg(\varphi^* D) = \deg(\varphi) \cdot \deg(D)$. Specialising
266	  to \(D = [Q]\) (a divisor of degree \(1\) since \(\kappa(Q) = \bar k\)) gives
267	  \(\deg(\varphi^*[Q]) = \deg(\varphi)\). Equivalently, the degree of
268	  \(\varphi\) equals the number of preimages of any closed point of $\mathbb
269	  P^1\(, counted with the ramification multiplicities \)\ord_P(\varphi^*
270	  t_\infty)\( at the preimage points \)P \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)$.
271	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The lemma threads two auxiliary inputs:
272	  ``non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is finite'' (the
273	  surjective-and-proper-and-quasi-finite argument above; Hartshorne~II.6.8)
274	  and ``multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback'' (Hartshorne~II.6.9
275	  and the multiplicativity of \(\deg\) under composition of \(\div\) and
276	  \(\deg\); the closely related ``\(\mathrm{div}(f)\) has degree zero'' specialisation
277	  is \texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}). Both are separable
278	  sub-lemmas inside \texttt{RR.4}'s Lean file
279	  (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean}) and are
280	  slotted for closure in a follow-up iteration after the
281	  \texttt{morphism\_degree\_via\_pole\_divisor} skeleton lands.
282	\end{proof}
283	\section{A degree-\(1\) morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism}
284	% NOTE (iter-181 Lane I signature refinement; see analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md
285	% Decision 2, verdict DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY). The iter-177 file-skeleton
286	% placeholder hypothesis "Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)"
287	% (an abstract function-field iso, unrelated to \(\varphi\)) was strictly
288	% weaker than the birational-extension body needs and was refined in
289	% iter-181 to the explicit function-field-extension-degree formulation
290	% [K(C):K(C')] = 1, i.e. Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField
291	% = 1, with C.functionField a C'.functionField-algebra via the canonical
292	% \varphi-induced map (Scheme.Hom.stalkMap at the generic point composed
293	% with IsFractionRing.lift). The proof-prose body below (the Hartshorne
294	% I.6.12 chain + scheme-theoretic alternative argument) is unchanged ---
295	% only the input shape changes.
296	\begin{lemma}
297	
298	\leanok
299	[Degree-\(1\) morphism between smooth proper curves is an isomorphism]
300	  \label{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso}
301	  
302	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso_of_degree_one}
303	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], I \S6 Corollary~6.12 (function-field-to-curve
304	  % equivalence of categories) (read from
305	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 62, doc p.~45)
306	  % + [Hartshorne], IV \S2 opening paragraph (degree of a finite morphism
307	  % of curves is the function-field extension degree) (read from
308	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF page 316, doc p.~299).
309	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Corollary 6.12. \emph{The following three categories are
310	  % equivalent:}
311	  % (i) \emph{nonsingular projective curves, and dominant morphisms;}
312	  % (ii) \emph{quasi-projective curves, and dominant rational maps;}
313	  % (iii) \emph{function fields of dimension 1 over \(k\), and \(k\)-homomorphisms.}"
314	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Recall that the \emph{degree} of a finite morphism
315	  % \(f: X \to Y\) of curves is defined to be the degree \([K(X):K(Y)]\) of the
316	  % extension of function fields (II, \S 6)."
317	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, I.6 Corollary~6.12 (p.~45) + IV.2 opening
318	  (p.~299).}
319	  Let \(C, C'\) be smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves over an
320	  algebraically closed field \(\bar k\), and let \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) be
321	  a non-constant morphism whose induced function-field extension is of
322	  degree one,
323	  \[
324	    [\,K(C) : K(C')\,] \;=\; 1,
325	  \]
326	  formally
327	  \(\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{K(C')} K(C) = 1\) in the Lean signature.
328	  Then \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes.
329	  \emph{Convention on the function-field map.} At each call-site of this
330	  lemma, the \(K(C')\)-algebra structure on \(K(C)\) used to interpret the
331	  degree \([K(C) : K(C')]\) is the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced
332	  function-field map, i.e.\ the composite
333	  \[
334	    K(C') \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{germ}\;}\;
335	    \mathcal O_{C', \varphi(\xi_C)}
336	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\varphi^\#\;}\;
337	    \mathcal O_{C, \xi_C}
338	    \;\xrightarrow{\;\text{frac.}\,\text{lift}\;}\; K(C)
339	  \]
340	  of the germ at the image of the generic point \(\xi_C \in C\) with the
341	  stalk-map of \(\varphi\) at \(\xi_C\) and the fraction-field lift at
342	  \(\xi_C\) (per \texttt{analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md}, Decision~2). On
343	  the Lean side this canonical instance is carried as a
344	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
345	  to \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one}; consumers
346	  supply it explicitly at each call-site.
347	\end{lemma}
348	% NOTE (iter-189 review): Pin 3 Step 1 (function-field iso K(C') ≃+* K(C)
349	% from `Module.finrank = 1`) closed axiom-clean inline iter-189 (~10 LOC via
350	% `Subalgebra.bot_eq_top_of_finrank_eq_one` + `Algebra.surjective_algebraMap_iff`
351	% + `RingHom.injective` + `RingEquiv.ofBijective`). Step 2 (scheme-level lift
352	% via `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization`) remains a typed sorry in the body, with 4
353	% sub-obligations (a)-(d) documented inline at the sorry. Iter-190+ closure
354	% budget ~80-150 LOC per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md`.
355	\begin{proof}
356	  The function-field hypothesis \([K(C) : K(C')] = 1\) states that the
357	  canonical \(\varphi\)-induced map \(\varphi^\# \colon K(C') \to K(C)\) is a
358	  finite extension of degree one; since \(K(C')\) is a field and the
359	  extension has dimension one as a \(K(C')\)-vector space, \(\varphi^\#\) is
360	  an isomorphism of function fields \(K(C') \cong K(C)\). (Equivalently, by
361	  the IV.2-opening identification \(\deg(\varphi) = [K(C):K(C')]\), this is
362	  the statement \(\deg(\varphi) = 1\); the morphism \(\varphi\) is finite in
363	  this case by the standard argument that a non-constant morphism of
364	  smooth proper curves is finite, identical to the
365	  \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} preamble.)
366	  By Hartshorne's Corollary~I.6.12, the assignment \(C \mapsto \bar k(C)\) is
367	  an equivalence of categories between smooth projective curves over $\bar
368	  k$ (with dominant morphisms) and function fields of transcendence degree
369	  one over \(\bar k\) (with \(\bar k\)-homomorphisms). Under this equivalence,
370	  the isomorphism \(\bar k(C') \cong \bar k(C)\) produced by the previous
371	  paragraph lifts to a unique isomorphism of smooth proper curves $C \to
372	  C'\(, and this isomorphism agrees with \)\varphi$ on the generic point (by
373	  construction: it is the inverse-image of \(\varphi^\#\) under the
374	  equivalence). The two morphisms \(C \to C'\) that agree on the generic
375	  point of the integral scheme \(C\) agree everywhere (a morphism out of an
376	  integral scheme to a separated scheme is determined by its restriction
377	  to the generic point, Hartshorne~II Ex.~4.2). Hence \(\varphi\) is itself
378	  the lift, and is therefore an isomorphism.
379	  \emph{Alternative direct argument.} Without invoking the
380	  equivalence-of-categories framing, the same conclusion is obtained
381	  scheme-theoretically: a finite degree-\(1\) morphism $\varphi \colon C \to
382	  C'$ between integral normal noetherian schemes is an isomorphism.
383	  Indeed, the direct image \(\varphi_* \mathcal O_C\) is a coherent
384	  \(\mathcal O_{C'}\)-module that is generically of rank $\deg(\varphi) =
385	  1\(, and an inclusion \)\mathcal O_{C'} \hookrightarrow \varphi_*
386	  \mathcal O_C\( of coherent rank-\)1$ sheaves whose generic ranks agree
387	  is an isomorphism over the locus where the quotient
388	  \(\varphi_* \mathcal O_C / \mathcal O_{C'}\) has trivial stalk. The
389	  quotient is a torsion coherent \(\mathcal O_{C'}\)-module of generic rank
390	  \(\deg(\varphi) - 1 = 0\), hence is itself torsion-free (a
391	  finitely-generated torsion module over a Dedekind domain of generic
392	  rank zero is zero), so vanishes. Therefore $\varphi_* \mathcal O_C =
393	  \mathcal O_{C'}\( and \)\varphi$ is the structure morphism of an
394	  isomorphism. Cf.\ Stacks tag~\textbf{0AVX} for the categorical version.
395	  \emph{Sub-build note.} The Lean signature
396	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.iso\_of\_degree\_one} consumes a
397	  non-constant morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to C'\) together with a
398	  \texttt{[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]} instance argument
399	  (the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced function-field map) and the
400	  function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis
401	  \texttt{Module.finrank C'.functionField C.functionField = 1}, and
402	  produces an isomorphism \(C \cong C'\) in the slice category
403	  \(\mathtt{Over (Spec (.of\ kbar))}\). The proof body threads the Mathlib
404	  API \texttt{IsIntegralHom.iso\_of\_isUnit} (or the equivalent
405	  \texttt{IsAffineHom + Finite + RankOne} chain) and, where needed, the
406	  project-bespoke ``finite-morphism-of-degree-one is an isomorphism''
407	  helper. The Hartshorne~I.6.12 equivalence-of-categories framing is the
408	  intended high-level proof structure; the scheme-theoretic argument
409	  above is the spelled-out form for the Lean body.
410	\end{proof}
411	\section{Headline: genus-\(0\) curve over \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\)}
412	\begin{theorem}
413	
414	\leanok
415	[Genus-\(0\) curve over \(\bar k\) is isomorphic to \(\mathbb P^1\)]
416	  \label{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}
417	  
418	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
419	  % already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) (I, Ex. 7.2), so conversely
420	  % suppose given a curve \(X\) of genus 0. Let \(P, Q\) be two distinct
421	  % points of \(X\) and apply Riemann-Roch to the divisor \(D = P - Q\).
422	  % Since \(\deg(K - D) = -2\), using (1.3.3) above, we have \(l(K - D) = 0\),
423	  % and so we find \(l(D) = 1\). But \(D\) is a divisor of degree 0, so by
424	  % (1.2) we have \(D \sim 0\), in other words \(P \sim Q\). But this implies
425	  % \(X\) is rational (II, 6.10.1)."
426	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, IV.1, Example~1.3.5, p.~297.}
427	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, and let \(C\) be a smooth
428	  proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\) with \(g(C) = 0\).
429	  Then there exists an isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes
430	  \[
431	    \varphi \colon C \stackrel{\sim}{\longrightarrow} \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}.
432	  \]
433	  Concretely, the Lean signature
434	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1} (the existing pinned
435	  target at \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:290}) is
436	  closed by the proof below; the existing interface block
437	  \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} of
438	  \texttt{chap:avr_for_rr} is its consumer-side restatement,
439	  pinning the same Lean declaration.
440	\end{theorem}
441	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the verbatim Example IV.1.3.5 text in the
442	% statement block above (Hartshorne states the classification and its
443	% proof inside the single Example; the proof is the second half beginning
444	% "We already know that \(p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0\) \ldots"). The present
445	% proof body restates Hartshorne's argument in the project's notation
446	% (linear-system construction \(C \to \mathbb P^1\) via $H^0(C, \mathcal O_C
447	% ([P]))\(, degree-\)1$ identification, isomorphism).
448	\begin{proof}
449	  Over the algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) the smooth proper
450	  geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) has closed points, all of which are
451	  \(\bar k\)-rational (the residue field of a closed point of a curve over
452	  \(\bar k\) is a finite extension of \(\bar k\), hence is \(\bar k\) itself by
453	  algebraic closedness). Fix one such \(\bar k\)-rational point \(P \in C\);
454	  this is the marked point used to build the linear system below.
455	  \emph{Step 1: non-constant function \(f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\).}
456	  By Hartshorne's Riemann--Roch in genus zero
457	  (\texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}) applied to the
458	  effective divisor \([P]\) of degree \(1\), we obtain
459	  \(\ell([P]) = \deg([P]) + 1 = 2\). The constant section
460	  \(1 \in \bar k \cdot 1 \subset H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) accounts for a
461	  one-dimensional \(\bar k\)-subspace; the two-dimensional quotient supplies
462	  a non-constant section $f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P])) \setminus
463	  \bar k \cdot 1$. This is the corollary
464	  \texttt{cor:nonconstant\_function\_genus\_zero} of \texttt{RR.3} (sibling
465	  chapter, \texttt{RiemannRoch\_OCofP.tex}).
466	  \emph{Step 2: morphism \(C \to \mathbb P^1\) via the linear system \((1, f)\).}
467	  The pair \((1, f) \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))^2\) generates the
468	  invertible sheaf \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) at every point of \(C\): at points
469	  \(Q \in C \setminus \{P\}\) the section \(1\) does not vanish in
470	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])_Q \cong \mathcal O_{C, Q}\), and at the marked point
471	  \(P\) the section \(f\) does not vanish in \(\mathcal O_C([P])_P\) (because \(f\)
472	  has a pole of order exactly \(1\) at \(P\) in the rational-function
473	  picture: \(f \in K(C) \setminus \bar k\) with \(\mathrm{div}(f)_\infty = [P]\)).
474	  Therefore by \texttt{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections} there
475	  exists a unique morphism
476	  \[
477	    \varphi \;:=\; \varphi_{(1, f)} \colon C \longrightarrow \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}
478	  \]
479	  with \(\varphi^* x_0 = 1\) and \(\varphi^* x_1 = f\) (so the pullback of
480	  the standard ratio coordinate \(u = x_1 / x_0\) on $\mathbb P^1 \setminus
481	  \{\infty\}\( is \)f$, and the pullback of the inverse coordinate
482	  \(t_\infty = x_0 / x_1 = 1 / f\) is the local parameter \(1/f\) at \(P\)).
483	  \emph{Step 3: \(\deg(\varphi) = 1\) via the pole divisor of \(f\).} The
484	  morphism \(\varphi\) is non-constant (since \(f\) is non-constant as a
485	  rational function on \(C\), the pullback \(\varphi^* u = f\) takes
486	  infinitely many values, ruling out a constant \(\varphi\)). By
487	  \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor} applied to \(\varphi\),
488	  \[
489	    \deg(\varphi)
490	    \;=\;
491	    \deg\!\left(\,\sum_{Q \in \varphi^{-1}(\infty)}
492	                  \ord_Q(\varphi^* t_\infty) \cdot [Q]\,\right).
493	  \]
494	  We compute the pole divisor of \(f\) as a rational function on \(C\):
495	  since \(f \in H^0(C, \mathcal O_C([P]))\) with \(f\) non-constant and
496	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) the line bundle of the effective divisor \([P]\), the
497	  rational-function \(f\) has pole divisor \((f)_\infty = [P]\) (the
498	  unique pole of order \(1\) at \(P\), no other poles; this is the standard
499	  identification of \(H^0(C, \mathcal O_C(D))\) with the rational functions
500	  \(g \in K(C)\) satisfying \(\mathrm{div}(g) + D \geq 0\), specialised to
501	  \(D = [P]\)). Hence \(\varphi^{-1}(\infty) = \{P\}\) with multiplicity~\(1\)
502	  and
503	  \[
504	    \deg(\varphi) \;=\; \deg([P]) \;=\; 1.
505	  \]
506	  \emph{Step 4: \(\varphi\) is an isomorphism.} By Hartshorne's IV.2
507	  identification \(\deg(\varphi) = [K(C) : \bar k(\mathbb P^1)]\), the
508	  degree-one conclusion of Step~3 rewrites as
509	  \(\operatorname{Module.finrank}_{\bar k(\mathbb P^1)} K(C) = 1\) (with
510	  the canonical \(\varphi\)-induced
511	  \texttt{[Algebra \,(ProjectiveLineBar kbar).functionField\,
512	   C.functionField]} instance). By
513	  \texttt{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} applied to the non-constant
514	  morphism \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) (smooth proper
515	  geometrically irreducible curves over \(\bar k\) on both sides), under
516	  this function-field-extension-degree-one hypothesis, \(\varphi\) is an
517	  isomorphism of \(\bar k\)-schemes.
518	  This produces the required isomorphism \(C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\)
519	  closing the headline target
520	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero\_curve\_iso\_P1}.
521	  \emph{Comparison with Hartshorne's original argument.} Hartshorne's
522	  Example~IV.1.3.5 phrases the argument in terms of two distinct points
523	  \(P, Q \in C\) and the degree-zero divisor \(D = P - Q\), deriving \(P \sim Q\)
524	  and concluding via II.6.10.1 that \(C\) is rational; combined with I.6.12
525	  this yields \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\). The present proof restates the same
526	  combinatorial content via the more directly formalisable linear-system
527	  construction: instead of going through the \(P \sim Q\) statement and
528	  invoking II.6.10.1 as a black box, we produce the explicit morphism
529	  \(\varphi \colon C \to \mathbb P^1\) from the two sections \((1, f)\) of
530	  \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) given by Riemann--Roch and identify its degree
531	  directly. The two arguments are mathematically equivalent and have the
532	  same dependency footprint (Riemann--Roch in genus zero \(\Rightarrow\)
533	  \(\ell([P]) = 2\) \(\Rightarrow\) non-constant \(f\) \(\Rightarrow\) degree-\(1\)
534	  map to \(\mathbb P^1\) \(\Rightarrow\) iso); the linear-system phrasing maps
535	  more cleanly onto the Mathlib \texttt{Proj.fromOfGlobalSections} API and
536	  the project's existing Weil-divisor/degree pipeline of \texttt{RR.1}.
537	\end{proof}
538	\begin{remark}

552	\section{Out of scope}
553	The following items are deliberately \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.4} and
554	are sequenced elsewhere:
555	\begin{itemize}
556	  \item The general ``smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve \(C\) of
557	    genus \(0\) over a \emph{non-closed} field \(k\) with a \(k\)-rational point
558	    \(\cong \mathbb P^1_k\)'' statement is the \(\bar k \to k\) descent of
559	    \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso}. The descent is hosted in
560	    \texttt{Jacobian.tex} via \texttt{genusZeroWitness.key}; it consumes
561	    \texttt{chap:RiemannRoch_RationalCurveIso} together with Galois descent for
562	    smooth proper curves and is out of scope for \texttt{RR.4}.
563	  \item The Mathlib API \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections}
564	    (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic}) is
565	    consumed as a black box by
566	    \texttt{lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections}; its own proof inside
567	    Mathlib (the universal property of \(\mathrm{Proj}\), graded-ring
568	    presentation of \(\mathbb P^n\)) is not re-derived here.
569	  \item Multiplicativity of degree under finite pullback (Hartshorne~II.6.9)
570	    is consumed as a black box by \texttt{lem:degree_via_pole_divisor};
571	    its proof is a separable sub-lemma threaded inside
572	    \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/RationalCurveIso.lean} and is
573	    \emph{not} part of \texttt{RR.4}'s blueprint statement (it lives at
574	    the same logical level as the closely related
575	    \texttt{thm:principal_deg_zero} of \texttt{RR.1}).
576	  \item The full equivalence of categories ``smooth projective curves over
577	    \(\bar k\) with dominant morphisms'' \(\cong\) ``function fields of
578	    transcendence degree~\(1\) over \(\bar k\) with \(\bar k\)-homomorphisms''
579	    (Hartshorne~I.6.12) is consumed as a high-level framing in
580	    \texttt{lem:degree_one_morphism_iso} but is \emph{not} re-formalised at
581	    the equivalence-of-categories level here. The proof body in the Lean
582	    file threads the spelled-out scheme-theoretic ``finite-degree-one
583	    morphism between integral normal schemes is an isomorphism''
584	    argument (Stacks~\textbf{0AVX}), which is the minimal-Mathlib-dependence
585	    pin compatible with the chapter's downstream needs.
586	  \item ``A smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over \(\bar k\)
587	    has at least two distinct \(\bar k\)-points'' (the non-empty / infinite
588	    cardinality of \(C(\bar k)\)) is consumed implicitly by the present
589	    chapter but its formal statement lives in the project's curve API
590	    (\texttt{Genus.tex} / \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian.Genus}); the chapter
591	    does not restate it.
592	  \item Cohomology of the line bundle \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) on a curve
593	    (Hartshorne~III.5 + IV.1) is fully consumed via \texttt{RR.3}; in
594	    particular the \(H^1\)-vanishing of \(\mathcal O_C([P])\) on a genus-\(0\)
595	    curve is part of \texttt{RR.3}'s payload feeding
596	    \texttt{thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero} of \texttt{RR.2}, and is not
597	    re-derived here.
598	\end{itemize}

1	/-

4	Authors: Andrew Yang
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Sites.SmallAffineZariski
9	public import Mathlib.Tactic.DepRewrite
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Integral
11	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Morphisms.Smooth
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Smooth.IntegralClosure
13	
14	/-!
15	# Relative Normalization
16	
17	Given a qcqs morphism `f : X ⟶ Y`, we define the relative normalization `f.normalization`,
18	along with the maps that `f` factor into:
19	- `f.toNormalization : X ⟶ f.normalization`: a dominant morphism
20	- `f.fromNormalization : f.normalization ⟶ Y`: an integral morphism
21	
22	It satisfies the universal property:
23	For any factorization `X ⟶ T ⟶ Y` with `T ⟶ Y` integral,
24	the map `X ⟶ T` factors through `f.normalization` uniquely.
25	The factorization map is `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.normalizationDesc`, and the uniqueness result
26	is `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.normalization.hom_ext`.
27	
28	We also show that normalization commutes with disjoint unions and smooth base change.
29	
30	-/
31	
32	@[expose] public noncomputable section
33	
34	open CategoryTheory Limits
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom
37	
38	universe u
39	
40	variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)
41	
42	open AffineZariskiSite
43	
44	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
45	/-- Given a morphism `f : X ⟶ Y`, this is the presheaf of integral closure of `Y` in `X`. -/
46	def normalizationDiagram : Y.Opensᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat where
47	  obj U :=
48	    letI := (f.app U.unop).hom.toAlgebra
49	    .of (integralClosure Γ(Y, U.unop) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U.unop))
50	  map {V U} i :=
51	    CommRingCat.ofHom ((X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.preimage_mono i.unop.le)).op).hom.restrict
52	      _ _ fun x hx ↦ by
53	      obtain ⟨U, rfl⟩ := Opposite.op_surjective U
54	      obtain ⟨V, rfl⟩ := Opposite.op_surjective V
55	      algebraize [(f.app U).hom, (f.app V).hom, (Y.presheaf.map i).hom,
56	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.preimage_mono i.unop.le)).op).hom,
57	        (f.appLE V (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) (f.preimage_mono i.unop.le)).hom]
58	      have : IsScalarTower Γ(Y, V) Γ(Y, U) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := .of_algebraMap_eq' <| by
59	        simp [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra, ← CommRingCat.hom_comp]; rfl
60	      have : IsScalarTower Γ(Y, V) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ V) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := .of_algebraMap_eq' rfl
61	      exact (hx.map (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom Γ(Y, V) _ Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U))).tower_top)
62	  map_id U := by simp; rfl
63	  map_comp i j := by
64	    simp only [← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
65	    rw [← homOfLE_comp (f.preimage_mono j.unop.le) (f.preimage_mono i.unop.le), op_comp]
66	    simp_rw [X.presheaf.map_comp]
67	    rfl
68	
69	/-- The inclusion from the structure presheaf of `Y` to the integral closure of `Y` in `X`. -/
70	def normalizationDiagramMap : Y.presheaf ⟶ f.normalizationDiagram where
71	  app U :=
72	    letI := (f.app U.unop).hom.toAlgebra
73	    CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap Γ(Y, U.unop) (integralClosure Γ(Y, U.unop) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U.unop)))
74	  naturality {U V} i := by ext x; exact Subtype.ext congr($(f.naturality i) x)
75	
76	variable [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f]
77	
78	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
79	lemma coequifibered_normalizationDiagramMap :
80	    ((toOpensFunctor Y).op.whiskerLeft f.normalizationDiagramMap).Coequifibered := by
81	  refine coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway.mpr fun U r ↦ ?_
82	  let : Algebra Γ(Y, U.1) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) := (f.app U.1).hom.toAlgebra
83	  let : Algebra Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ Y.basicOpen r) :=
84	    (f.app (U.basicOpen r).1).hom.toAlgebra
85	  let : Algebra (integralClosure Γ(Y, U.1) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))
86	      (integralClosure Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ Y.basicOpen r)) :=
87	    ((normalizationDiagram f).map (homOfLE (Y.basicOpen_le r)).op).hom.toAlgebra
88	  let inst : Algebra Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ Y.basicOpen r) :=
89	    (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.preimage_mono (Y.basicOpen_le r))).op).hom.toAlgebra
90	  have : IsLocalization.Away r Γ(Y, Y.basicOpen r) :=
91	    U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen _
92	  have : IsLocalization.Away ((algebraMap ↑Γ(Y, U.1) ↑Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U.1)) r)
93	      Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ Y.basicOpen r) := by
94	    let : Algebra Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen (f.app _ r)) :=
95	      (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le _)).op).hom.toAlgebra
96	    dsimp +instances [inst]
97	    rw! (castMode := .all) [f.preimage_basicOpen r]
98	    exact isLocalization_basicOpen_of_qcqs (f.isCompact_preimage U.2.isCompact)
99	        (f.isQuasiSeparated_preimage U.2.isQuasiSeparated) (f.app _ r)
100	  change IsLocalization.Away ((algebraMap Γ(Y, U.1) (integralClosure Γ(Y, U.1) Γ(X, f ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))) r)

19:- `f.toNormalization : X ⟶ f.normalization`: a dominant morphism
136:def toNormalization : X ⟶ f.normalization :=
158:lemma ι_toNormalization (U : Y.affineOpens) :
160:    (f ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι ≫ f.toNormalization = (f ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toSpecΓ ≫
164:  trans ((directedCover Y).pullback₁ f).f U ≫ f.toNormalization
166:  delta toNormalization
186:lemma toNormalization_fromNormalization :
187:    f.toNormalization ≫ f.fromNormalization = f := by
190:  refine (f.ι_toNormalization_assoc _ _).trans ?_
224:lemma toNormalization_app_preimage (U : Y.affineOpens) :
226:    dsimp% f.toNormalization.app (f.fromNormalization ⁻¹ᵁ ↑U) =
237:  have H : f.toNormalization ⁻¹ᵁ f.fromNormalization ⁻¹ᵁ U =
238:      (f ⁻¹ᵁ U).ι ''ᵁ (((f ⁻¹ᵁ U).ι ≫ f.toNormalization) ⁻¹ᵁ f.fromNormalization ⁻¹ᵁ U) := by
240:  convert congr($(Scheme.Hom.congr_app (f.ι_toNormalization U) (f.fromNormalization ⁻¹ᵁ U)) ≫

159	lemma isFinite_iff_locallyOfFiniteType_of_jacobsonSpace
160	    {X Y : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ Y} [Subsingleton X] [IsReduced X] [JacobsonSpace Y] :
161	    IsFinite f ↔ LocallyOfFiniteType f := by

165	    have inst (i) := ((Y.affineCover.pullback₁ f).f i).isOpenEmbedding.injective.subsingleton
166	    have inst (i) := isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion ((Y.affineCover.pullback₁ f).f i)
167	    have inst (i) := JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding (Y.affineCover.f i).isOpenEmbedding
168	    exact forall_congr' fun i ↦ this ⟨_, rfl⟩
169	  obtain ⟨S, rfl⟩ := hY
170	  wlog hX : ∃ R, X = Spec R generalizing X
171	  · rw [← MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := @IsFinite) X.isoSpec.inv,
172	      ← MorphismProperty.cancel_left_of_respectsIso (P := @LocallyOfFiniteType) X.isoSpec.inv]
173	    have inst := X.isoSpec.inv.isOpenEmbedding.injective.subsingleton
174	    refine this ⟨_, rfl⟩
175	  cases isEmpty_or_nonempty X
176	  · exact ⟨inferInstance, inferInstance⟩
177	  have : IrreducibleSpace X := ⟨‹_›⟩
178	  obtain ⟨R, rfl⟩ := hX
179	  have : IsDomain R := (affine_isIntegral_iff R).mp (isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced _)
180	  obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective f
181	  rw [IsFinite.SpecMap_iff, HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFiniteType)]
182	  have := (PrimeSpectrum.t1Space_iff_isField (R := R)).mp (show T1Space (Spec R) by infer_instance)
183	  letI := this.toField
184	  letI := φ.hom.toAlgebra
185	  have := PrimeSpectrum.isJacobsonRing_iff_jacobsonSpace.mpr ‹_›
186	  change Module.Finite _ _ ↔ Algebra.FiniteType _ _
187	  exact ⟨fun _ ↦ inferInstance, fun _ ↦ finite_of_finite_type_of_isJacobsonRing _ _⟩

22	/-- A ring hom `R →+* S` is quasi-finite if `S` is a quasi-finite `R`-algebra. -/
23	@[algebraize RingHom.QuasiFinite.toAlgebra]
24	def QuasiFinite {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S) : Prop :=
25	  @Algebra.QuasiFinite R S _ _ f.toAlgebra
26	
27	/-- Helper lemma for the `algebraize` tactic -/
28	lemma QuasiFinite.toAlgebra {f : R →+* S} (hf : QuasiFinite f) :
29	    @Algebra.QuasiFinite R S _ _ f.toAlgebra := hf
30	
31	variable {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
32	
33	lemma quasiFinite_algebraMap [Algebra R S] :
34	    (algebraMap R S).QuasiFinite ↔ Algebra.QuasiFinite R S := by
35	  rw [RingHom.QuasiFinite, toAlgebra_algebraMap]
36	
37	lemma QuasiFinite.comp {f : S →+* T} {g : R →+* S} (hf : f.QuasiFinite) (hg : g.QuasiFinite) :
38	    (f.comp g).QuasiFinite := by
39	  algebraize [f, g, (f.comp g)]
40	  exact .trans R S T
41	
42	lemma QuasiFinite.of_comp {f : S →+* T} {g : R →+* S} (h : (f.comp g).QuasiFinite) :
43	    f.QuasiFinite := by
44	  algebraize [f, g, (f.comp g)]
45	  exact .of_restrictScalars R S T
46	
47	lemma QuasiFinite.comp_iff {f : S →+* T} {g : R →+* S} (hg : g.QuasiFinite) :
48	    (f.comp g).QuasiFinite ↔ f.QuasiFinite :=
49	  ⟨.of_comp, (.comp · hg)⟩
50	
51	lemma QuasiFinite.of_finite {f : S →+* T} (hf : f.Finite) : f.QuasiFinite := by
52	  algebraize [f]
53	  exact inferInstanceAs (Algebra.QuasiFinite _ _)
54	
55	lemma QuasiFinite.stableUnderComposition : StableUnderComposition QuasiFinite :=
56	  fun _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ hf hg ↦ comp hg hf
57	
58	lemma QuasiFinite.respectsIso : RespectsIso QuasiFinite :=
59	  stableUnderComposition.respectsIso fun e ↦ .of_finite e.finite
60	
61	lemma QuasiFinite.isStableUnderBaseChange : IsStableUnderBaseChange QuasiFinite := by
62	  refine .mk respectsIso ?_
63	  introv H
64	  rw [quasiFinite_algebraMap] at H ⊢
65	  infer_instance
66	
67	lemma QuasiFinite.holdsForLocalizationAway : HoldsForLocalizationAway QuasiFinite := by
68	  introv R _
69	  exact quasiFinite_algebraMap.mpr (.of_isLocalization (.powers r))
70	
71	attribute [local instance high] Algebra.TensorProduct.leftAlgebra Algebra.toModule
72	    IsScalarTower.right DivisionRing.instIsArtinianRing in
73	lemma QuasiFinite.ofLocalizationSpanTarget : OfLocalizationSpanTarget QuasiFinite := by
74	  rw [RingHom.ofLocalizationSpanTarget_iff_finite]
75	  introv R hs H
76	  algebraize [f]
77	  refine ⟨fun P _ ↦ ?_⟩
78	  have (r : s) : Module.Finite P.ResidueField (P.Fiber (Localization.Away r.1)) := by
79	    have : Algebra.QuasiFinite R (Localization.Away r.1) := quasiFinite_algebraMap.mp (H r)
80	    infer_instance
81	  let φ (r : s) : P.Fiber S →ₐ[P.ResidueField] P.Fiber (Localization.Away r.1) :=
82	    Algebra.TensorProduct.map (.id _ _) (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom _ _ _)
83	  let f : P.Fiber S →ₐ[P.ResidueField] Π r : s, (P.Fiber (Localization.Away r.1)) :=
84	    Pi.algHom _ _ φ
85	  have : IsNoetherian P.ResidueField (Π r : s, (P.Fiber (Localization.Away r.1))) :=
86	    isNoetherian_of_isNoetherianRing_of_finite ..
87	  suffices Function.Injective f from .of_injective f.toLinearMap this
88	  rw [injective_iff_map_eq_zero]
89	  intro a ha
90	  apply eq_zero_of_localization _ fun J hJ ↦ ?_
91	  let I := (PrimeSpectrum.primesOverOrderIsoFiber R S P).symm ⟨J, inferInstance⟩
92	  have : ¬ (s : Set S) ⊆ I.1 := fun h ↦
93	    Ideal.IsPrime.ne_top' (top_le_iff.mp (hs.symm.trans_le (Ideal.span_le.mpr h)))
94	  obtain ⟨r, hrs, hrI⟩ := Set.not_subset.mp this
95	  let ψ : P.Fiber (Localization.Away r) →ₐ[P.ResidueField] Localization.AtPrime J :=
96	    Algebra.TensorProduct.lift (Algebra.ofId _ _) ⟨IsLocalization.map (M := .powers r)
97	      (T := J.primeCompl) _ Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight.toRingHom (by
98	      simpa [Submonoid.powers_le] using hrI), by
99	      simp [IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R S (Localization.Away r),
100	        -Algebra.TensorProduct.algebraMap_apply,
101	        ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply R _ (Localization.AtPrime J)]⟩ (fun _ _ ↦ .all _ _)
102	  have hψ : ψ.comp (φ ⟨r, hrs⟩) = IsScalarTower.toAlgHom _ _ _ := by ext; simp [φ, ψ]
103	  refine congr($hψ a).symm.trans
104	    (show ψ (f a ⟨r, hrs⟩) = 0 by simp only [ha, Pi.zero_apply, map_zero])

106	lemma QuasiFinite.propertyIsLocal : PropertyIsLocal QuasiFinite where
107	  localizationAwayPreserves := isStableUnderBaseChange.localizationPreserves.away
108	  ofLocalizationSpanTarget := ofLocalizationSpanTarget
109	  ofLocalizationSpan := ofLocalizationSpanTarget.ofLocalizationSpan
110	    (stableUnderComposition.stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway
111	      holdsForLocalizationAway).left
112	  StableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAwayTarget :=
113	    (stableUnderComposition.stableUnderCompositionWithLocalizationAway
114	      holdsForLocalizationAway).right

117	/-- If `T` is both a finite type `R`-algebra, and the localization of an integral `R`-algebra,
118	then `T` is quasi-finite over `R` -/
119	lemma QuasiFinite.of_isIntegral_of_finiteType
120	    {R S T : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [CommRing T] {f : R →+* S} (hf : f.IsIntegral)
121	    {g : S →+* T} (hg : g.IsStandardOpenImmersion) (hg : (g.comp f).FiniteType) :
122	    (g.comp f).QuasiFinite := by
123	  algebraize [f, g, g.comp f]
124	  obtain ⟨s, hs⟩ := Algebra.IsStandardOpenImmersion.exists_away S T
125	  exact Algebra.QuasiFinite.of_isIntegral_of_finiteType s
126	
127	/-- The predicate for a ring hom being quasi-finite at a prime. -/
128	abbrev QuasiFiniteAt {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S) (p : Ideal S)
129	    [p.IsPrime] : Prop := letI := f.toAlgebra; Algebra.QuasiFiniteAt R p

  --     `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom` once `[IsAffineHom φ.left]`
  --     is exhibited; the latter from non-constancy of φ +
  --     `SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1` on both C and C'
  --     (a quasi-finite proper morphism is finite; smooth-dim-1 + non-
  --     constancy ⟹ quasi-finite via fibre-dim argument).
  --
  -- (b) **`[QuasiCompact φ.left]`, `[QuasiSeparated φ.left]`** —
  --     automatic from `[IsProper C.hom]` + `[IsProper C'.hom]`
  --     (proper ⟹ qcqs over a fixed base, but here we need them for
  --     `φ.left` as a morphism of schemes; the chain via
  --     `IsProper.instOfIsFinite`-companion lemmas closes this).
  --
  -- (c) **`Scheme.Hom.normalization φ.left = C'.left`** — under
  --     smoothness of `C'.hom`, `C'.left` is regular hence normal,
  --     hence integrally closed, so the integral closure of
  --     `O_{C'.left}` inside `(φ.left)_* O_{C.left}` is `O_{C'.left}`
  --     itself. Combined with the degree-1 hypothesis of Step 1
  --     (which says `K(C') ≃ K(C)` so the integral closure
  --     extension is trivial), `fromNormalization φ.left` is iso.
  --
  -- (d) **Slice-category lift**: package `C ≅ C'` in `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`
  --     from the underlying `C.left ≅ C'.left`, using `Over.isoMk` plus
  --     the commutation with the structure morphisms `C.hom`, `C'.hom`.
  --     The commutation follows from uniqueness of morphisms to
  --     `Spec (.of kbar)` (since `Spec` is the right adjoint of `Γ`,
  --     and both `C.left` and `C'.left` carry the same `kbar`-algebra
  --     structure on global sections, induced by the iso `_ψ` between
  --     their function fields — restricted via the closed embedding
  --     `Spec.map (algebraMap kbar _)`).
  --
  -- Each of (a)-(d) is ~50-150 LOC of new infrastructure; the budget
  -- per `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` is ~80-150 LOC for the body.
  -- This work is deferred to iter-190+ with a possible
  -- `mathlib-analogist` consult on (a) (the smooth-proper-curve →
  -- IsAffineHom Mathlib gap) and (c) (the
  -- `fromNormalization = id under normal target + degree-1` chain).

-- **iter-192 Lane I incremental progress on sub-step (a): `LocallyOfFiniteType φ.left`**
  -- (the first half of `IsAffineHom φ.left` ⟸ `IsFinite φ.left` ⟸ `IsProper` + `IsAffineHom`).
  -- Available immediately from `IsProper.toLocallyOfFiniteType` (no new infrastructure).
  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType φ.left := IsProper.toLocallyOfFiniteType
  -- **iter-193 Lane RCI Pin 3 Step 2 carving — chain through three named helpers**
  -- (helper budget = 3; HARD BAR axiom-clean closure on helper (c)).
  --
  -- **Step 2(a) — `[LocallyQuasiFinite φ.left]`** (helper (a); typed sorry, fibre-
  -- dimension argument). Combined with `[IsProper φ.left]` (above), this upgrades
  -- to `[IsFinite φ.left]` via `IsFinite.of_isProper_of_locallyQuasiFinite`
  -- (Stacks 02LS).
  haveI : LocallyQuasiFinite φ.left :=
    phi_left_locallyQuasiFinite_of_finrank_one φ _hφ_deg
  haveI : IsFinite φ.left := IsFinite.of_isProper_of_locallyQuasiFinite φ.left
  -- `[IsFinite φ.left]` auto-derives `[IsAffineHom φ.left]` and
  -- `[IsIntegralHom φ.left]` via Mathlib instance chasing
  -- (`IsFinite.iff_isIntegralHom_and_locallyOfFiniteType`).
  -- **Step 2(c) — `[IsIso φ.left.toNormalization]`** (helper (c); axiom clean
  -- via Mathlib's `instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`).
  haveI : IsIso φ.left.toNormalization :=
    phi_left_toNormalization_isIso_of_isIntegralHom φ
  -- **Step 2(d) — `[IsIso φ.left.fromNormalization]`** (helper (d); typed sorry,
  -- smooth ⟹ normal ⟹ trivial integral closure argument).
  haveI : IsIso φ.left.fromNormalization :=
    phi_left_fromNormalization_isIso_of_smoothProper_finrank_one φ _hφ_deg
  -- **Step 2 assembly — `[IsIso φ.left]` from the factorisation
  -- `φ.left = φ.left.toNormalization ≫ φ.left.fromNormalization`.**
  haveI : IsIso φ.left := by
    rw [← Scheme.Hom.toNormalization_fromNormalization φ.left]
    infer_instance
  -- **Step 2 slice lift — `Nonempty (C ≅ C')` via `Over.isoMk`** (axiom clean;
  -- commutation `(asIso φ.left).hom ≫ C'.hom = C.hom` discharged by `cat_disch`
  -- using `φ.w`).
  exact ⟨CategoryTheory.Over.isoMk (asIso φ.left)⟩

647	curve equivalence of categories) specialised to a non-constant
648	degree-`1` morphism: such a morphism induces an isomorphism on function
649	fields, and lifts to an isomorphism of schemes by the equivalence.

654	`[Algebra C'.functionField C.functionField]` typeclass binder (the

667	/-- **Degree-`1` non-constant morphism between smooth proper curves is an
668	isomorphism** (Hartshorne I Corollary 6.12 specialised to degree `1`,
669	or equivalently Stacks tag `0AVX`).
670	
671	For smooth proper geometrically irreducible curves `C, C'` over an
672	algebraically closed field `k̄` and a non-constant morphism
673	`φ : C ⟶ C'` such that the induced `k̄`-algebra map on function fields
674	`φ^# : K(C') → K(C)` makes `K(C)` a one-dimensional `K(C')`-module
675	(equivalently `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`), the morphism `φ` is an
676	isomorphism of `Over (Spec k̄)`-objects.
677	
678	**Signature refinement (iter-181 Lane I).** The degree-`1` hypothesis
679	is encoded as a typeclass binder
680	`[Algebra C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField]` together with
681	the equation `Module.finrank C'.left.functionField C.left.functionField = 1`.
682	The intended `Algebra` instance at every call site is the canonical
683	function-field map induced by `φ` (the composite of
684	`Scheme.Hom.stalkMap` at the generic point with the `IsFractionRing`
685	extension to the fraction-field; see Mathlib
686	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.functionField`). The previous signature took
687	an abstract existence wrapper
688	`Nonempty (C'.functionField ≃+* C.functionField)` which is strictly
689	weaker than what the iter-182+ body needs: the birational-extension
690	argument requires the iso to be **induced by `φ` itself**, not an
691	arbitrary ring iso. See `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` (Decision 2,
692	DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY) for the analysis prompting this refinement.
693	
694	iter-182+ body: invoke the scheme-theoretic argument of Hartshorne's
695	Corollary I.6.12 (Stacks `0AVX`) — equivalently
696	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom`
697	(see `analogies/ratcurveiso-pin3.md` Steps 1–4):
698	
699	1. Reduce to `φ` finite (proper + quasi-finite ⟹ finite via
700	   `IsFinite.iff_isProper_and_isAffineHom`; quasi-finiteness follows
701	   from the degree-`1` hypothesis collapsing each fibre to a single
702	   point at the generic point and then extending by closedness).
703	2. Apply `Scheme.Hom.instIsIsoToNormalizationOfIsIntegralHom` to get
704	   `Scheme.Hom.toNormalization φ : C ⟶ normalization φ` is an iso.
705	3. Identify `normalization φ = C'` via smoothness of `C'` (smooth
706	   proper curve over `k̄` ⟹ regular ⟹ normal). The integral
707	   closure `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is then an equality under the
708	   `finrank = 1` hypothesis (rank-`1` torsion-free coherent inclusion
709	   on a Dedekind base is an iso).
710	4. Compose to extract `C ≅ C'` in the slice category.
711	
712	Alternative route (cleaner conceptually but more sheaf API): the
713	pushforward `φ_* O_C` is a coherent `O_{C'}`-module of generic
714	rank `[K(C) : K(C')] = 1`; the inclusion `O_{C'} ↪ φ_* O_C` is an
715	iso of coherent rank-`1` sheaves (torsion-free coherent of generic
716	rank `0` is zero on a Dedekind base), so `φ_* O_C = O_{C'}` and `φ`
717	is the structure morphism of an iso.
718
